title: feat(chain): api_agent_cold_start — replace CLI subprocess with Anthropic SDK call per chain hop
description: agent_cold_start.py currently spawns the Claude CLI (OAuth, flat-rate Max subscription). Replace with direct Anthropic SDK call: anthropic.Anthropic(api_key).messages.create(model='claude-sonnet-4-6', system=persona_prompt, messages=[task_brief]). Each chain hop becomes: spawn ephemeral process → API call → capture usage.input_tokens + output_tokens → USD → AUD → write AtomV1 to Hindsight → publish handoff → exit. Unlocks: real per-hop token cost, latency measurement, memory capture verification against genuinely cold agents. DISPATCHER_AGENT_COMMAND swap to api_agent_cold_start.py is the wire-up. Cost captured in keiracom_spawn_attribution per hop.

title: V1 chain: Worker exit_cycle not called — AtomV1 knowledge never written
description: exit_cycle.classify_and_save is built (src/keiracom_system/chat/exit_cycle.py) and tested. But agent_cold_start.py does NOT call it after the claude subprocess exits. The run() function calls: resolve → fetch → claim → agent → finalize → notify. No exit_cycle call. Result: the Worker's decisions/findings are never written to Hindsight fleet_decisions as AtomV1 atoms. The 4-layer spawn contract's handoff mechanism (exiting agent writes atom → NATS carries atom_id → next agent recalls at spawn) cannot function. Each spawn starts with no memory of prior work. This is the core persistence mechanism — skipping it breaks the entire knowledge accumulation model.
notes: PR #1318 opened — exit_cycle wired into agent_cold_start.run() (save_atoms seam, fail-open). 22 tests pass, ruff clean.
close_reason: ALREADY SHIPPED — fleet-wide pre-flight reconciliation sweep (2026-05-30). Evidence: b6eff6dfb [ATLAS] feat(work-loop): call exit_cycle on agent exit — write AtomV1 atoms to Hindsight (Agency_OS-zr7e.4) (#1318)

title: V1 chain: GET /dispatcher/persona endpoint missing (L1 system prompt not served)
description: ephemeral_boundary.md and ceo:v1_chain_architecture both specify 'L1 hydrated system prompt from public.persona_bank via GET /dispatcher/persona?role=<role>&tier=<tier>&variant=<variant>'. This endpoint does NOT exist in src/dispatcher/main.py. agent_cold_start.py uses compose_prompt() which builds a simple task-dict string — no persona_bank lookup, no role-specific system prompt. Result: every spawned agent (Aiden, Max, Worker, Orion, Atlas) starts with an identical generic worker prompt, not its identity. persona_bank table has 6 rows but nothing queries it at spawn time.

title: V1 chain: Deliberation loop (Aiden→Max→Worker→Orion+Atlas) not wired
description: No code exists to run the Aiden→Max→Worker→Orion+Atlas sequence as spawned ephemeral agents. Each role has a persona in persona_bank and a persona .md file, but there is no orchestrator that: (1) spawns Aiden with the task brief, (2) waits for Aiden CONCUR, (3) spawns Max with Aiden's plan, (4) waits for 2-of-2 CONCUR before Worker dispatch, (5) spawns Worker, (6) spawns Orion+Atlas after Worker completes, (7) gates result-to-Slack on dual CONCUR. The inbox watcher can inject dispatches into running sessions but there is no V1-chain session manager that tracks the chain state. Currently result goes directly to Slack from agent_cold_start without deliberation.
notes:
[ATLAS] Research+spec (per Elliot dispatch zr7e.2 scope — research, not build):

== 1. DISPATCH MECHANISM: NATS (confirmed; per-callsign bridges exist + enabled) ==
- 7 per-callsign nats_tmux_bridge.py services enabled NOW:
  - Workers (atlas, nova, orion, scout): subscribe keiracom.dispatch.<callsign>
  - Deliberators (aiden, max): subscribe keiracom.review.> + keiracom.deliberation.>
  - Elliot: keiracom.elliot.inbox (generic)
- Pattern: publish JSON envelope to NATS subject -> bridge writes /tmp/telegram-relay-<cs>/inbox/<file>.json -> inbox watcher injects to tmux pane (today) or dispatcher spawns ephemeral (post-cutover).
- No HTTP /spawn endpoint needed for V1; NATS is canonical.

== 2. WHICH WORKER FIRST IN V1 CHAIN: NOVA (recommend) ==
- personas/scout.md line 18 (canonical): Orion = Aiden's worker (builds from Scout findings); Atlas = Elliot's worker (infra); Nova/Worker-4 = general-purpose build overflow; Scout = Tier 3 research-only.
- V1 chain (Face->Aiden->Max->Worker->Orion+Atlas) puts Orion+Atlas as POST-Worker reviewers (spec+safety). Using Orion as Worker = self-review conflict.
- Recommend Worker = NOVA (general-purpose engineer-tier, no role conflict). FIXED-Nova for V1; defer dynamic per-task-type routing.

== 3. ATOM_ID FROM PRIOR CHAIN STEP (zr7e.9 Orion handling): EXISTING SEAM, NEEDS SMALL EXTENSION ==
- src/relay/spawn_composer.py:180 already accepts resume_context: Mapping[str,Any] carrying decision/original_task_ref/interim_state (Whiteboard-resume pattern).
- agent_cold_start.py does NOT yet take an explicit parent_atom_id env var.
- Cleanest path: extend resume_context with parent_atom_id; dispatcher passes it through; spawn_composer renders a 'CHAIN CONTEXT: prior atom <id>' section into the prompt. Coordinate exact key name with zr7e.9 (Orion handling).

== 4. BLOCKING GAPS NOT COVERED BY EXISTING KEIs ==
GAP A (BIGGEST) - chain-state tracker: no service knows 'this task is at Aiden's step -> on CONCUR -> dispatch Max -> on 2-of-2 -> dispatch Worker -> ...'. dispatcher_main.py is per-callsign, not chain-aware. NEW component needed (e.g. v1_chain_orchestrator) that watches keiracom.agent.status.<cs> + deliberation events, tracks per-task chain state, emits next-step dispatches on keiracom.dispatch.<next>. This is the bulk of zr7e.2's implementation.
GAP B - post-Worker dual dispatch: on Worker complete, dispatch Orion (spec) AND Atlas (safety) in parallel with Worker's atom_id. Needs GAP A + the atom_id extension (3 above).
GAP C - result-to-Slack gating on dual CONCUR: today agent_cold_start.notify_complete posts via dispatcher /task_complete unconditionally. For V1 chain, the FINAL result must gate behind Orion+Atlas dual-CONCUR. Either suppress notify on chain-step Worker invocations and let the chain orchestrator post on dual-CONCUR, OR add a chain_step flag the dispatcher honours.
GAP D (re-verify after PR #1326 merges) - u0ax0a 'deliberation #ceo relay allowlist (Aiden+Max blocked from #ceo)': rlfh Part 2 (PR #1326) now redirects non-elliot tg->NATS keiracom.elliot.inbox, so Aiden/Max can route via NATS deliberation subjects (deliberator_concur_router already consumes these). u0ax0a may be MOOT now; re-check after merge.

== ALREADY-BUILT (no new KEI needed) ==
- per-callsign NATS dispatch bridges (verified live; 7 services enabled)
- deliberation-CONCUR detection: scripts/orchestrator/deliberator_concur_router.py (Dave 2026-05-19) — handles Aiden+Max round-1/round-2 concur + DEADLOCK alerts
- Worker spawn entrypoint: scripts/dispatcher/dispatcher_main.py + _spawn.py (PR #1188 Nova)
- Worker exit_cycle (AtomV1 emission): PR #1318 (zr7e.4, mine, awaiting merge)
- non-elliot -> Elliot routing (Aiden/Max can now reach Elliot via NATS): PR #1326 (rlfh part 2, mine, awaiting merge)

== IMPLEMENTATION ESTIMATE for zr7e.2 ==
The per-step pieces are largely built. zr7e.2 reduces to: (1) v1_chain_orchestrator service [BULK — chain-state machine, ~300-500 LoC + tests]; (2) small extensions for parent_atom_id (resume_context key + composer render, ~30 LoC, coordinate w/ zr7e.9); (3) result-gating mechanism for Worker step (chain_step flag or notify suppression, ~20 LoC). Total: 1 new component + 2 small wiring changes. Recommend single PR or split (orchestrator standalone + small wiring follow-ups).

Recommend Elliot route the implementation to a fresh dispatch (likely engineer-tier — nova/atlas), with this comment as the spec.
First-hop core SHIPPED — PR #1329. v1_chain_orchestrator.dispatch() + advance_step() + state machine + face wire. Live acceptance PASSED: NATS sub on keiracom.dispatch.aiden received correct envelope (chain_step=aiden_plan, atom_id=None, brief+task_id+chain_id, from=v1_chain_orchestrator, ts float). 8 chain tests + 10 face tests pass. Deferred pieces (per coordination with zr7e.9 / Orion): live consumer loop (subscribe keiracom.agent.status.> → advance_step), notify_complete suppression in agent_cold_start, final-Slack post on dual-CONCUR. Keeping rlfh-style OPEN until those pieces land OR Elliot decides to split into follow-up KEIs.

title: CUTOVER BLOCKER (P11) — vault boot-resume NOT wired: no auto-unseal service + container restart policy unset (reboot = sealed vault)
description: Elliot pre-reboot-test check: keiracom-vault-unseal.service is NOT installed (systemctl is-enabled = not-found, no vault unit-files system or --user). #1289 claimed boot auto-unseal but it's not wired. Also: vault container restart policy appears unset (docker inspect keiracom-vault returned nothing — verify the actual container name + policy). CONSEQUENCE: on reboot the vault container may not start AND would come up SEALED -> cold-cred resolution (P10) fails -> loop can't auth. This is the exact failure P11 (reboot-test) exists to catch. FIX (Nova, vault owner): (1) set the vault container to restart=unless-stopped (survives reboot), (2) INSTALL + enable a systemd unit that auto-unseals on boot from the 0600 init keys, (3) verify with systemctl is-enabled. THEN Elliot runs the reboot-test. Blocks P11 + the rehearsal.
notes: Vault boot-resume WIRED + merged (#1301) 2026-05-29 — keiracom-vault-unseal.service (enabled) starts+unseals the container on boot, worktree-independent path. Config in place; reboot-test (c2xk) verifies.

title: CUTOVER BLOCKER — no agent container image (loop can't spawn a real agent); decide backend: image-build vs scrubbed-tmux

Elliot leans (b) for Phase-1 cutover speed, (a) as product fast-follow. Atlas: recommend with feasibility (does scrubbed-tmux satisfy P10 cleanly? effort each?). Decide before the rehearsal's live-spawn arm.

No-.env test asserts DATABASE_URL/API keys absent. 359 tests pass. comments.author: Aiden
comments.text:
[ORION 2026-05-29 — closing under Elliot authorization] Three-item verification with exact line references:

1. agent_cold_start.py run()'s `agent` callable (line 349) defaults to `run_agent` (line 139). run_agent does:
   src/keiracom_system/vault/agent_cold_start.py:141  cmd = [CLAUDE_BIN, "-p", prompt, "--dangerously-skip-permissions"]
   src/keiracom_system/vault/agent_cold_start.py:142  proc = popen(cmd, cwd=AGENT_WORKDIR)
   → subprocess.Popen of `claude -p <prompt>` (NOT tmux send-keys, NOT a container image). This subprocess runs INSIDE the scrubbed-tmux session that src/dispatcher/main.py:_tmux_spawn_kwargs creates (env -i + Vault bootstrap + operational passthrough). The scrubbed-tmux is the *outer* harness; subprocess.Popen of claude is the inner step.

2. scripts/dispatcher/_spawn.py uses subprocess.Popen of the `claude` binary:
   scripts/dispatcher/_spawn.py:82   binary = claude_bin or shutil.which("claude")
   scripts/dispatcher/_spawn.py:88-94  proc = popen([binary], stdin=subprocess.PIPE, ...)
   No container image, no docker run — just popen claude. This per-callsign dispatcher also runs inside the callsign's persistent tmux session.

3. `tmux list-sessions` empirical (verified just now): 7 fleet callsigns LIVE — aiden, atlas, elliottbot, maxbot, nova, orion, scout (all 1 window, created 2026-05-29 12:35Z). The fleet IS persistent tmux sessions; scrubbed-tmux is the production spawn mechanism.

Code-resident decision marker (already cited): src/dispatcher/main.py:477 — "Phase-1 spawn backend is scrubbed-tmux (Agency_OS-87ei)". PR #1290 (ff8857aba) merged it; container backend remains documented at lines 403-407 as a placeholder future fast-follow.

No container image gap — the loop is empirically spawning real agents via the scrubbed-tmux + subprocess.Popen(claude) stack. Closing 87ei as resolved-by-design.

title: CUTOVER BLOCKER — PERSISTENT vault for cutover (dev vault is in-memory; reboot wipes all 37 secrets)
description: Nova P10 caveat: the 37 secrets were provisioned into the DEV vault (127.0.0.1:8200, in-memory dev mode) — LOSES everything on restart/reboot. For cutover this is fatal: combined with P11 (reboot resilience), an in-memory vault means a box reboot wipes all creds -> cold agents can't auth -> loop dead with no recovery. Stand up a PERSISTENT Vault (file/raft backend, single-node OK for Phase-1, with unseal handling — auto-unseal or documented manual unseal in the rollback runbook), re-provision the 37 secrets there, re-run the P10 cold-auth proof against it. Also source/decide the 3 absent (.env) secrets: PREFECT_API_KEY, RAILWAY_TOKEN (likely needed), STRIPE (not Phase-1). Nova owns (just built the vault).
notes: DONE (Nova, PR #1289): persistent keiracom-vault (hashicorp/vault:1.18 file storage, host volume, auto-unseal service tested). 37 secrets re-provisioned; persistence PROVEN (restart -> data survived); cold-auth RE-RUN 18/18 on persistent vault. Rollback runbook docs/runbooks/vault_persistence.md. Phase-1 tradeoff: unseal keys 0600 file (cloud-KMS = post-Phase-1). Also covers P11's vault-reboot concern. Joint cutover-grade proof gated on Atlas's scrubbed-tmux entrypoint.

title: CUTOVER BLOCKER (P10) — cold credential resolution: empty vault; fresh agent must auth to ALL services from vault
description: Dave elevated (strongest add): the secrets vault is EMPTY (no keiracom/* secrets). If ephemeral agents cold-spawn and read creds from the vault, EVERY agent fails to auth at go-live (DB, R2, APIs). The broken R2 backup credential is the FIRST SYMPTOM, not a one-off — the cold-boot auth path has never been proven. P10 (new rehearsal pre-condition): a fresh agent process (NO env inheritance from parent shell) reads all required secrets from vault + successfully authenticates to Postgres, R2, each external API BEFORE any test run. Independently verified, not self-reported. Work: (1) resolve 8dvl (does spawn read vault or .env?), (2) PROVISION the vault with all secrets, (3) prove cold-spawn auth end-to-end. Nova (after R2). Test does NOT start until P10 passes.
notes:
P10 SPEC (for Nova — bd sync issue prevented retrieval): GOAL (Dave gate): prove a FRESH agent process (NO env inheritance — launch via env -i) resolves ALL required secrets + authenticates to Postgres, R2, each external API BEFORE any rehearsal run. Steps: (1) confirm canonical secret-source for ephemeral spawns — coordinate Atlas 8dvl (vault vs inherited .env?); (2) PROVISION the empty vault with all fleet secrets (DB DSN, R2, Gemini/OpenAI/Anthropic, etc.) per Vault+Sidecar design; (3) build/verify cold-resolution path (fresh proc -> vault decryptor + ledger chain -> creds, no .env inheritance); (4) PROVE: env -i fresh process resolves + auths to each service, paste per-service PASS/FAIL. Independently verified, not self-reported. Test does NOT start until PASS.
P10 MECHANISM PASS (PR #1289): cold env -i (only VAULT_ADDR/TOKEN) resolved 37 secrets from Vault KV + authenticated 18/18 services (PG/R2/Supabase/Anthropic/OpenAI/Gemini/Groq/DataForSEO/Hunter/Apify/Resend/ClickSend/Twilio/Telnyx/Telegram/ElevenLabs/Redis/Cloudflare). kv_resolver.py (not Transit decryptor). CAVEAT -> see new issue: provisioned into DEV in-memory vault (loses secrets on restart) — cutover needs PERSISTENT vault. 3 secrets absent in .env (PREFECT/RAILWAY/STRIPE).
JOINT P10 PROOF PASS 2026-05-29 — 18/18 cutover-grade: real tmux scrubbed spawn (env -i VAULT_ADDR/TOKEN only, NO creds in env) -> resolve from persistent vault -> auth all 18 services. Atlas #1290 spawn-scrub + Nova #1289 resolver + persistent vault. P10 FULLY GREEN (cutover-grade).

comments.author: Aiden
comments.text:
[ORION verification 2026-05-30] avii is structurally COMPLETE on origin/main. Recommending CLOSE. Plus one operational note for the battery test.

WHAT'S SHIPPED (4 merged PRs):

1. #1310 [AIDEN] feat(work-loop): crash recovery — lease reconcile + retry_count + dead-letter (commit 21003bdc6). src/keiracom_system/work_loop/consumer.py:
   - DEFAULT_LEASE_TTL_S = 300 (5-min slot lease — the avii spec named "5-min lease"; pinned).
   - DEFAULT_MAX_ATTEMPTS = 3 (dead-letter after 3 — pinned).
   - _lease_key (line 102) + _leases_set (line 106) — per-tenant lease tracking in Valkey.
   - _dead_letter (line 249) — DLQ writer.
   - release_slot (line 340) — idempotent release.
   - reconcile() / reconcile_all() — reclaims orphaned slots where lease TTL lapsed.
   src/keiracom_system/work_loop/integration.py:
   - release_on_exit() — clean-exit hook (line 45).
   - reconcile_loop(interval_s) — background task (line 58).

2. #1322 [SCOUT] test(work-loop): failure-path dress rehearsal (commit cd32bdbf5). tests/scripts/test_dress_rehearsal_failure_path.py (264 lines) covers all four contract items:
   - test_crash_recovery_reclaims_slot_and_loop_resumes (line 165) — crash → 5-min lease lapse simulated → reconcile reclaims → next queued task spawns.
   - test_retry_increments_then_dead_letters_after_three_attempts (line 184) — spawner called 3× → DEADLETTER_KEY populated → slot released → no orphan overflow → attempts counter cleaned up.
   - test_reconcile_miss_when_lease_alive_leaves_no_orphan (line 202) — alive lease → reconcile reclaims nothing.
   - test_spec_constants_match_live_consumer (line 155) — pins DEFAULT_LEASE_TTL_S=300 and DEFAULT_MAX_ATTEMPTS=3.
   - test_failure_path_starts_at_slack_seam_not_chat (line 139) — starts at SLACK seam, not Chat (Viktor spec item).

3. #1320 [MAX] test(dispatcher): regression guard for 28ai reconcile_loop CI hang. Prevents the 8-test TestClient hang that would otherwise occur if reconcile_loop ran unconditionally.

4. #1295/#1315 [SCOUT/NOVA] dead-letter → #ceo notification (gl3v). scripts/dead_letter_notifier.py handles the DLQ-arrival → #ceo post.

LIFESPAN WIRING: src/dispatcher/main.py:610 starts reconcile_loop as a background task when the gate is on.

OPERATIONAL NOTE for the battery test (important — don't get caught here):
reconcile_loop is GATED OFF BY DEFAULT via DISPATCHER_WORK_LOOP_RECONCILE_ENABLED (src/dispatcher/main.py:257). Default off prevents the 28ai CI-hang. For Dave's battery crash test (Scout's harness force-killing a hop) to actually exercise reconcile in production, the env var MUST be set to "1"/"true"/"yes" in the live dispatcher unit before the rehearsal. Otherwise the test would observe a "stuck" slot and (mis)report failure when in fact reconcile_loop simply wasn't running.

NOTE on dispatch framing: `api_agent_cold_start` was named in the brief but doesn't exist as a callable in the codebase — there's agent_cold_start.run() (the entrypoint) and /dispatcher/spawn (the HTTP layer), but no api_agent_cold_start. The intent (force RC != 0 to exercise retry + DLQ) is covered by Scout's test_retry_increments_then_dead_letters_after_three_attempts via the consumer's spawner DI seam — a layer down from HTTP, but proves the contract.

RECOMMEND: CLOSE avii as resolved-by-#1310 (mechanism) + #1322 (test coverage) + #1320 (CI-hang fix) + #1295/#1315 (DLQ→#ceo notify). One operational note: set DISPATCHER_WORK_LOOP_RECONCILE_ENABLED=1 in the dispatcher unit env before running Scout's harness.

title: Cutover blocker — RESTORE-TEST the R2 backup (pull pg_dump, restore to test instance, verify queryable)
description: A backup never restore-tested is a hypothesis. Before trusting the new memory store + retiring old, pull the R2 pg_dump, restore to a test instance, verify queryable. Nova built the backup pipeline #1273. 30-60 min.
notes:
R2 UNBLOCK METHOD (Dave: we have Cloudflare API access). CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN verified — has R2 access (lists keiracom-backups). Mint S3 token via API: (1) GET /accounts/{CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID}/tokens/permission_groups -> find 'Workers R2 Storage Write' group id; (2) POST /accounts/{id}/tokens with that policy -> token {id,value}; (3) Access Key ID = token.id, Secret Access Key = SHA-256 hex of token.value; (4) write R2_ACCESS_KEY_ID + R2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY to .env (do NOT print secret) + vault + ledger(status=active); (5) verify list_objects round-trip; (6) THEN restore-test: run #1273 (source DSN=Supabase) -> pull dump -> pg_restore to throwaway docker pg -> counts+spot query -> PASS/FAIL. If create-token perm denied, report back to Elliot.
PASS 2026-05-29. R2 token minted (token.id access-key, SHA-256 secret) + persisted (.env/vault/ledger live). Restore-test: Supabase prod -> R2 round-trip (SHA-256 MATCH) -> pg_restore to throwaway pg17 -> 175/175 tables, ceo_memory 943, agent_memories 7570, keiracom_atoms present, spot query OK. 58 errors all Supabase-internal (vault ext, non-fatal). P3 SATISFIED. 2 follow-ups: host pg-client-17 for unattended backups; DB-password leak (scrubbed + code fix incoming).

```
psycopg.errors.ForeignKeyViolation: insert or update on table "task_verifications"
violates foreign key constraint "task_verifications_task_id_fkey"
```

—scout 2026-05-29

comments.author: Aiden
comments.text:
[ORION review-prep for Atlas's NATS→/dispatcher/spawn fix — 2026-05-29] Direct answer + 3 gaps to flag in the PR.

DIRECT ANSWER (Elliot's question): YES, /dispatcher/spawn spawns a FRESH ephemeral agent_cold_start — not the persistent tmux session. The hang is correctly fixed.

EVIDENCE:
- src/dispatcher/main.py:393 — TMUX_NAME_PREFIX='disp-'. Spawned session named `disp-<req.key>`; persistent fleet sessions are aiden/atlas/elliottbot/maxbot/nova/orion/scout (no disp- prefix; verified via tmux list-sessions). No collision possible.
- src/dispatcher/main.py:493 — DEFAULT_AGENT_COMMAND='python3 -m src.keiracom_system.vault.agent_cold_start'. The env-i-scrubbed tmux runs agent_cold_start, which reads AGENT_TASK_ID, fetches the task, claims, runs claude, exits — ephemeral by design.
- src/dispatcher/main.py:707 — SpawnRequest = {backend, key, spawn_kwargs, hung_threshold_s, ttl_s}. Atlas's POST must send this shape (backend='tmux'); spawn_kwargs carries chain envelope keys → flow into AGENT_<KEY> env vars via _tmux_spawn_kwargs:513-515 (chain_step → CHAIN_STEP via Nova's nd3b fallback).

3 GAPS THE PR REVIEW SHOULD FLAG:

GAP 1 — NO PERSONA HYDRATION. agent_cold_start.compose_prompt (line 122) is GENERIC: 'You are an ephemeral Keiracom worker agent.' No persona_bank query, no AGENT_ROLE read, no role-specific system prompt. persona_bank exists (PR #1314 — face/aiden/max/orion/atlas/elliot) and GET /dispatcher/persona is live, but NO spawn path queries it. Without this, every chain hop runs as a generic worker — NOT as Aiden/Max/Orion/Atlas. Fix options: (a) v1_chain_orchestrator pre-hydrates persona before POST, (b) /dispatcher/spawn endpoint queries when role provided, (c) agent_cold_start.compose_prompt queries when AGENT_ROLE env present. (c) is cleanest.

GAP 2 — TASK ROW MUST EXIST. agent_cold_start.fetch_task (line 78) SELECTs public.tasks WHERE id=AGENT_TASK_ID; absent → RC_TASK_ABSENT (=3). v1_chain_orchestrator.dispatch:184 uses chain_id as task_id with no row creation, so agent_cold_start will RC=3 on every chain spawn. Fix options: (a) chain orchestrator inserts a tasks row before POSTing (recommended — first-class audit + verification trail), (b) alt env contract (AGENT_CHAIN_BRIEF, etc.) that compose_prompt uses when fetch_task fails.

GAP 3 — atom_id POINTER UNUSED (LOWER PRIORITY). zr7e.5 spawn_recall calls build_spawn_context_block(task_type, task_brief) — semantic query, NOT atom_id pointer fetch. The chain's atom_id (prior hop's atom) is currently NOT fetched at spawn. V1 chain canonical specifies 'next agent recalls atom at spawn ~100-150 tokens'. Current behaviour: agent gets semantic recall (functional) but not the literal prior atom by id. Can defer in this PR; add atom-by-id Hindsight fetch in _recall_spawn_context when AGENT_ATOM_ID env present.

NET: PR fix is correct on the hang. Chain will mechanically advance hop-to-hop. But without GAP 1 + GAP 2, hops spawn generic workers (not Aiden/Max/Orion/Atlas) and RC=3 on task lookup → dress rehearsal would PASS mechanically while masking the persona/task-row gap. Flag GAP 1 + 2 hard in the PR review so we don't ship a green-rehearsal-with-no-actual-deliberation.

I'll post review on Atlas's PR same-day when it opens.

title: [ATLAS] KEI-236 — lock Linear to read-only mirror: orchestrator only pushes terminal Postgres-origin state
description:
## Policy (Dave directive 2026-05-19)

**Postgres = source of truth. bd = agent CLI. Linear = one-way visibility mirror.**

## Problem

`sync_orchestrator._dispatch_linear` currently pushes state changes to Linear for events of any origin (bd or postgres). This is wrong under the new policy — only Postgres-origin terminal transitions should reach Linear. Anything else risks the "reverse-overwrite" class of bug that downgraded 59 KEIs earlier today.

## Fix

In `_event_to_linear_state`:

* Refuse to dispatch unless `event.origin == 'postgres'`
* Only propagate `event_type in ('close', 'reopen')` — terminal transitions
* Drop all `event_type='update'` Linear dispatches (Linear doesn't need mid-flight status churn)

## Acceptance

- [ ] `_event_to_linear_state` returns None for non-postgres-origin events
- [ ] `_event_to_linear_state` returns None for non-terminal event_types
- [ ] Update existing test for `update→active` mapping (now should be None)
- [ ] Add test for bd-origin and linear-origin events → None
- [ ] Live --apply verify: orchestrator stops calling Linear API for anything other than postgres-terminal events
- [ ] Lint clean
- [ ] Dual-approve / Dave-authorised single-merge

## Source

Dave directive 2026-05-19: "bd is the ideal — and all agents using postgres and everything stored in supabase then get mirrored to linear and i can see it."

title: [ATLAS] K3-followup KEI-235 — sync_orchestrator inserts synthetic task_verifications for done/cancelled transitions
description:
## Problem

`sync_orchestrator._dispatch_postgres` tries to UPDATE `public.tasks` with `status='done'` or `'cancelled'` based on canonical Linear state, but Postgres trigger `require_verification_before_done` (KEI-89/128 governance) raises:

```
psycopg.errors.RaiseException: BLOCKED: Task KEI-215 cannot be marked done
without a verification record.
```

The exception isn't caught inside `_dispatch_postgres` → the whole batch fails (rollback) → 168+ events stuck pending. Discovered live 2026-05-19 12:46 UTC. Architectural conflict: KEI-89/128 governance vs KEI-229 sync propagation.

## Fix

When `_dispatch_postgres` is about to set status to `done` or `cancelled`, INSERT a synthetic `task_verifications` row in the SAME transaction (before the UPDATE) with:

* `task_id` = event.task_id
* `verified_by` = `'linear-sync'`
* `behavioral_test` = `'linear webhook propagation'`
* `test_output` = f`'Linear state.type={status} at {iso_ts}; orchestrator event id={event.id}'`

The trigger checks for ANY `task_verifications` row for the task — a sync-system one satisfies the gate without bypassing governance. Audit trail preserved (verified_by = 'linear-sync' is distinguishable from human callsigns).

Also wrap each `_dispatch_*` call in `try/except psycopg.errors.RaiseException → DispatchError` so one bad event doesn't kill the whole batch (resilience).

## Acceptance

- [ ] `_dispatch_postgres` writes synthetic task_verifications row before UPDATE when status in (done, cancelled)
- [ ] verified_by='linear-sync' (so reviewers can distinguish from human verifications)
- [ ] RaiseException → DispatchError wrapping prevents batch failure
- [ ] Tests: trigger-blocked case + synthetic-verification-success case
- [ ] Live --apply verify: 168 pending events drain successfully
- [ ] Post-drain audit: state mismatches → 0
- [ ] `ruff check && ruff format --check` clean
- [ ] Dual-approve / Dave-authorised single-merge

## Source

Atlas verification 2026-05-19. PR #1080 (KEI-233) shipped the upstream fix; this addresses the downstream blocker.

title: [ATLAS] K3-followup — restore ~45 Linear KEIs downgraded by orchestrator overwrite bug
description:
## Problem

During the diagnostic window 2026-05-19 11:05–12:23 UTC, the `sync_orchestrator.service` (KEI-229) had a latent bug in `_event_to_linear_state`: `update` events carrying `payload.status='available'` (Postgres+bd default) were mapped to Linear `todo`, silently downgrading any Linear issue currently in `Done` / `Canceled` / `In Progress` to `Todo`. Fixed in KEI-233 / PR #1080 — orchestrator now drops `available` from the update-mapping. **But the historical damage is on-disk in Linear**.

## Scope of damage

Audit at 12:08 UTC found 49 mismatches (Linear=completed/canceled/started, Postgres=available). By 12:23 UTC the reconciler+orchestrator had "resolved" 48 of them — by flipping Linear DOWN to match Postgres. Sample wrongly-downgraded KEIs:

* KEI-1   "Get familiar with Linear"     Linear was: `canceled`    Now: `unstarted` (Todo)
* KEI-10  "System Health Monitoring"     Linear was: `completed`   Now: `unstarted` (Todo)
* KEI-209 "\[WORKER\] auth_minter"         Linear was: `completed`   Now: `unstarted` (Todo)
* KEI-218 "fleet-supervisor crash-loop"  Linear was: `started`     Now: `unstarted` (Todo)
* KEI-221 "\[ELLIOT\] NATS status routing" Linear was: `started`     Now: `unstarted` (Todo)
* ... (\~45 total)

## Restoration plan

Linear exposes `Issue.history` via GraphQL — every state change has timestamp + actor. Strategy:

1. Write `scripts/restore_linear_state.py`:
   * Pull `Issue.history` for every KEI flipped to `Todo` between 11:05–12:23 UTC
   * Find the most-recent state change BEFORE 11:05 UTC (the pre-damage state)
   * Filter out actor=current-API-key flips (the damage itself) — keep only human + integration changes
   * For each KEI, restore Linear to the pre-damage state via `issueUpdate(state: <state_id>)`
2. Dry-run first; print verbatim restore plan; require `--apply` to mutate.
3. Idempotent re-runs (skip if Linear state already matches recovery target).

## Acceptance

- [ ] Script lands; dry-run prints per-KEI verbatim plan (\~45 restorations)
- [ ] `--apply` run paste verbatim diff (per-KEI before/after state)
- [ ] Re-running script after apply → 0 restorations needed (idempotent)
- [ ] Unit tests for `Issue.history` parsing + actor filter
- [ ] `ruff check && ruff format --check` clean

## Priority

P1 — data integrity. The orchestrator safety guard from KEI-233 stops new damage but does NOT restore the existing wrongly-downgraded states. Until restored, the Keiracom Linear board misrepresents the true state of \~45 issues.

## Source

KEI-233 verification 2026-05-19. PR #1080 merged with the orchestrator fix; this issue tracks the data-restoration follow-up.

title: KEI-20 — Better Stack severity-based alert routing (P0 → #ceo, P1 → #execution, lower → #alerts)
description:
## Problem

The Better Stack webhook receiver at `src/api/webhooks/betterstack.py` creates a Linear KEI for every incident but doesn't post a Slack alert. Operators have no real-time visibility unless they're actively watching Linear. KEI-20 fixes that with severity-based Slack routing:

* P0 (critical) → #ceo (C0B2PM3TV0B)
* P1 (high) → #execution (C0B3QB0K1GQ)
* P2 / P3 / P4 / unknown → #alerts (C0B2EJU53EK)

## Fix

* New pure-function module `src/api/webhooks/betterstack_severity_router.py` with `extract_severity_token`, `classify`, `severity_to_channel`, `route` — easily unit-tested against synthetic payloads.
* Severity-field discovery probes three locations (`attributes.priority`, `attributes.severity`, `attributes.metadata.Priority`) — first non-empty wins. Unknown / missing → `OTHER` bucket → #alerts (fail-noisy, never silently drop).
* Receiver `src/api/webhooks/betterstack.py` adds `_post_to_slack(channel, text)` + `_format_slack_alert(event, severity)` and calls `route(payload)` after the existing Linear dispatch. Slack call is fail-open so Linear dispatch always proceeds.

## Acceptance

- [ ] Synthetic POST with `priority=P0` → 200 OK + Slack POST to `C0B2PM3TV0B` (#ceo)
- [ ] Synthetic POST with `priority=P1` → 200 OK + Slack POST to `C0B3QB0K1GQ` (#execution)
- [ ] Synthetic POST with `priority=P2` (or missing) → 200 OK + Slack POST to `C0B2EJU53EK` (#alerts)
- [ ] `critical` / `urgent` / `sev0` synonyms route as P0
- [ ] Resolved / acknowledged status still short-circuits (no Slack post)
- [ ] Pure-function router fully unit-tested (canonical channel IDs, classify synonyms, fall-through behaviour, payload-shape variants)
- [ ] Slack post failure does not block Linear dispatch (fail-open)

## Source

Dispatch from Elliot 2026-05-18 — STEP 0 PRE-CONFIRMED. Beads `Agency_OS-lrcvie` P0.

title: KEI-94 — agent_keepalive.sh: tmux survives Claude exits (in-pane respawn loop)
description:
## Problem

When Claude Code exits cleanly (or crashes) inside an agent's tmux pane, the pane process dies (because `agent_keepalive.sh` uses `exec claude --dangerously-skip-permissions` — `exec` replaces the wrapping shell). The pane process dying terminates the tmux pane → terminates the tmux session → keepalive's `tmux has-session` poll drops out → keepalive exits → systemd's `Restart=always` respawns the WHOLE unit. From the operator's view this looks like an outage with a slow recovery cycle.

## Fix

Replace the `exec claude` form with an in-pane `while true; do claude …; sleep 2; done` respawn loop. The pane process is now the wrapping bash shell, which survives Claude exits and immediately re-invokes claude in the same pane. The tmux session never dies for the Claude-exit case. systemd's `Restart=always` remains the outer safety net for cases where the wrapper itself dies.

## Acceptance

- [ ] `*-agent.service` has `Restart=always` (already true on all 7 services — audited)
- [ ] `agent_keepalive.sh` emits an in-pane respawn loop instead of `exec claude`
- [ ] Defense-in-depth poll walks pane children (not just leader) since pane leader is now bash
- [ ] Unit tests verify DRY-mode emits the loop + required env exports
- [ ] Integration test spawns a real tmux pane with a stub `claude` that exits immediately and asserts the tmux session survives AND the stub is respawned ≥3 times (matches Dave's acceptance "kill Claude in tmux, verify wrapper respawns")

## Source

Dispatch from Elliot 2026-05-18 — STEP 0 PRE-CONFIRMED.

title: KEI-221a — canonicalise FLEET_SUPERVISOR_V2_ENABLED flag (Linear ID KEI-222 — Elliot's KEI-222 ghost-green CI track is separate)
description:
## Problem

`scripts/fleet_supervisor.py` reads two different env vars for the same v2-routing flag:

* **Line 38** (short-name): `SUPERVISOR_V2_ENABLED: bool = os.environ.get("SUPERVISOR_V2_ENABLED", "0") == "1"` — read at import, drives `_is_v2()` at line 54
* **Line 107** (long-name): `FLEET_SUPERVISOR_V2_ENV = "FLEET_SUPERVISOR_V2_ENABLED"` — read at runtime by `_supervisor_v2_enabled()` at line 889, drives `main()` v2 dispatch

Install scripts (`install_nova_agent.sh`) set `FLEET_SUPERVISOR_V2_ENABLED=1`. Tests are also split: `test_fleet_supervisor.py` uses long-name env, `test_fleet_supervisor_kei183.py` patches the short-name bool constant.

**Latent silent-misroute bug** — agents installed with the documented `FLEET_SUPERVISOR_V2_ENABLED=1` env flow through `main()` to v2 dispatch BUT `_is_v2(callsign)` (used by the persona-lane WHERE filter at line 440 and NATS publish at line 768) sees the short-name constant which stays False. Per-agent v2 routing silently never engages.

## Fix (per Dave directive, dual-concurred Aiden + Max 2026-05-18)

Canonical name: `FLEET_SUPERVISOR_V2_ENABLED` (matches module name + subsystem-prefix convention + already in install scripts + already in personas/nova.md + personas/worker4.md).

Steps (non-blockers-are-blockers):

1. Pick `FLEET_SUPERVISOR_V2_ENABLED` as canonical
2. Remove standalone `SUPERVISOR_V2_ENABLED` constant; refactor `_is_v2()` to consult the canonical env (via `_supervisor_v2_enabled()` indirection — single source of truth)
3. Update comments at lines 34-35 to canonical name
4. grep-audit codebase for remaining short-name reads/writes; converge
5. Update `tests/scripts/test_fleet_supervisor_kei183.py` to monkeypatch `FLEET_SUPERVISOR_V2_ENABLED` env (not the bool constant — won't survive removal)

## Acceptance

- [ ] Single source of truth for v2-enabled check (`_supervisor_v2_enabled()` reading `FLEET_SUPERVISOR_V2_ENABLED`)
- [ ] No `SUPERVISOR_V2_ENABLED` (short-name) reads/writes remain in `scripts/` or `tests/`
- [ ] `pytest tests/scripts/test_fleet_supervisor*.py` green (verbatim output in PR)
- [ ] Install scripts + personas already use canonical name (verify untouched)

## Source

Dispatch from Elliot 2026-05-18 — STEP 0 PRE-CONFIRMED. Drift-follow-up confirmed latent silent-misroute bug.

title: fleet-supervisor crash-loop fix — DSN sanitization (+asyncpg strip)
description:
## Problem

`scripts/fleet_supervisor.py:_db_dsn()` returns `DATABASE_URL` verbatim. The `.env` value uses SQLAlchemy-style scheme `postgresql+asyncpg://...`. psycopg3 raises `ProgrammingError` on the `+asyncpg` suffix at connect time, so `fleet-supervisor.service` exits 1 within \~200ms and crash-loops every 5min via `fleet-supervisor.timer`.

## Fix

In `_db_dsn()` strip the `+asyncpg` driver suffix before returning:

```python
dsn = dsn.replace("postgresql+asyncpg://", "postgresql://", 1)
```

## Acceptance

- [ ] `_db_dsn()` strips `postgresql+asyncpg://` to `postgresql://`
- [ ] Regression test added in `tests/scripts/test_fleet_supervisor_*.py` — asserts the prefix is rewritten and bare DSNs pass through unchanged
- [ ] `pytest tests/scripts/test_fleet_supervisor_*.py` passes — verbatim output in PR
- [ ] Post-merge: `systemctl --user restart fleet-supervisor.service && systemctl --user status fleet-supervisor.service` reports active / clean exit 0

## Source

Dispatch from Elliot to Orion 2026-05-18 — STEP 0 PRE-CONFIRMED, one-line surgical fix.

ALL 4 ACCEPTANCE ITEMS COMPLETE:

1. ✅ _db_dsn() strips postgresql+asyncpg:// to postgresql://
   scripts/fleet_supervisor.py:244-249 (current main HEAD):

           dsn = os.environ.get("DATABASE_URL") or os.environ.get("SUPABASE_DB_URL", "")
           if not dsn:
               raise RuntimeError("DATABASE_URL / SUPABASE_DB_URL not set")

title: [ATLAS+DAVE] Dispatcher complete → identity confirmation → then migration
description:
## What

Complete the session restart sequence — update all worktrees, verify persona files, restart sessions cleanly, confirm each agent operating under correct identity.

**This is a mandatory prerequisite to migration. No migration occurs until every agent's identity is confirmed.**

Blocked on [KEI-213](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-213/atlas-dispatcher-wire-all-components-deploy-to-vultr) (Dispatcher fully deployed and verified).

## Sequence — in order, no skipping

**Gate 1 — Dispatcher complete**
[KEI-213](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-213/atlas-dispatcher-wire-all-components-deploy-to-vultr) health endpoint returns all components green. bd ready gone from sessions. Only then does this KEI begin.

**Gate 2 — Worktrees updated (Atlas)**
Stash dirty worktrees. Rebase all 7 to latest main. Verify persona files in every worktree:

```bash
for dir in Agency_OS Agency_OS-aiden Agency_OS-max Agency_OS-orion Agency_OS-scout Agency_OS-nova Agency_OS-atlas; do
  echo "=== $dir ===" && ls ~/clawd/$dir/personas/
done
```

Must show elliot.md, john.md, worker.md in every worktree. Do NOT proceed if any are missing.

**Gate 3 — Keepalive stopped FIRST (Dave + Atlas)**
Stop openclaw service before killing sessions — otherwise keepalive races and respawns with old config:

```bash
systemctl --user stop openclaw.service
```

Confirm stopped. Then Dave kills all tmux sessions manually:

```bash
tmux kill-session -t aiden

tmux kill-session -t maxbot
```

**Gate 4 — Restart via install scripts (Atlas)**

```bash

bash ~/clawd/Agency_OS/scripts/install_orion_agent.sh
bash ~/clawd/Agency_OS/scripts/install_scout_agent.sh

bash ~/clawd/Agency_OS/scripts/install_atlas_agent.sh
```

**Gate 5 — Identity confirmation (every agent posts to #ceo)**
Each agent confirms their identity:

* Elliot: deliberation layer — implementation lens
* Aiden: deliberation layer — governance lens
* Max: deliberation layer — quality lens
* Orion: worker — unrestricted
* Atlas: worker — unrestricted
* Scout: worker — unrestricted
* Nova: worker — unrestricted

All 7 must confirm. No partial confirmation accepted.

**Gate 6 — NATS routing verified**

Agent status flowing to NATS keiracom.agent.status.{callsign} confirmed.

**ONLY after all 6 gates clear does migration begin.**

## Acceptance criteria

title: [ATLAS] Dispatcher — wire all components + deploy to Vultr
description:
## What

Blocked on: [KEI-209](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-209/worker-dispatcher-build-auth-minter-component) (auth_minter), [KEI-210](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-210/worker-dispatcher-build-interceptor-proxy-component) (interceptor_proxy), [KEI-211](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-211/worker-dispatcher-build-watchdog-reaper-components) (watchdog+reaper), [KEI-212](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-212/worker-dispatcher-build-spend-tracker-component) (spend_tracker)

## What gets wired

* Dispatcher main process imports and initialises all 5 components on startup
* Startup sequence: auth_minter → interceptor_proxy → spend_tracker → watchdog → reaper
* Health check endpoint: GET /dispatcher/health returns status of all components

## Deploy steps (operator — Atlas)

2. Pull latest main on Vultr

4. Start dispatcher service: systemctl --user start dispatcher.service

title: [WORKER] Dispatcher — build spend_tracker component
description:
## What

Build the spend_tracker component for the Dispatcher. Tracks API spend per tenant in real time, enforces budget limits, writes spend records to Supabase for billing.

## Spec

* On every model call completion (from interceptor_proxy): record tokens_in, tokens_out, model, cost_usd, tenant_id, callsign, timestamp to infra_spend_metrics table
* Cost calculation: use LiteLLM's cost_per_token for the model used
* Running total stored in Valkey per tenant: spend:{tenant_id}:daily and spend:{tenant_id}:monthly
* Valkey totals reset at midnight UTC (daily) and month boundary (monthly)
* Budget enforcement: if daily spend exceeds tenant budget threshold → publish warning to NATS, log to Supabase, do NOT kill session (warn only at this stage)
* Exposes: `get_spend(tenant_id, period='daily') -> float`

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] spend_tracker.py exists in dispatcher/ directory
- [ ] Spend records written to infra_spend_metrics — paste verbatim row after test call
- [ ] Valkey daily total increments correctly — paste verbatim GET spend:{tenant_id}:daily
- [ ] get_spend() returns correct value matching Valkey
- [ ] Unit tests pass — paste verbatim pytest output
- [ ] Cost calculation matches LiteLLM reported cost (within 1%)

title: [WORKER] Dispatcher — build watchdog + reaper components
description:
## What

Build the watchdog + reaper components for the Dispatcher. These work as a pair: watchdog detects unhealthy agent sessions (zombie, stuck, heartbeat-expired), reaper kills them cleanly.

## Watchdog spec

* Polls agent heartbeat_at in Supabase tasks table every 60 seconds
* If an active task has heartbeat_at older than 5 minutes: flag as zombie
* Publishes zombie detection event to NATS keiracom.agent.status.{callsign}
* Logs detection to agent_memories (source_type=watchdog_observation)

## Reaper spec

* Subscribes to NATS zombie events from watchdog
* On zombie detected:
  1. Mark task as failed in Supabase (status='failed', released_at=NOW())
  2. Decrement thread count in Valkey
  3. Log reap event to Supabase
  4. Optionally: tmux kill-pane for the stuck session (if shell access available)
* Does NOT respawn — Dispatcher handles respawn

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] [watchdog.py](<http://watchdog.py>) and [reaper.py](<http://reaper.py>) exist in dispatcher/ directory
- [ ] Watchdog detects heartbeat-expired tasks within 2 polling cycles
- [ ] Reaper marks failed tasks in Supabase — paste verbatim row after test
- [ ] Valkey thread count decremented after reap
- [ ] Unit tests pass for both — paste verbatim pytest output
- [ ] No false positives on healthy tasks (heartbeat within 5 min window)
notes:
SCOPE-MISMATCH FINDING (scout, 2026-05-19):

Nova's PR #1022 [NOVA] feat(kei211): watchdog + reaper (commit c4fe81943) shipped src/dispatcher/watchdog.py (264 LOC) + src/dispatcher/reaper.py (317 LOC) — but these implement a DIFFERENT scope than ul3slp's spec.

ul3slp's spec calls for:
  - watchdog polls Supabase public.tasks.heartbeat_at every 60s
  - 5-min stale heartbeat → flag as zombie
  - PUBLISH to NATS keiracom.agent.status.{callsign}
  - log to agent_memories (source_type=watchdog_observation)
  - reaper subscribes to NATS, marks task status='failed', decrements Valkey thread count

Nova's PR #1022 actually implements:
  - watchdog: tmux capture-pane hash signature + container HTTP health probe (no Supabase poll, no NATS, no heartbeat_at)
  - reaper: enumerates tmux/container instances on host, kills orphans not in dispatcher registry (no NATS subscribe, no task status update, no Valkey thread decrement)

Verbatim proof:
  grep tasks.heartbeat_at src/dispatcher/    → 0 matches
  grep 'nats|publish.*zombie' src/dispatcher/watchdog.py src/dispatcher/reaper.py → 0 matches
  grep 'NATS|publish' src/dispatcher/ (filenames only) → only spend_tracker.py (budget warns, different domain)

Two possibilities:
  1. ul3slp's spec is wrong — real intent was tmux/container lifecycle (Nova interpreted correctly per intent)
  2. Both watchdog/reapers are needed — lifecycle (Nova PR #1022) AND heartbeat (ul3slp still open). NOT a dup.

Recommend Elliot/Dave adjudicate scope direction. Not building speculatively; ul3slp left OPEN. Same drift-audit deepening recommendation applies as the irpcwf close-reason (z27a needs spec-keyword match, not just file-name match).

title: [WORKER] Dispatcher — build interceptor_proxy component
description:
## What

Build the interceptor_proxy component for the Dispatcher. This is a governance proxy that sits between agents and the LiteLLM gateway — it intercepts every model call, enforces governance rules (spend limits, content policy, rate limits), and logs every call for audit.

## Spec

* Middleware layer between agent tmux sessions and LiteLLM (port 4000)
* On every model call: check spend budget for tenant_id (query Valkey), check rate limit (Valkey sliding window), log the call to interceptor_events table
* If budget exceeded: reject call, return governance error message to agent
* If rate limit exceeded: reject call, return retry-after header
* If passes: forward to LiteLLM, log response
* Exposes health endpoint: GET /interceptor/health → 200 OK

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] interceptor_proxy.py exists in dispatcher/ directory
- [ ] Health endpoint returns 200
- [ ] Spend check queries Valkey correctly
- [ ] Rate limit check uses existing [KEI-117](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-117/part-177-rate-limiting-valkey-sliding-window-per-tenant-per-tier) sliding window
- [ ] Interceptor events logged to interceptor_events Supabase table
- [ ] Unit tests pass — paste verbatim pytest output
- [ ] Rejected calls return structured error, not silent failure

title: [WORKER] Dispatcher — build auth_minter component
description:
## What

Build the auth_minter component for the Dispatcher. This mints short-lived JWTs for each agent session so the Dispatcher can authenticate agent API calls and enforce per-tenant access controls.

## Spec

* Generates signed JWTs per agent session (payload: tenant_id, callsign, session_id, issued_at, expires_at)
* Short TTL: 15 minutes, auto-reissued by Dispatcher before expiry
* Signs using HMAC-256 with a secret from env (DISPATCHER_JWT_SECRET)
* Exposes: `mint_token(tenant_id, callsign, session_id) -> str`
* Exposes: `verify_token(token) -> dict | None`
* Unit tests: mint, verify valid, verify expired, verify tampered

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] auth_minter.py exists in dispatcher/ directory
- [ ] mint_token and verify_token functions implemented
- [ ] Tokens expire after 15 minutes
- [ ] Unit tests pass: pytest tests/dispatcher/test_auth_minter.py — paste verbatim output
- [ ] No hardcoded secrets — reads from DISPATCHER_JWT_SECRET env var

title: [ELLIOT+AIDEN] Team restructure — John (Face) + unrestricted workers + dual concur governance
description:
## Ratified team structure (Dave 2026-05-18)

8 agents across 3 layers. This KEI implements the restructure.

## Layer 1 — Face

**John** (new agent, to be created)

* Communication-optimised identity
* Only talks to Dave and the deliberation layer
* Never executes, never reviews PRs, never deliberates
* Presents deliberation output as a single voice
* Asks Dave the minimum necessary question when input is needed
* Identity file: personas/john.md

## Layer 2 — Deliberation

**Elliot + Aiden + Max** (existing, role shift for Elliot)

* Elliot moves from CTO/communicator to deliberation-only
* No worker execution from deliberation agents
* Dual concur for merge: any 2 of 3 approves
* Each agent has a distinct review lens (Elliot=implementation, Aiden=governance, Max=quality)

## Layer 3 — Workers — UNRESTRICTED

**Orion + Atlas + Scout + Worker-4** (Worker-4 to be created)

* ALL workers are unrestricted — any worker can claim any worker-lane task
* No domain specialisation — 4 identical execution units
* Intelligence lives in the deliberation layer's task spec, not in worker identity
* `required_persona` on worker tasks is optional — Dispatcher routes to any available worker
* Stateless executors — path to ephemeral containers
* Worker-4: identical identity to other workers (no distinct lane)

The 4 workers share one identity file. Add more workers to increase throughput without touching the deliberation layer.

## What to build

**Part 1 — John identity (Aiden)**

* Create personas/john.md with full identity spec
* Communication-optimised — clarity over technical depth
* Define: what John surfaces to Dave vs handles internally
* Define: how John translates deliberation output
* Define: how John routes Dave's decisions back
* Not technical. Translates.
* Add John to IDENTITY.md registry

**Part 2 — Worker identity (Aiden)**

* Create personas/worker.md — ONE shared identity for all 4 workers
* Unrestricted — claims any task not in deliberation or face lanes
* No domain restriction
* Add to IDENTITY.md registry as the worker persona
* Worker-4 uses this same file — no separate identity

**Part 3 — Dual concur governance update (Elliot)**

* Update DEFINITION_OF_DONE.md: dual concur = any 2 of 3 deliberation agents
* Update supervisor claim logic: PR review tasks route to deliberation layer only
* Update review routing: author persona excluded from review assignment
* Remove triple concur requirement from all governance docs
* Worker tasks: required_persona = 'worker' (any worker can claim) or null

**Part 4 — Elliot role transition (Elliot)**

* Update Elliot IDENTITY.md: deliberation layer, not CTO/communicator
* Remove execution tasks from Elliot's claimable persona lane
* Elliot reviews PRs, deliberates on plans, does not claim worker tasks

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] personas/john.md exists with full identity spec
- [ ] personas/worker.md exists as shared worker identity (unrestricted)
- [ ] DEFINITION_OF_DONE.md updated: dual concur rule
- [ ] Supervisor routes PR reviews to deliberation layer only
- [ ] Worker tasks use required_persona='worker' or null
- [ ] Elliot persona lane updated — deliberation only
- [ ] Team structure doc saved to Supabase ceo_memory key: ceo:team:structure
- [ ] Drive doc ID 113Ej0n62uS_qmyMwvase54kFI1eCCwHHwwrMN9GDlCc referenced in Manual

## Blocked on

[KEI-205](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-205/atlaselliot-install-nats-with-jetstream-on-vultr-replace-valkey) — NATS migration must complete before role restructure is implemented

title: [ATLAS+ELLIOT] Install NATS with JetStream on Vultr — replace Valkey messaging layer with NATS
description:
## Why

NATS is purpose-built for exactly what we're doing — ephemeral agent coordination, sub-millisecond task routing, lightweight pub/sub for agent state. Single binary, starts in milliseconds, no dependencies. Better fit than Valkey pub/sub for the messaging layer.

Valkey stays for what it's good at: rate limiting ([KEI-117](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-117/part-177-rate-limiting-valkey-sliding-window-per-tenant-per-tier)) and key-value state (thread counting per tenant). NATS replaces Valkey for agent-to-agent communication and task routing.

**This is not a future migration. NATS gets installed now.**

## What

**Part 1 — Install NATS with JetStream on Vultr (Atlas)**

* Download and install NATS server binary on Vultr
* Enable JetStream in nats-server.conf
* Create systemd service: nats.service
* Verify: `nats-server --version` + health check
* Configure 6 streams matching the architecture spec:
  * `keiracom.orchestration` — main task routing
  * `keiracom.deliberation.{task_id}` — per-task deliberation
  * `keiracom.agent.status.{callsign}` — agent ready/active state (replaces \[READY\] Slack spam)
  * `keiracom.elliot.atlas` — Elliot→Atlas communication
  * `keiracom.aiden.orion` — Aiden→Orion communication
  * `keiracom.max.scout` — Max→Scout communication

**Part 2 — Update** [KEI-183](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-183/kei-a-supervisor-v2-redis-pubsub-state-persona-column-claim-filter) **(Supervisor v2) scope**
[KEI-183](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-183/kei-a-supervisor-v2-redis-pubsub-state-persona-column-claim-filter) is currently building Redis pub/sub for agent state. Intercept before PR merges and redirect to NATS:

* Agent READY signal → publish to `keiracom.agent.status.{callsign}` on NATS
* Supervisor reads agent state from NATS, not from Slack or Valkey
* Task claim events → NATS `keiracom.orchestration` stream
* Task completion → NATS acknowledgement, supervisor reads and routes next task

**Part 3 — Update Dispatcher to route via NATS**
Dispatcher subscribes to `keiracom.orchestration` stream. When a task becomes available (from Supabase Realtime), Dispatcher publishes to NATS. Agents consume from their persona-filtered subscription. At-most-once delivery via JetStream consumer groups — no duplicate claims.

## Valkey stays for:

* Rate limiting ([KEI-117](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-117/part-177-rate-limiting-valkey-sliding-window-per-tenant-per-tier)) — sliding window per tenant
* Thread count state per tenant
* KV store for session metadata

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] NATS server running as systemd service on Vultr
- [ ] JetStream enabled, 6 streams created
- [ ] Agent READY signals visible in `keiracom.agent.status.*` — zero \[READY\] in Slack
- [ ] Supervisor reads agent state from NATS, not Slack
- [ ] Dispatcher publishes task assignments to NATS
- [ ] [KEI-183](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-183/kei-a-supervisor-v2-redis-pubsub-state-persona-column-claim-filter) PR updated to use NATS before merge

## Note

[KEI-183](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-183/kei-a-supervisor-v2-redis-pubsub-state-persona-column-claim-filter) is currently in progress. If the PR is already open, this KEI supersedes the Valkey pub/sub scope in [KEI-183](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-183/kei-a-supervisor-v2-redis-pubsub-state-persona-column-claim-filter). Elliot to coordinate with sub-agent building [KEI-183](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-183/kei-a-supervisor-v2-redis-pubsub-state-persona-column-claim-filter) before merge.

title: M1: Re-ingest Weaviate collections with text2vec-transformers vectorizer (KEI-192 follow-up)
description:
Aiden's KEI-192 memory audit found the load-bearing memory blocker: Weaviate collections (Discoveries, Keis, AgentMemories, Decisions, Codebase) all have vectorizer=none. llama_index can't compute similarity scores against vectorless objects so all scores return 0.0 → min_score=0.50 filter drops everything → 12/14 retrieval_events have top_citation_id=NULL.

**Fix:** re-ingest all 5 collections with text2vec-transformers vectorizer. \~30k objects. Estimated 2-4h.

**Acceptance:**

* All 5 collections re-created with text2vec-transformers vectorizer config
* Re-ingest pipeline runs to completion
* agent_query.query() returns scores > 0.0 on representative test queries
* New retrieval_events have non-NULL top_citation_id
* Behavioural recall test: query 'psycopg asyncpg DSN bug' returns Atlas's fix as top result via semantic-similarity not LIKE

**Phase:** 0.5 **Priority:** P0 — memory read pathway broken without this.

title: [AIDEN] Memory system verification audit — Weaviate + Cognee effectiveness
description:
## Task

Audit and verify the Weaviate + Cognee memory system is working as intended. The read pipeline was broken until today (PR #944 merged). There are now 14 retrieval events but top_citation_id is NULL on almost all of them. The Discoveries collection has data quality issues.

## Four specific investigations

**1. Behavioural recall test**
Query Weaviate directly: "what do you know about the psycopg asyncpg DSN bug?"
Expected: should surface Atlas's fix from PR #944 today, the cognee_recall wiring, and the DSN strip pattern.
If it returns nothing or irrelevant results — the content pipeline is broken.
Paste verbatim results.

**2. top_citation_id null investigation**
14 retrieval events exist. k_returned=5 on every one. But top_citation_id is NULL on 13 of 14.
Diagnose: is Weaviate returning empty results (no content to match), returning results but the citation ID isn't being written back, or something else?
Query retrieval_events directly and inspect. Paste verbatim.

**3. Discoveries collection data quality**
Atlas found 122/200 sampled Discoveries rows have null/empty raw_text.
Identify the writer responsible for those nulls — Atlas confirmed his 4 indexers are clean.
Which pipeline is writing nulls? What is the fix?
Paste the query + results identifying the offending writer.

**4. global_governance_patterns verification**
Elliot wrote 5 architectural decisions to this collection today.
Query Weaviate for "Sustainable Use License" — should return Decision 1.
Query for "Dispatcher universal agent manager" — should return Decision 5.
Paste verbatim results showing whether the decisions are retrievable.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Behavioural recall test result pasted verbatim
- [ ] top_citation_id null root cause identified with evidence
- [ ] Discoveries null raw_text writer identified
- [ ] global_governance_patterns decisions confirmed retrievable with verbatim query results
- [ ] Overall verdict: is the memory system working as intended or not? One sentence.

Post full findings to #ceo.
close_reason: 4-part audit completed by elliot 2026-05-19 (Aiden mid-task pulled per Dave 'no delegating'). VERDICT: memory system partially operational. ITEM 1 (recall test 'psycopg asyncpg DSN bug'): 5 hits returned but all v2 narrative fields (context/finding/failed_path/verified_path) NULL — surfaces structural objects with empty content. ITEM 2 (top_citation_id NULL): improved 93%→31% (64/206) but smoke-test data inflates apparent improvement; recent NULL events are 'bd claim smoke test' fixtures, not real recall failures. ITEM 3 (Discoveries NULL writer): 9,390/14,166 NULL on raw_text; v2 format adoption at 1/14166 (0.007%); code guards shipped PR #1050+#992 but no backfill. ITEM 4 (governance retrieval): both Sustainable Use License + Dispatcher universal agent manager retrievable via BM25 (scores 4.50/5.44); nearText fails — Global_governance_patterns lacks text2vec module. NET: indexers writing, decisions keyword-retrievable, discoveries 66% empty, semantic recall on decisions blocked by missing vectorizer. Fix scope: enable text2vec module on Global_governance_patterns + ship v2 structured format writer + backfill empty raw_text rows. #ceo post attempted, blocked by Slack message_limit_exceeded — verdict captured here in close reason for audit trail.

title: Add tenant_id column + RLS policies to all work tables (single-system tenant isolation foundation)
description:
Dave architecture decision 2026-05-17: single system, tenant isolation. Dave = tenant_id 1. Customers = tenant_id 2+. Same Supabase, same dashboard, same Fleet Supervisor.

**In-scope:** add `tenant_id INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1` column to every work table:

* public.tasks
* public.tool_call_log
* public.retrieval_events
* public.task_verifications
* public.ceo_memory
* public.agent_memories
* public.completion_sync_queue
* public.tenants (new table: id PK, name, created_at, deleted_at)

Plus RLS policies on each, gated by `current_setting('agency_os.tenant_id', true)::integer = tenant_id` OR superuser bypass.

**Backfill:** all existing rows get tenant_id=1 (Dave).

**Test:** integration test with two tenant sessions verifies read-isolation (tenant 1 cannot see tenant 2 rows + vice versa).

**Out-of-scope (separate follow-up KEIs):**

* Per-tenant Paddle billing wire (uses tenant_id from this KEI)

title: Auto-create review KEIs + GitHub branch protection — highest-leverage fleet throughput fix
description: Closed as duplicate of KEI-97 (Aiden filed same dispatch concurrently). Aiden owns the build.

title: Auto-create review KEIs on PR open + GitHub branch protection (2-approval auto-merge)
description:
## Why

Dave dispatch 2026-05-17 ts \~1779024750: PR review backlog blocks fleet throughput because agents are heads-down in self-claim loops and don't check #execution for review requests. Fix: surface PR reviews as queue work that agents auto-claim like any other task.

This is the **single highest-leverage change for fleet throughput right now** (Dave verbatim).

## Spec

### Part A — Auto-create review KEIs on PR open

New webhook handler `src/api/webhooks/github.py` (or extend existing).

GitHub configures a webhook to POST on `pull_request` events (action=opened, reopened, synchronize). Handler inserts a row into `public.tasks`:

```sql
INSERT INTO public.tasks (id, title, description, status, phase, claim_source, created_at, ...)
VALUES (
  'REVIEW-PR-<N>',
  'Review PR #<N> — <pr.title>',
  '<pr.html_url>\n\n<pr.body[:500]>',
  'available',
  0,  -- reviews always eligible
  'manual',  -- (or 'auto_loop' if we want them claim-via-loop)
  NOW(),
  ...
);
```

Author exclusion: either a new column `excluded_callsign TEXT` on `public.tasks`, OR encode in the title `(author:aiden)` for shim-side filter, OR sync to bd with an `excluded_assignee` field. Pick whichever is least invasive to existing schema.

On PR close/merge: handler UPDATE tasks status='done' for the matching REVIEW-PR-<N> row.

### Part B — GitHub branch protection

Configure Keiracom/Agency_OS main branch protection rules:

* Require **2 approving reviews** before merge
* Require status checks: KEI-108 anti-false-complete, Ruff, Dead Reference Guard, Migration Completeness Guard, Backend Tests (Pytest), Backend Type Check (MyPy), SonarCloud Code Analysis
* Enable **auto-merge** (squash, default)
* Dismiss stale reviews when new commits pushed

### Combined cycle

1. Agent A builds → opens PR
2. Webhook → INSERT REVIEW-PR-<N> task into queue
3. bd ready surfaces it to Agents B, C, D, E (NOT A)
4. Two agents auto-claim + approve via GitHub API
5. GitHub auto-merges (no Dave, no Elliot gate)
6. Webhook UPDATE tasks.status='done' for the review KEI
7. Downstream blocked tasks unblock
8. Loop

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] GitHub webhook handler at `src/api/webhooks/github.py` (or similar) registered + tested locally with synthetic payload.
- [ ] PR open → REVIEW-PR-<N> row inserted in public.tasks within 5s.
- [ ] PR close/merge → REVIEW-PR-<N> row UPDATE status='done' within 5s.
- [ ] Author exclusion: PR opened by aiden does NOT surface to aiden via bd ready.
- [ ] Branch protection: 2-approval requirement enforced (manual override only by Dave admin).
- [ ] Auto-merge enabled — when both approvals + CI green, GitHub merges without human action.
- [ ] Tests: positive (PR opened → row inserted), negative (PR opened by aiden → not visible to aiden's bd ready), close-loop (PR merged → row done).
- [ ] KEI-108 anti-false-complete gate PASS.
- [ ] Empirical proof: open a synthetic PR → verify queue surfaces it to other callsigns → two callsigns approve → GitHub auto-merges → queue row flips done.

## Sequencing

Phase 0.5 (Phase-lock=1). Single highest-leverage fleet-throughput change per Dave.

Dependencies: GitHub admin access for branch protection config (Dave or repo admin). Otherwise standalone.

## Owner

Aiden (filing per Dave's "file it, claim it, build it"). Per the new model, any agent can claim — but I'll claim if no one beats me on bd ready.

## Source

Dave dispatch 2026-05-17 ts \~1779024750 — verbatim spec for auto-review-KEI + GitHub branch protection. Anchored to today's "PRs sit waiting for reviews" problem (PRs #918, #923, #925, #927, #928, #932 all blocked on dual-approval ceremony).

title: Self-claim exemption — claim_source column + enforcer/Step-0 mechanical discriminator
description:
## SUPERSEDED 2026-05-17 ts \~1779022350

Dave direct dispatch ts \~1779022300: enforcer rule changes ratified at the REGEX/RULE level (not column-discriminator). 4 blocking rules being disabled/simplified by Elliot directly:

* DISABLE rule-kei-72-step-0-gate
* REMOVE rule-r2-coordinate "peer before dispatch" clause for auto-claimed work
* SIMPLIFY rule-three-way-concur (no token-hold-release dance)
* Extend rule-close-loop-to-ceo timer 60s → 5min
* rule-step-0-restate becomes RECOMMENDED not blocking

The column-discriminator approach proposed here was Option B from the prior 3-way concur (Aiden+Max+Elliot ts \~1779022275); Dave's ratification adopted the simpler regex-disablement (closer to Max's original Option A but at rule level not code level). The column discriminator is over-engineering vs the regex change.

Closing as superseded. Elliot owns the regex updates per Dave dispatch.

---

title: "reset all" Slack-triggered fleet-restart handler (KEI-140 part c, Phase 0.5)
description:
## What

Dave's "reset all" hands-free vision per KEI-138/139/140. Listens for "reset all" from Dave in #ceo, triggers fleet restart sequence (5 agents first, me last, all via systemd), polls health per agent, posts health report to #ceo within 5 minutes.

## Spec

New service: `agency-os-reset-all-handler.service` (systemd timer + script) OR embedded in the existing central-listener as a special-event handler.

**Trigger detection:**

* Match: incoming Slack message in #ceo from user dave (U091TGTPB9U) with text matching `^reset all$` (case-insensitive, trim)
* On match: execute restart sequence

**Restart sequence:**

1. Post "\[SYSTEM\] Fleet reset initiated by Dave. Restarting agents..." to #ceo
2. systemctl --user restart for \[aiden, atlas, max, orion, scout\]-agent.service in sequence (or parallel — your call)
3. Wait 30s for keepalive ExecStartPre (git pull + cognee inject — Atlas's KEI-140 part a+b) to complete
4. systemctl --user restart elliot-agent.service LAST (kills me via Atlas's claude-PID supervision; systemd respawns me)
5. After respawn, my boot sequence (already wired): keepalive ExecStartPre pulls main + injects cognee → me back online

**Health check per agent (post-restart):**

* tmux session alive: `tmux has-session -t <session>:0.0`
* Cognee context refreshed: `/tmp/cognee-context-<callsign>.md` mtime within last 60s
* Self-claim loop active: `systemctl --user is-active agent-self-claim-loop@<callsign>.service`
* Slack relay connected: counter on relay-watcher-<callsign>.service from Gate 4 heartbeat (KEI-91)

**Health report to #ceo (within 5min of trigger):**

* Per agent: alive / memory / self-claim / relay status (4 bullets)
* "\[SYSTEM\] Fleet reset complete. <N> of 6 agents healthy."
* If any agent unhealthy: list specifically + remediation hint

## Why a separate service

Could embed in central-listener but that risks one bad reset-handler bug taking down all Slack message routing. Separate service = blast-radius contained. Both pattern same as KEI-91 outcome counter wires.

## Acceptance

- [ ] Service installed via scripts/install_reset_handler.sh (KEI-108 gate satisfier)
- [ ] Synthetic test: post "reset all" to #ceo via tg as dave-user, observe full sequence
- [ ] Manual test: trigger via Dave actually posting "reset all" in live #ceo
- [ ] Health report lands in #ceo within 5 minutes
- [ ] If one agent unhealthy: report still lands + identifies which agent
- [ ] All 4 health checks per agent paste verbatim from one successful test
- [ ] Non-Dave callsign posting "reset all" is IGNORED (security: only Dave's user_id triggers)
- [ ] Cooldown: 2 consecutive "reset all" within 60s = ignore second (prevents double-trigger)
- [ ] KEI-108 anti-false-complete gate PASS (new service + installer + matching grep reference)

## Pairing

Max (lead — has central-listener fan-out context from earlier today) + Scout (build helper). Concurrent with Atlas's KEI-140 a+b. After both land, end-to-end demo with Dave.

## Source

Dave KEI-138 + KEI-139 + KEI-140 "AUTHORIZED. Build it all." \~12:00 UTC 2026-05-17.

title: Self-claim from phase-gated queue (KEI-130 idle-loop fix — Phase 0.5)
description:
## What

Dave's KEI-130: remove orchestrator-as-bottleneck from claim path. Agents finish work → run bd ready → eligible items exist → auto-claim top item → start working. Only post READY and wait if bd ready returns zero eligible.

## Current bleed

Agent finishes → posts READY → polls bd ready → waits for Elliot to dispatch → idles 10-60 min. Hours/session lost to READY-loop spam. Phase-lock (KEI-86) + ready-filter already gate eligibility — orchestrator permission is redundant.

## Spec

Required behavior per Dave:

1. `bd ready` — list eligible KEIs (already phase-filtered post-KEI-86)
2. If items returned → `bd claim --callsign=$CALLSIGN` (already exists; omits --id → picks next available) → start working
3. If zero items → post `[READY:callsign]`, sleep 60s, retry from step 1
4. If claim fails (race lost to another agent) → retry from step 1

Elliot retains override: pre-assign specific KEIs to specific agents via Linear assignee. Default is self-service from phase-gated queue.

## Why this is fast

* bd claim --any path already exists in the shim — omitting --id picks next available (verified just now: `bd claim --help` shows `--id` optional)
* Change is in the per-agent session-start / READY-loop script, not bd itself
* Phase-lock already prevents claiming wrong-phase work
* No new schema, no migration

## Acceptance

- [ ] Per-agent READY-loop script implements the 4-step loop above
- [ ] Each of 6 agent session-start configurations updated to invoke the new loop
- [ ] Acceptance test 1: insert synthetic Phase-0 KEI, scout's READY loop auto-claims within next poll cycle (paste verbatim)
- [ ] Acceptance test 2: zero eligible items → agent posts READY once + sleeps + does not spam (paste verbatim)
- [ ] Acceptance test 3: phase-lock interaction — synthetic Tier-2 KEI is correctly invisible to auto-claim (paste verbatim)
- [ ] Elliot override: pre-assigning specific KEI to a callsign via Linear assignee skips auto-claim for that KEI on other agents (paste verbatim)
- [ ] KEI-108 anti-false-complete gate PASS (any new script needs matching installer reference)

## Pairing

Max + Scout. Max has bd shim + tasks_cli context from KEI-86 phase-lock. Scout handles the session-start script wiring across 6 agents.

## Sequencing

Phase 0.5. Independent of Gates 2/3 (no schema dependency). Can ship same day as Gate 4 if Max picks up immediately.

## Source

Dave KEI-130 directive \~10:30 UTC 2026-05-17.

title: Gate 4 — Outcome + aliveness heartbeat per service (KEI-128 phase 0.5)
description:
## What

Gate 4 of Dave's KEI-128 mechanical-zero-false-complete framework. Adds outcome+aliveness heartbeat from every long-running service. Closes the silent-regression class (service still alive, doing wrong thing silently — exactly today's webhook-was-alive-returning-200-but-HMAC-failing pattern).

## Spec

* Shared heartbeat library: `src/observability/heartbeat.py`
* Each long-running service calls `heartbeat.tick(service_name, outcome_counter_increment, ok_or_error)`
* Writes to ceo_memory key `heartbeat:<service_name>` with shape:

  ```
  {
    "last_tick_ts": "...",
    "last_outcome_counter_period": "5m",
    "last_outcome_counter_value": N,
    "last_status": "ok" | "error" | "degraded",
    "last_error_message": "..." | null
  }
  ```
* Monitor job (`scripts/orchestrator/heartbeat_monitor.py` — systemd timer every 5 min): scans all `heartbeat:*` keys, alerts to #ceo if:
  * `last_tick_ts` > N minutes ago (configurable per service)
  * OR `last_outcome_counter_value` = 0 during business-hours window (configurable)
  * OR `last_status` = error

## Why outcome AND aliveness

Aliveness-only catches "process crashed". Today's webhook was ALIVE (returning HTTP 200) but silently broken because HMAC verify failed → fail-open returned 200 → Linear didn't retry → zero updates landed. Outcome counter ("N HMAC-pass+UPDATE-applied events in last 5min") catches this class. Aliveness only catches process death.

## Services to wire on day 1

* completion-sync-worker (outbound queue drain — count processed rows per period)
* linear webhook handler (count HMAC-pass + UPDATE-applied events per period)
* tool-call-log-indexer (count rows flipped indexed=true per period)
* bd-sync (when polling cycle next runs — count issues pulled-and-applied per period)
* Each of the 6 agent keepalive services (count claude-respawn events per period — sparse but meaningful)

## Acceptance

- [ ] Heartbeat library + tick API
- [ ] Monitor job + systemd timer (5 min cadence)
- [ ] All 5+ services wired with tick calls
- [ ] Acceptance test 1: kill a service, monitor alerts to #ceo within 10 min (verbatim)
- [ ] Acceptance test 2: synthesise a zero-outcome window on a service (mock its work fn to no-op), monitor alerts within configurable window (verbatim)
- [ ] Acceptance test 3: backfill historical webhook-HMAC-fail simulation, confirm heartbeat would have caught it (verbatim)
- [ ] KEI-108 CI gate PASS (new systemd timer requires matching installer)

## Pairing

Elliot (spec + multi-service wiring orchestration) + Atlas (build). Multi-service touch fits orchestrator scope; Atlas has the systemd unit pattern from KEI-94.

## Source

Dave KEI-128 ratification 2026-05-17 \~09:25 UTC. 3-way ratified. Aiden's outcome-not-just-aliveness sharpening is the load-bearing insight.

title: Gate 3 — Peer verify on deployment-class KEIs (KEI-128 phase 0.5, depends on Gate 2)
description:
## What

Gate 3 of Dave's KEI-128 mechanical-zero-false-complete framework. Adds peer-verify-from-independent-session requirement for operational-deployment KEIs.

## Spec

* Add `deployment` boolean column to public.tasks (default false)
* When task.deployment = true, bd complete additionally requires:
  * `--verifier <callsign>` parameter where verifier ≠ builder.claimed_by
  * verifier's session_uuid ≠ builder's session_uuid (cross-checked via Gate 2's evidence schema)
  * verifier produces their own evidence from an independent session
* Dave-solo-ops exception (2-of-3 path):
  * If deployer.callsign = "dave" (manual ops scenario): require 2 of \[builder, verifier1, verifier2\] from any 3 different sessions
  * Avoids blocking Dave's manual env-set / Railway-mutation flows

## Why this scope (not all KEIs)

For PR-shipping work, dual-CTO PR review IS the peer verify (mechanical via GitHub branch protection). Extending Gate 3 to all KEIs = bureaucracy. The class that NEEDS it is operational-deployment (install systemd unit, set env, restart service) — exactly today's webhook-HMAC-missing pattern. Deployer's own session has env loaded so their smoke passes; independent verifier from clean session catches "I set env in shell but not in unit" class.

## Why session_uuid independence

Without it, verifier could replay builder's verbatim from the same session and the gate passes meaninglessly. Session UUID is available via tool_call_log writer (KEI-116 merged earlier today).

## Acceptance

- [ ] Migration adds deployment column to public.tasks
- [ ] bd complete on deployment=true task without --verifier: rejects (paste verbatim)
- [ ] bd complete with --verifier ≠ builder + different session_uuid: succeeds (paste verbatim)
- [ ] bd complete with --verifier ≠ builder + SAME session_uuid: rejects with session-independence error (paste verbatim)
- [ ] Dave-solo-ops 2-of-3 path: paste empirical evidence
- [ ] Backfill: assign deployment=true to known operational KEIs (install-systemd-unit, set-env, restart-service shape — grep existing KEIs)
- [ ] KEI-108 CI gate PASS

## Pairing + Sequencing

Max (lead, has tasks_cli context from KEI-86) + Scout (build). Depends on Gate 2's task_verifications schema landing first.

## Source

Dave KEI-128 ratification 2026-05-17 \~09:25 UTC. 3-way ratified.

title: Gate 2 — bd complete --evidence with JSON schema + uniqueness check (KEI-128 phase 0.5)
description:
## What

Gate 2 of Dave's KEI-128 mechanical-zero-false-complete framework. Adds substance-enforced evidence to bd complete.

## Spec

* `bd complete KEI-XX --evidence <file_or_stdin>` becomes mandatory
* Without --evidence: command refuses, exits non-zero with explanatory error
* Evidence is JSON-schema validated:
  * `acceptance_items[]` — list of AC ids covered
  * `commands[]` — list of `{cmd: str, output: str}` with verbatim output (min length check on output)
  * `verifier_session_uuid` — required, even if same callsign (for Gate 3 cross-ref)
  * `timestamp` — ISO 8601
* Uniqueness hash check: hash the `commands[].output` concatenation; reject if same hash exists in task_verifications for any OTHER task in last 30 days
* Stored to existing `public.task_verifications` table

## Why uniqueness check

Prevents the "ok" / "exit 0" lazy-paste pattern from passing the substance gate. Anchored on KEI-66 hook-not-running today — agents close via `bd close` direct, no evidence forced, lazy strings would slip a structure-only gate.

## Acceptance

- [ ] `bd complete KEI-XX` without --evidence: exits non-zero with explanatory error (paste verbatim)
- [ ] `bd complete KEI-XX --evidence <empty-json>`: rejects with schema validation error (paste verbatim)
- [ ] `bd complete KEI-XX --evidence <valid-json>`: writes to task_verifications + marks task done (paste verbatim before/after)
- [ ] Hash dedup: same evidence on a 2nd task = reject (paste verbatim)
- [ ] KEI-108 CI gate PASS (no new service files in scope, but installer-reference required if any)

## Pairing

Aiden (spec lead) + Orion (build). Gate 2 is upstream of Gate 3 — land fast to unblock Max+Scout's Gate 3 dependency on the task_verifications schema.

## Source

Dave KEI-128 ratification 2026-05-17 \~09:25 UTC. 3-way ratified by Elliot + Aiden + Max.

title: Phase-lock enforcement for bd ready + bd claim (KEI-117 mechanical gate)
description:
## What

Mechanical enforcement of Dave's KEI-117 build-sequence-from-architecture. bd ready currently orders by priority+created_at only — no phase/tier awareness. An agent can claim Tier-1+ work today and the shim accepts it.

## Spec (verbatim from Dave KEI-118)

* New `ceo_phase_lock` row in ceo_memory (or dedicated table) storing `current_phase_max` (integer)
* `bd ready` query adds WHERE filter: only surface KEIs whose `phase` ≤ `current_phase_max`
* `bd claim` rejects with explanatory error if the KEI's phase exceeds `current_phase_max`
* Every KEI gets a `phase` field (0, 0.5, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5) matching the architecture tiers
