[KEI-22](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-22/kei-22-automated-linear-beads-bidirectional-sync) (atomic claiming) — bd ready is built on the same Beads layer.

## Owner

Max — after [KEI-22](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-22/kei-22-automated-linear-beads-bidirectional-sync) ships.

title: KEI-50 — Structured discovery log: 4-field format + bd discover command + CLAUDE.md standing practice
description:
## What

Implement the structured discovery log format and update all 6 agents' CLAUDE.md files to make discovery logging a standing practice. This is the one deliberate agent action in the otherwise automatic memory system.

## Why

Git captures what was written. Slack captures what was said. Neither captures what was learned. The discovery log is the agent's extraction of signal from their reasoning — the "I discovered X because Y" that would otherwise disappear when the session ends.

## Discovery log format v2 — final (Dave's KEI-55 amendment 2026-05-14)

`do_not_repeat` is replaced by explicit `failed_path` and `verified_path`. LLMs handle positive instructions better than negative — so the format presents BOTH what failed AND what works. `finding` is the standalone signal extraction. `context_version` is auto-populated for staleness detection (KEI-56). `validation_tier` is auto-classified by the system (KEI-53).

```json
{
  "agent": "callsign",
  "kei": "KEI-XX",
  "context": "one-line description of the problem",
  "finding": "what is true about this system",
  "failed_path": "what approach was tried and why it failed",
  "verified_path": "what approach works and why",
  "tags": ["tag1", "tag2"],
  "context_version": {auto-populated from environment},
  "validation_tier": {1|2|3 — auto-classified by system}
}
```

**This is the final complete format. No further changes without a KEI.** (Dave ratify 2026-05-14)

**Example (Max's interim-ulimit lesson):**

```json
{
  "agent": "max",
  "kei": "KEI-44",
  "context": "Cognee Streams 3+4 resume under memory cap",
  "finding": "ulimit -v limits virtual memory, not RSS. Python+numpy and graph DBs reserve large virtual ranges at startup that aren't backed by real RSS — the limit fires before any work begins. cgroup v2 memory.max measures RSS only.",
  "failed_path": "ulimit -v 3145728 (3GB virtual). Tried at session ts ~1778705680. Result: all chunks failed; Cognee graph DB couldn't reserve its ~4GB virtual mmap; OOM before ingest started.",
  "verified_path": "systemd-run --user --scope -p MemoryMax=3G. Wraps the child process with a cgroup ceiling on RSS only; virtual reservations pass; kernel OOM-kills only if real memory exceeds. Landed as scripts/orchestrator/cognee_capped.sh in KEI-44 / PR #846.",
  "tags": ["cognee", "memory", "cgroup", "ulimit", "oom"],
  "context_version": {"cognee": "1.0.9", "kernel": "6.8.0-111-generic", "systemd": "user-mode"},
  "validation_tier": 2
}
```

Not the reasoning chain. The signal extracted from it.

## Two write moments

### 1\. Mid-task discovery (agent-initiated)

When an agent hits a significant insight, they write immediately. Not at the end of the task — at the moment of discovery, while it's fresh.
Command: `bd discover` — opens a structured prompt with the v2 fields.

### 2\. Task completion (system-prompted)

When `bd complete` fires, the system prompts: "What did you discover? (optional — press Enter to skip)"
If the agent writes something: indexed immediately (via staging → validation tier → permanent per KEI-53). If they skip: no penalty.

## CLAUDE.md update (all 6 agents)

Add standing instruction:

> "When you make a significant discovery — an approach that failed and why, a gotcha, a key design decision, a constraint you found — write a discovery log immediately. Use `bd discover`. Six fields (agent / kei / context / finding / failed_path / verified_path / tags), plus auto-populated context_version and validation_tier. One sentence per field. This is how the team learns from your experience."

## Indexing (with KEI-53 governance)

Every discovery log written → enters `staging` collection → validation tier check (KEI-53):

* **Tier 1 (routine):** auto-promotes to `permanent` collection after 24h.
* **Tier 2 (architecture):** requires peer `[CONCUR:<callsign>]` before promotion.
* **Tier 3 (contradicts ratified decision):** requires Dave approval before promotion.

Once promoted to permanent, available to all agents within 5 seconds via `bd recall` and via auto-injected context on `bd claim` (with 500-token ceiling per KEI-55).

## Acceptance criteria

* `bd discover` command implemented (opens v2 structured prompt — 6 fields + auto-context_version)
* `bd complete` prompts for optional discovery entry
* All 6 agents' CLAUDE.md updated with v2 standing instruction
* Discovery logs enter staging within 5 seconds of writing
* Tier 1 auto-promotion after 24h works
* Tier 2 requires CONCUR before promote
* Tier 3 requires Dave approval before promote
* Test: write a discovery at each tier, verify correct promotion path

## Dependencies

Dave's KEI-46 (Weaviate / Linear KEI-48) + KEI-47 (LlamaIndex / Linear KEI-49) for indexing. KEI-53 (epistemic-poisoning / Linear KEI-55) for validation tiers. KEI-55 (token ceiling / Linear KEI-57) for the format. KEI-56 (staleness / Linear KEI-58) for context_version. CLAUDE.md standing-instruction update can start immediately — Elliot owns and shipped PR #848 (v1 format, will amend to v2).

## Owner

Elliot — CLAUDE.md update (PR #848 in flight, v2 amend pending). `bd discover` and `bd complete` integration after the Weaviate + LlamaIndex foundations ship.

## Authoritative trace

Dave verbatim #ceo 2026-05-14 — Collective Intelligence Architecture + four governance KEIs amendment (KEI-53/54/55/56):

> "Updated discovery log format (all agents use this): { agent, kei, context, finding, failed_path, verified_path, tags, context_version, validation_tier }. This is the final complete format. No further changes without a KEI."

title: KEI-49 — bd claim context injection: auto-retrieve and inject prior knowledge on task start
description:
## What

When any agent runs `bd claim [KEI-XX]`, the system automatically queries Weaviate and injects a context brief into the agent's tmux pane before they begin work. `bd claim` **blocks** — does not return exit code 0 until the brief is fully written to the agent's tmux pane and the write is verified. Sequential, not fire-and-forget.

## Why

Today agents start tasks blank. They have no visibility into prior decisions, related work, or discoveries made by other agents. This KEI ensures every agent starts every task already informed — without having to ask for it.

## Architecture amendments 2026-05-14

### 500-token injection ceiling (Dave's KEI-55, ts \~1778713000)

The injected context brief is **hard-capped at 500 tokens**. Reasoning:

* Unbounded injection inflates every API call across 6 agents × multiple claims per day.
* Larger briefs dilute agent attention; 10-line distilled brief beats 50-line dump.
* LlamaIndex orchestrator re-ranks results to fit the ceiling. Lower-priority items drop, not truncate mid-finding.
* If the brief overflows, the cited-references list is preserved (so the agent can `bd recall <tag>` for full content); summaries are tightened.

This ceiling is enforced at the LlamaIndex retrieval layer, not the relay layer — relay ships whatever brief LlamaIndex produces.

### Blocking semantics (Dave's KEI-49 amendment, ts \~1778715200)

`bd claim` is sequential, not fire-and-forget:

1. Claim row in Supabase (atomic, SKIP LOCKED per KEI-22).
2. Update Linear assignee (same transaction).
3. Query Weaviate across the 5 collections.
4. LlamaIndex generates ≤500-token brief.
5. Inject brief into agent's tmux pane via relay.
6. **Verify write** — read back the last N lines of the pane, confirm the brief is present and complete.
7. Only then return exit code 0.

If any step fails (Weaviate timeout, relay error, write verification fail), `bd claim` returns non-zero with the failing step in stderr. Agent does not proceed on a partial brief.

Fire-and-forget gives zero benefit here: the agent that just ran `bd claim` is the SAME agent that will read the injected brief one beat later. Returning 0 before the brief lands means the agent might start work blind. The whole point is to inform the agent before they begin — that requires the inject to land.

## How it works

```
Agent runs: bd claim KEI-51

bd claim (BLOCKING):
  1. Supabase txn: SELECT FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED on claim row + Linear assignee update (KEI-22)
  2. If rows_affected = 0: return 1 — agent picks next from bd ready
  3. Query Weaviate across 5 collections (discoveries / decisions / sessions / codebase / prior KEIs)
  4. LlamaIndex generates ≤500-token context brief (KEI-55 ceiling)
  5. Inject brief into agent's tmux via existing relay
  6. Verify write: tmux capture-pane → grep for brief sentinel — confirm presence
  7. Return 0
  8. Agent begins work with brief in pane

Brief format:
[CONTEXT BRIEF — KEI-51]
Prior discoveries: [cited, 2-3 most relevant]
Related decisions: [cited, 2-3 most relevant]
Relevant codebase: [file paths + one-line description]
Watch out for: [failed_path from discoveries collection]
Verified approach: [verified_path from discoveries collection]
```

## What the agent sees before they begin

Not a wall of text. A 10-line brief, ≤500 tokens. Enough to be informed. Not enough to overwhelm.

## Acceptance criteria

* bd claim triggers Weaviate query automatically (no agent action)
* bd claim is BLOCKING — does NOT return 0 until brief is written + verified
* Context brief injected into tmux within 5 seconds of claim
* Brief is ≤500 tokens (verifiable: token counter logs each injection)
* Brief contains at minimum: 1 prior discovery, 1 related decision, 2 relevant files
* Empty brief (no results) still injects + verifies — confirms no prior context exists
* Verification step: tmux capture-pane confirms brief sentinel present after inject; bd claim returns non-zero on missing-sentinel
* Verified across all 6 agents and all KEI types
* When the brief would exceed 500 tokens, lower-priority items drop entirely (not truncate mid-finding); cited references preserved so the agent can `bd recall` for full content.

## Dependencies

Dave's KEI-46 (Weaviate / Linear KEI-48) + KEI-47 (LlamaIndex / Linear KEI-49) + KEI-48 (auto-indexing / Linear KEI-50, so there's content to retrieve). Also references KEI-55 (Linear KEI-57) for the token ceiling. Pre-conditions: KEI-57 (secret redaction / Linear KEI-59) before any data enters; KEI-58 (persistent storage / Linear KEI-60) before Weaviate starts; KEI-59 (durable queue / Linear KEI-61) before webhooks connect.

## Owner

Aiden — after Dave's KEI-48 (Linear KEI-50) ships. This is the retrieval payoff of the indexing investment.

## Authoritative trace

Dave verbatim #ceo 2026-05-14 — Collective Intelligence Architecture + four governance KEIs amendment:

> "KEI-55 — Context injection ceiling (500 tokens hard limit on bd claim injection) + discovery log format v2: 'failed_path' and 'verified_path' replace 'do_not_repeat'. Positive and negative paths both explicit. Elliot updates KEI-49 and KEI-50 descriptions immediately."

Dave amendment #ceo 2026-05-14 (ts \~1778715200):

> "KEI-49 (bd claim context injection): bd claim must BLOCK — cannot return exit code 0 until the context brief is fully written to the agent's tmux pane and verified. Sequential: claim → query Weaviate → inject → verify write → return 0 → agent begins. Fire-and-forget gives zero benefit. Update the description now."

title: KEI-48 — Auto-indexing pipeline: git, Slack, Linear, ceo_memory → Weaviate
description:
## What

Four automatic indexing pipelines that capture knowledge from existing infrastructure without any agent action required.

## Why

The current memory system requires agents to deliberately save knowledge. They forget. They crash. Knowledge is lost. This KEI makes the capture infrastructure-driven — knowledge flows into Weaviate automatically as agents do their normal work.

## Four pipelines

### Pipeline 1: Git commits → Weaviate codebase collection

* LlamaIndex post-commit hook on Agency_OS repo
* On every merge to main: parse changed Python/MD/YAML/JSON files
* Chunk with HierarchicalNodeParser (respects code structure)
* Index into `codebase` collection with metadata: file_path, commit_sha, author (agent callsign), timestamp
* Backfill: index entire current codebase on first run

### Pipeline 2: Slack messages → Weaviate sessions collection

* Slack webhook on #ceo and #execution
* New message → LlamaIndex processes → index into `sessions`
* Metadata: channel, agent, timestamp, message_type
* Enables query: "what did we decide about X in #ceo last week?"

### Pipeline 3: Linear KEIs → Weaviate keis collection

* Linear outbound webhook on issue create/update
* KEI description + comments + status → index into `keis`
* Metadata: kei_id, priority, status, owner, tags
* Enables query: "has this problem been worked on before?"

### Pipeline 4: ceo_memory entries → Weaviate decisions collection

* Supabase trigger on INSERT/UPDATE to ceo_memory table
* New entry → LlamaIndex processes → index into `decisions`
* Metadata: key, updated_at, author
* Enables query: "what are all the standing rules established this month?"

## All pipelines run as systemd services — persistent, restart on failure.

## Acceptance criteria

* All 4 pipelines running as systemd services
* Test query per pipeline returns results within 10 seconds of source update
* Backfill complete: full codebase, full KEI history, full Slack history (30 days), all ceo_memory entries indexed

## Dependencies

[KEI-46](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-46/kei-45-layer-3-idle-enforcement-daemon-systemd-polling-service-per) (Weaviate) + [KEI-47](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-47/kei-migration-completeness-guard-regex-false-positive-on-update-from) (LlamaIndex) must ship first.

## Owner

Scout — research and implementation. Runs in parallel with [KEI-49](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-49/kei-47-llamaindex-oss-retrieval-orchestration-layer-over-weaviate)/50/51.

title: KEI-47 — LlamaIndex OSS: retrieval orchestration layer over Weaviate
description:
## What

Install LlamaIndex OSS as the retrieval orchestration layer sitting between agents and Weaviate. This is the intelligence layer that handles chunking, indexing, hierarchical retrieval, and context injection.

## Why

Weaviate stores and searches. LlamaIndex decides what to search for, how to chunk documents, how to combine results, and how to inject context into agent prompts. Without LlamaIndex, agents talk directly to Weaviate with raw queries. With LlamaIndex, retrieval is orchestrated and context-aware.

## Spec

### Installation

```bash
pip install llama-index llama-index-llms-anthropic llama-index-vector-stores-weaviate
```

### Key components to configure

* `WeaviateVectorStore` — connects LlamaIndex to Weaviate collections
* `VectorStoreIndex` — indexes documents into Weaviate via LlamaIndex
* `HierarchicalNodeParser` — structure-aware chunking for code and markdown
* `CitationQueryEngine` — every retrieval result includes source attribution
* `FunctionAgent` with Claude Opus 4.7 — agent query interface

### Cognee relationship

LlamaIndex becomes the primary retrieval layer. Cognee is retained as a graph reasoning sidecar for multi-hop queries only. Both can be queried by agents — LlamaIndex for precision retrieval, Cognee for relationship traversal.

### Query interface for agents

```python
# Agent queries the collective memory
results = index.query(
    "how was the Cognee memory cap solved?",
    agent="max",
    collections=["discoveries", "decisions", "keis"]
)
# Returns: cited, ranked results with source attribution
```

## Acceptance criteria

* LlamaIndex installed and configured on Vultr Sydney
* Successfully queries Weaviate codebase collection (test: "where is the Slack relay implemented?")
* Returns cited results with source attribution
* Cognee graph queries still functional as parallel path

## Dependencies

[KEI-46](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-46/kei-45-layer-3-idle-enforcement-daemon-systemd-polling-service-per) (Weaviate) must be installed first.

## Owner

Orion — after [KEI-46](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-46/kei-45-layer-3-idle-enforcement-daemon-systemd-polling-service-per) ships.

title: KEI-46 — Install Weaviate on Vultr Sydney: collective intelligence foundation
description:
## What

Install and configure Weaviate on the Vultr Sydney server. This is the foundation of the Keiracom collective intelligence architecture — the primary knowledge store that all agents share.

## Why

Cognee alone is a graph-first memory tool designed for agent session memory. It lacks native hybrid BM25 + vector search, production-grade multi-tenancy, and the retrieval precision needed for the collective experience cache. Weaviate provides all three.

## Spec

### Installation

* Self-hosted via Docker on Vultr Sydney (BSD-3 licence, $0 cost)
* Requires \~2-3GB RAM — accommodated by VX1 16GB server upgrade

### Five collections

```
codebase    — Agency_OS repo files, chunked and indexed
decisions   — ceo_memory entries, ratified decisions, KEI rationale
discoveries — structured agent discovery logs (4-field format)
sessions    — Claude Code tool call logs, Slack session history
keis        — All Linear KEIs, descriptions, comments, status
```

### Embeddings

* Use existing Gemini `gemini-embedding-001` (already configured across 3 GCP projects)
* No new API accounts required

### Hybrid search

* Enable BM25 + dense vector per collection
* BM25 for exact queries (KEI numbers, function names, error codes)
* Dense vector for semantic queries (concept-level retrieval)

### Schema metadata per document

At minimum: `source`, `agent`, `kei`, `timestamp`, `tags[]`, `content_type`

## Acceptance criteria

* Weaviate running as systemd service on Vultr Sydney
* 5 collections created with correct schema
* Gemini embeddings confirmed working (test query returns results)
* Survives server reboot (systemd enabled)

## Owner

Atlas — after [KEI-43](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-43/kei-43-agent-auto-start-systemd-services-boot-recovery-zero-dave-touch) (auto-start services) ships. Foundation for all subsequent collective intelligence KEIs.

title: KEI-45 — Layer 3 idle enforcement daemon
description: Mirror of Linear KEI-45. systemd --user daemon polling all 6 agents every 5min. At idle >=10min: bd ready -> check BUSY guard -> check rate-limit -> inject dispatch via tmux send-keys. Weekly-cap detection -> #ceo escalation + retry-suppress until reset date. ceo:boot_state_current jsonb state writes. Reuses elliot_polling_loop.py CALLSIGN_TO_TMUX + THROTTLE_RE + _capture_pane_tail.
close_reason: Superseded by KEI-45 scope flip ratified by Dave ts ~1778710800 — event-driven Realtime + Supabase trigger + bd watch + Linear webhook replaces polling-every-5min. PR #845 closed. KEI-22 (atomic claim foundation) is now prerequisite per Elliot dispatch; KEI-45 will rebuild on top of KEI-22 once that lands. Atlas preferred-agent per Dave; awaiting pull-model claim window.

title: KEI-45 — Layer 3 idle enforcement daemon (systemd polling service per agent)
description:
## Background

Idle-with-work-available agent state is a system gap, not a discipline problem. Layer 1 (discipline docs) + Layer 2 (outbound gates) + post-claim verification can't fix mechanical idleness. Need a Layer 3 daemon that mechanically dispatches when an agent goes idle with available work.

## Objective

A `systemd --user` polling service (one per agent OR one master polling all 6) that detects idleness, checks `bd ready` for unclaimed work, and mechanically injects dispatch into the agent's tmux. Cannot be bypassed by agent reasoning.

## Scope IN

1. **Heartbeat read** — every 5 minutes per agent, read `HEARTBEAT.md` (or equivalent activity timestamp) to determine last activity.
2. **Idle threshold #1 — 10 min**: if agent idle > 10 minutes, run `bd ready` (callsign-scoped); if unclaimed tasks exist, inject dispatch into agent's tmux session (using the canonical tmux name from `auto_session_recovery.py CALLSIGN_TO_TMUX`).
3. **Rate-limit handling**:
   * Detect Anthropic rate-limit signal from tmux pane (capture-pane + grep for "Server is temporarily limiting requests" or "You've used X% of your weekly limit").
   * Transient: exponential backoff + retry (don't escalate).
   * Weekly cap: surface to #ceo via `slack_relay.py -c ceo` with the reset date parsed from the limit message.
4. **Idle threshold #2 — 30 min with work available**: post to #ceo (escalation).
5. **Idle state write** — write current per-agent state (last_activity, idle_minutes, work_available, last_dispatch_at) to `ceo:boot_state_current` key in `public.ceo_memory` (jsonb).
6. **Pure Layer 3** — no agent-reasoning involvement. Service runs independently of claude processes. Restart=on-failure + WantedBy=default.target so it survives reboot.

## Scope OUT

* KEI-43 (agent auto-start) — pre-requisite, already shipped.
* KEI-44 (Cognee cap) — Orion's lane, parallel.
* Changing what `bd ready` returns (this consumes the existing API).
* Replacing KEI-35 detection-based recovery (complementary).

## Acceptance criteria

* One or six systemd `--user` units checked into `infra/systemd/idle-enforcement/` or similar.
* Idle dispatch demonstrably fires on a test idle scenario (manually leave an agent idle > 10 min, confirm a `bd ready` task gets injected).
* Rate-limit distinguishes transient (backoff in journal) from weekly-cap (#ceo post with reset date).
* `ceo:boot_state_current` populated and queryable: `SELECT value FROM public.ceo_memory WHERE key='ceo:boot_state_current';` shows fresh per-agent rows.
* Reboot test: service auto-starts on host reboot.

## Authoritative directive

Dave verbatim #ceo 2026-05-14:

> "Layer 3 idle enforcement daemon ... systemd polling service (every 5 minutes per agent) that reads HEARTBEAT.md, checks bd ready, injects dispatch, handles rate-limits, escalates idle > 30 min, writes ceo:boot_state_current. Atlas owns this after KEI-43 ships."

## Sequencing

* KEI-43 shipped 2026-05-13 sha afdb692a → KEI-45 starts now.
* Independent of KEI-44 (Cognee cap).

title: KEI-44 — Cognee subprocess 3GB memory cap (cgroup/ulimit)
description:
## Background

2026-05-13 OOM crash: Streams 3+4 ran uncapped for 4+ hours, consumed all 6GB server RAM, took down all 6 agent tmux sessions. Required manual Dave intervention to recover.

## Objective

Cognee subprocess cannot consume more than 3GB RAM. Hard stop at the cap; ingest fails fast rather than taking down the server.

## Scope IN

* Implementation: `ulimit -v` (virtual-memory) OR `systemd-run --user --scope -p MemoryMax=3G` OR a dedicated cgroup. Implementer's call — whichever cleanly enforces a hard ceiling without false-positive OOM kills inside the 3GB envelope.
* Wraps `scripts/cognee_ingest.py` invocations (and the uvicorn cognee.api.client serving on :8000 if it's the same memory pool).
* Verify: ingest a small batch, confirm the cap shows in `/proc/<pid>/limits` or the systemd scope's `MemoryMax` property.
* Failure mode: when the cap is hit, process dies cleanly with a logged error rather than dragging the server down.

## Scope OUT

* KEI-43 (systemd auto-start for agents). Separate work.
* Cognee architectural changes (chunking strategy, batch size). The cap is a containment, not a fix for memory growth.

## Acceptance criteria

* Cognee process limited to 3GB measurable via `/proc/<pid>/limits` or `systemctl --user status <scope>`.
* Reproducible test: run ingest with a 10-chunk synthetic batch; confirm cap is enforced.
* Documented runbook entry: "Resuming Streams 3+4 — always launch under the cap."

## Authoritative directive

Dave verbatim in #ceo 2026-05-13 23:38 AEST + 23:44 AEST:

> "Add a memory limit to the Cognee subprocess. ... Use ulimit or cgroup — your call on implementation. The cap should allow Cognee to run but hard-stop before it can take down the server. 6GB RAM on the server — cap Cognee at 3GB max."

Source: Slack #ceo ts 1778679489 + 1778679878 (David Stephens).

## Dependency

* Blocks: Streams 3+4 ingest resume (Cognee freeze in effect until this lands).
* Does NOT block KEI-43; they ship in parallel.
acceptance_criteria: Process memory hard-capped at 3GB measurable via /proc/<pid>/limits or systemctl scope; reproducible test with 10-chunk batch; runbook entry for resume procedure.
notes: Aiden routing recommendation: Orion (Cognee context). Atlas fallback. Dispatch via Aiden, Orion is Aiden's clone.

title: KEI-43 — agent auto-start systemd services (boot recovery, zero-Dave-touch)
description:
## Background

Server OOM crash on 2026-05-13 (Cognee Streams 3+4 uncapped) took down all 6 agent tmux sessions. Dave had to SSH in, create a tmux session, and run `claude` manually to recover. Unacceptable.

## Objective

Every agent restarts automatically on server reboot. Zero Dave involvement.

## Scope IN

* One `systemd --user` service per agent:
  * `elliot-agent.service`
  * `aiden-agent.service`
  * `max-agent.service`
  * `atlas-agent.service`
  * `orion-agent.service`
  * `scout-agent.service`
* Each service:
  1. Creates the tmux session (correct name from `CALLSIGN_TO_TMUX` map: `elliottbot / aiden / maxbot / atlas / orion / scout`) with the correct worktree as `-c <path>`.
  2. Starts Claude Code inside it using the existing `scripts/agent_session_launcher.sh <callsign>` so session-resume + UUID claim semantics are preserved.
  3. `WantedBy=default.target` — auto-starts on boot.
  4. `Restart=on-failure` with reasonable backoff so a crashed agent comes back without intervention.
* `systemctl --user enable` each service so they survive reboot permanently.
* Reference pattern: `openclaw.service` (existing).
* Post-merge verification: simulate reboot (or actually reboot test VM if safe); confirm all 6 agents come back within 2 minutes.

## Scope OUT

* Cognee memory cap (separate work, also gates ingest resume).
* Any agent business-logic change.
* KEI-35 detection-based auto-recovery — already shipped; this is the boot-side complement.

## Acceptance criteria

* 6 systemd user units checked into `infrastructure/systemd/` or equivalent.
* Each enabled via `systemctl --user enable`.
* Reboot test: all 6 agents online within 2 minutes, no Dave touch.
* Documented operator runbook entry: "How to recover from a server reboot (you don't — it auto-recovers)."

## Dependencies

* None blocking.
* Memory cap on Cognee is a separate KEI; both must ship before Streams 3+4 resume.

## Authoritative directive

Dave verbatim in #ceo 2026-05-13 23:50 AEST:

> "agent auto-start on boot. This is urgent and ships before anything else. ... A server reboot means all 6 agents come back online automatically, fully loaded, within 2 minutes. Dave touches nothing."

Source: Slack #ceo ts 1778680246 (David Stephens).
acceptance_criteria: 6 systemd user units checked in; each enabled via systemctl --user enable; reboot test → all 6 agents online within 2min, no Dave touch.
notes: Atlas dispatch (engineering, Elliot's clone). Coordinate with Aiden on overlap vs orion/agency-os-34s-systemd-installer branch.

title: KEI-41 — Telegram dead reference sweep (Slack/BS replacement)
description:
Per Dave verbatim ts ~1778666800 + Resolution A lift ts ~1778671100 (URGENT assignment to Aiden).

Audit found 7 active Telegram-code locations + 8 legacy-named-Slack-backed paths.

Active Telegram-code (REMOVE/REPLACE per Dave):

- Skill doc referencing Telegram

Legacy-named-Slack-backed (NAMING CLEAN, no code change):

URGENT P0 per Dave assignment.

title: KEI-42 — ceo:governance_current: single Supabase key documenting complete live governance state
description:
## Problem

No single queryable record exists of the complete current governance architecture. A new CEO session can load `ceo:rule:*` keys and strategic context, but has blind spots on:

* Exact Enforcer rule list (what rules are active, what each rule triggers on)
* Beads schema (what `bd claim`, `bd ready`, `bd complete`, `bd check-claim` do internally)
* Relay architecture (TG-\* prefix legacy, slack_relay.py structure, Socket Mode vs webhooks)
* Three-store save current state (Drive archived, what the two active stores are)
* CONCUR gate logic (exact regex, what triggers it, what bypasses it)
* Fabrication Defense layers (all three active? current state?)
* Better Stack monitors (what's monitored, what channels alerts route to)
* Agent worktree structure (all 6 callsigns, paths, IDENTITY.md locations)
* Cognee integration state (what's wired, what's pending [KEI-7](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-7/cognee-universal-memory-layer-phase-2-wire-into-agent-sessions) wiring)

## Required

Elliot produces a single Supabase key: `ceo:governance_current`

Schema:

```json
{
  "written_at": "ISO timestamp",
  "written_by": "callsign",
  "enforcer": {
    "rules": [{"id": "R1", "name": "...", "triggers_on": "...", "action": "...", "known_issues": "..."}],
    "known_misfires": ["R2 fires on completion actions — KEI-38 fix pending"]
  },
  "beads": {
    "commands": {"bd_claim": "...", "bd_ready": "...", "bd_complete": "...", "bd_check_claim": "pending KEI-22"},
    "schema": "...",
    "layer3_status": "tracking only — hard block pending KEI-22"
  },
  "relay": {
    "architecture": "...",
    "tg_prefix_origin": "Telegram gateway legacy — being removed KEI-41",
    "slack_relay_path": "...",
    "socket_mode": true/false
  },
  "concur_gate": {
    "regex": "exact pattern",
    "triggers_on": ["..."],
    "does_not_trigger_on": ["..."],
    "known_issues": "KEI-38 fix pending"
  },
  "three_store_save": {
    "store_1": "Supabase ceo_memory (primary SSOT)",
    "store_2": "cis_directive_metrics",
    "store_3": "Linear issue comment",
    "drive_manual": "ARCHIVED — no longer written to"
  },
  "fabrication_defense": {
    "layer_1": {"name": "Discipline", "file": "...", "status": "active/inactive"},
    "layer_2": {"name": "Outbound gate", "file": "...", "status": "active/inactive"},
    "layer_3": {"name": "Replay-on-claim", "file": "...", "env_var": "REPLAY_ON_CLAIM_ENABLED", "status": "active/inactive"}
  },
  "better_stack": {
    "monitors": ["..."],
    "alert_routing": {"critical": "#ceo", "routine": "#execution"},
    "known_issues": ["False positive on clean process exit — fix pending"]
  },
  "agents": [
    {"callsign": "Elliot", "worktree": "...", "identity_md": "...", "service": "..."}
  ],
  "cognee": {
    "status": "Streams 3+4 running",
    "wired_into_agents": true/false,
    "pending": "KEI-7 wiring"
  },
  "pending_governance_keis": ["KEI-22", "KEI-37", "KEI-38", "KEI-39", "KEI-40", "KEI-41"]
}
```

## What NOT to include

* Build history or directive log
* Pipeline architecture details
* Aspirational design — current live state only
* Anything that belongs in Linear (issues) or git (code)

## Acceptance criteria

* `ceo:governance_current` exists in Supabase
* A new CEO session can read it and know exactly what governance is live, what is pending, and what is misfiring
* Updated within 24 hours of any governance change

## Owner

Elliot — read actual files, query actual state. No summarising from memory. LAW I-A applies. Ships before Elliot restart.

title: KEI-41 — Dead reference sweep: remove all Telegram code, replace with Slack
description:
## Problem

Elliot was found mid-build on a "Telegram alert system." Two compounding failures:

1. **Telegram is a dead channel** — system uses Slack exclusively
2. **Better Stack already exists as the alert system** — it is active, configured, and fired today (Stream 2 completion false-positive). Building a parallel alert system without checking what already exists is the exact failure this rule prevents.

## Required — two parts

### Part 1: Intent audit

What was the Telegram build trying to solve? Compare to Better Stack's current coverage. If covered — build was unnecessary, kill it. If genuine gap exists — extend Better Stack, do not build parallel infrastructure.

### Part 2: Telegram reference sweep

Full codebase audit for all Telegram references:

* `import telegram` or `from telegram`
* `TelegramBot`, `TelegramClient`
* `TG-*` prefixes in non-display code
* API keys or tokens referencing Telegram
* Config files with Telegram endpoints
* Any `scripts/` or `src/` files with Telegram logic

Report every file and line number. Do not remove without review.

After report: remove all dead Telegram code. Replace any legitimate notification intent with Better Stack configuration or Slack (#ceo channel via existing relay).

## Standing rule

Before building any monitoring, alerting, or notification system: check Better Stack first. If the capability exists — configure it. Never build parallel infrastructure.

## Owner

Elliot — audit first, removal second. Ships before Elliot restart.

title: KEI-39 — Pre-execution claim protocol: agents must signal before every task AND subtask transition
description:
## Cancelled — superseded by Supabase atomic claiming

The intent of this KEI was: no agent starts work without a formal claim recorded.

This is now enforced mechanically at the database level by the Supabase tasks table (created 2026-05-14). The `SELECT FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED` pattern in [KEI-22](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-22/kei-22-automated-linear-beads-bidirectional-sync) (Supabase SSOT — replacing Beads with direct Supabase claiming) makes it structurally impossible to start work without a claim being recorded. No markdown update to orchestrator.md required — the database IS the protocol.

Superseded by [KEI-22](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-22/kei-22-automated-linear-beads-bidirectional-sync) (Supabase SSOT) + [KEI-68](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-68/kei-68-living-governance-layer-rules-in-supabase-not-static-markdown) (living governance layer in Supabase).

title: KEI-38 — Concur-gate regex too broad: silencing Max's direct posts
description:
## Problem

The concur-gate regex is catching Max's legitimate factual responses and converting them to CONCUR-REQUEST stubs. Max cannot post directly to Slack. All his posts are being relayed by Elliot's bot, breaking visibility — Dave sees Max's content tagged as Elliot.

Observed: Max's "CONCUR with EMPIRICAL CORRECTION on step N" pattern trips the gate. Max's direct factual status answers trip the gate. Max's response to a direct Dave query trips the gate.

Root cause: the regex pattern is matching on the word "CONCUR" appearing anywhere in a post, not just governance concurrence actions.

## Impact

* Dave cannot see which agent is posting
* Max's independent perspective is invisible — undermines the peer governance model
* Elliot relay workaround masks the problem without fixing it

## Fix

Tighten the concur-gate regex to match only the specific governance patterns:

* `[CONCUR:callsign]` — explicit concurrence action
* `[BLOCK:callsign]` — explicit block action

Must NOT match:

* Factual posts containing the word "concur" in prose
* "CONCUR with EMPIRICAL CORRECTION" style review responses
* Direct answers to Dave queries

## Acceptance criteria

* Max can post directly to #execution without relay
* Governance concurrence actions still gate correctly
* Dave sees agent callsigns accurately in Slack

## Owner

Elliot — post-compact. Aiden peer reviews regex change.
notes: PR #844 opened, branch scout/kei38-concur-gate-regex off main afdb692a. Full CI green (lint/tests/mypy/sonar/frontend/dead-ref/migration). Local 28/28 + 213/213 pass. Awaiting triple-FINAL (Aiden + Elliot + Max). Self-merge after FINALs.

title: KEI-37 — CEO session boot state: single ceo_memory key for reliable new session startup
description:
## Problem

When a new CEO session starts on [claude.ai](<http://claude.ai>), there is no reliable single source of current state. The CEO must reconstruct context from:

* [claude.ai](<http://claude.ai>) memory edits (stale — updated periodically, not real-time)
* Project knowledge files (stale — static snapshots, months old)
* Google Drive Manual (current only if LAW XV compliance is perfect)
* 30+ fragmented ceo_memory keys (session ends, directive completions, individual ratified decisions)
* Linear board (unreliable — [KEI-22](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-22/kei-22-automated-linear-beads-bidirectional-sync) not yet fixed)

This fragmentation caused real operational failures today: stale memory led to bad dispatch decisions.

## Solution

A single `ceo:boot_state_current` key in ceo_memory that a new CEO session can query in ONE call and get a complete, current picture.

## Schema

```json
{
  "written_at": "ISO timestamp",

  },

  "supabase_project": "jatzvazlbusedwsnqxzr"
}
```

## When it gets written

1. **Hourly cron** — Elliot orchestrator writes a fresh snapshot every hour during peak hours
2. **On PR merge to main** — updated after every significant state change
3. **On session end** — each agent session end hook updates it
4. **On Dave directive completion** — after every ratified decision

## CEO boot path (new protocol)

1. Query `SELECT value FROM ceo_memory WHERE key = 'ceo:boot_state_current'` → current state in one call
2. If needs_dave is non-empty → address those first
3. Read Drive Manual for narrative context if needed
4. Query specific ratified decision keys from `ratified_decisions` map as needed

Replaces: read Drive Manual first → query 30+ keys → reconstruct state manually

## Acceptance criteria

* `ceo:boot_state_current` exists and is never more than 60 minutes stale during peak hours
* A new CEO session can read this one key and know: what's in progress, what's blocking, what needs Dave, where the restart gate stands
* Project knowledge files and [claude.ai](<http://claude.ai>) memory edits become supplementary only — not primary boot path

## Owner

Elliot — post-compact, after [KEI-22](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-22/kei-22-automated-linear-beads-bidirectional-sync) sync work. This is foundational infrastructure for CEO session reliability.

title: KEI-34 — Polling loop: idle-with-unblocked-work escalation to #ceo
description:
Expanded scope (2026-05-13):

ORIGINAL: Polling loop detects idle-with-unblocked-work → posts to #ceo immediately. 1-cycle threshold (60s peak / 60min off-peak).

ADDITION 1 — Better Stack dispatch heartbeat: Wire dedicated BS heartbeat to dispatch outcome. Loop ran + dispatched OR found nothing = heartbeat fires. Loop ran + idle agents exist + no dispatch = no heartbeat = BS alerts Dave within 2 minutes. Dave gets alerted without checking tmux.

ADDITION 2 — Dirty worktree detection: If auto-pull has been skipped more than 2 consecutive times due to dirty state → alert #ceo immediately. Root cause of today's silent stale-code failure.

ADDITION 3 — Background process awareness: Idle detection must distinguish "tmux pane quiet" from "no active work." Check CPU/subprocess activity before flagging agent as idle.

Owner: Aiden. All additions in one PR.

title: KEI-33 — R13 enforcer: blocker requiring CEO decision → #ceo immediately
description:
## Held — implement in [KEI-68](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-68/kei-68-living-governance-layer-rules-in-supabase-not-static-markdown) (living governance layer) instead

The rule itself is valid: any blocker requiring a CEO or Dave decision must post to #ceo immediately.

However, implementing this as a hardcoded Enforcer regex rule is the wrong approach. [KEI-68](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-68/kei-68-living-governance-layer-rules-in-supabase-not-static-markdown) (living governance layer — rules in Supabase, not static markdown) will implement all governance rules as structured data in a governance_rules table. This rule ships there as a single database row, not as a regex update across 6 worktrees.

Hold until [KEI-68](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-68/kei-68-living-governance-layer-rules-in-supabase-not-static-markdown) is in build. Orion — move to [KEI-36](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-36/kei-36-fix-heartbeatmd-placeholder-population-in-pre-compact-hook) (HEARTBEAT.md placeholder fix in pre-compact hook).

Update CLAUDE.md model field and HEARTBEAT.md model declaration per worktree. Update [KEI-31](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-31/kei-31-controlled-context-threshold-restart-with-full-memory-injection) gate thresholds to reflect correct context windows per model. Update ceo_memory with new callsign→model mapping. Eliminates rate limit hits from all-Opus concurrent sessions.

title: KEI-31 — Controlled context-threshold restart with full memory injection
description:
Controlled agent restart with full context injection. Callsign → model assignment per CLAUDE.md §"Callsign → Model Assignment" (ratified 2026-05-12 ts \~1778625870):

* Opus 4.7 (1M context): elliot, aiden, max
* Sonnet 4.6 (200K context): atlas, orion, scout
* Haiku 4.5 (200K context): scout (mechanical tasks only)

THREE GATES — percentage-based, model-agnostic (absolute thresholds derived from each agent's running model's context window):

* Gate 1 (70%): anti-amnesia capsule writes, agent warned
  * Opus 4.7 = 700K · Sonnet 4.6 = 140K · Haiku 4.5 = 140K
* Gate 2 (85%): on next task completion → Elliot triggers tmux restart
  * Opus 4.7 = 850K · Sonnet 4.6 = 170K · Haiku 4.5 = 170K
* Hard ceiling (95%): Elliot intervenes regardless of task state
  * Opus 4.7 = 950K · Sonnet 4.6 = 190K · Haiku 4.5 = 190K

CONTEXT INJECTION BUNDLE on restart:

1. Anti-amnesia capsule (20 most critical active facts)
2. Cognee session query (current task context, recent decisions, open items)
3. Active KEI issue + Beads task state (current step, blockers, next)
4. Session UUID (audit trail continuity)

POST-RESTART: agent posts one-line acknowledgement to #execution confirming injection bundle was read. Mechanical proof context landed.

HEARTBEAT.md must declare both the configured model (per CLAUDE.md table) and the running --model flag at startup. Gate logic reads the running model's context window to compute absolute thresholds. opusplan is NOT a valid CLI alias (CEO correction 2026-05-12) — plan-mode-first is a working convention for Aiden / Max, not a flag.

title: KEI-23 — Cognee Streams 2/3/4 data ingest (Supabase rows + sessions + mem0)
description:
CANCELLED — 2026-05-14

Stream 2 completed successfully (1,678 chunks, VQ1 passed). Streams 3 and 4 are cancelled.

Reason: The new hybrid memory architecture ([KEI-46](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-46/kei-45-layer-3-idle-enforcement-daemon-systemd-polling-service-per) through [KEI-62](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-62/kei-60-embedding-model-independence-raw-text-storage-re-embed-corpuspy)) makes Streams 3+4 redundant. Session history (Stream 3: 1,289 chunks of session turns) and mem0-rescued rows (Stream 4: 82 chunks) are better served by [KEI-48](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-48/kei-46-install-weaviate-on-vultr-sydney-collective-intelligence) (auto-indexing pipeline), which backfills full Slack history and all ceo_memory entries into Weaviate with hybrid search, sanitisation, and durable queue backing.

Loading raw session turns into Cognee's graph database provides marginal value — session history is not relationship-dense data and does not benefit from graph traversal. Weaviate hybrid search retrieves it faster and more accurately.

Cognee remains as a graph reasoning sidecar. If a targeted architectural knowledge ingestion (KEI decisions, ratified rules, governance relationships) is needed for Cognee specifically, that will be a separate scoped KEI.

Stream 2 outcome: complete and successful. [KEI-23](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-23/kei-23-cognee-streams-234-data-ingest-supabase-rows-sessions-mem0) marked Done on the basis of Stream 2 completion.

Max released to [KEI-62](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-62/kei-60-embedding-model-independence-raw-text-storage-re-embed-corpuspy) (epsilon-greedy exploration).

title: KEI-22 — Automated Linear ↔ Beads bidirectional sync
description:
## Priority: ABSOLUTE #1 — completes the idle agent fix

[KEI-63](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-63/kei-61-explicit-deprecation-layer-bd-deprecate-command-environment) shipped and solved the first half: agent finishes a task → next task injected within 2 seconds.

This KEI solves the second half: `bd ready` currently reads from Beads which can be 31+ minutes stale. When Beads is stale, `bd ready` returns empty even when 19 tasks exist in Linear. The hook fires, finds nothing, agent waits. Idle problem persists.

[KEI-22](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-22/kei-22-automated-linear-beads-bidirectional-sync) **makes bd ready read from Supabase as the single source of truth. Stale queue is permanently impossible.**

```
KEI-63 ✓  agent completes → next task injected immediately
KEI-22 ✗  bd ready reads Supabase → always accurate → never empty when work exists
```

Together: agents never sit idle when work is available. Ever.

## What to build

### 1\. Supabase tasks table as canonical SSOT

```sql
CREATE TABLE tasks (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,           -- KEI identifier
  title TEXT NOT NULL,
  status TEXT DEFAULT 'available',
  priority INT DEFAULT 2,
  claimed_by TEXT,
  claimed_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
  dependencies TEXT[],
  tags TEXT[],
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE UNIQUE INDEX tasks_active_claim 
  ON tasks(claimed_by) 
  WHERE status = 'active';
```

### 2\. bd claim — SELECT FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED

```sql
-- Atomic claim: exactly one agent wins, others skip to next
BEGIN;
  SELECT id FROM tasks
  WHERE status = 'available'
    AND (dependencies IS NULL OR dependencies = '{}')
    AND NOT (claimed_by IS NOT NULL AND status = 'active')
  ORDER BY priority ASC, created_at ASC
  LIMIT 1
  FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED;

  UPDATE tasks 
  SET status = 'active', claimed_by = $agent, claimed_at = NOW()
  WHERE id = $selected_id AND claimed_by IS NULL;
COMMIT;
```

### 3\. bd ready — reads Supabase directly, never Beads

Returns available unblocked unclaimed tasks ordered by priority. No Beads involved. Supabase is the data.

### 4\. Linear bidirectional sync

* Linear → Supabase: webhook on KEI create/update → writes to tasks table
* Supabase → Linear: on claim/complete → updates Linear status and assignee
* Linear remains the visual display adapter. Supabase is the truth.

### 5\. bd complete — updates Supabase + triggers hook

Updates tasks table status to 'done', clears claimed_by, triggers [KEI-63](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-63/kei-61-explicit-deprecation-layer-bd-deprecate-command-environment) hook to find and inject next task.

## Acceptance criteria

* `bd ready` reads from Supabase, not Beads
* Agent runs `bd ready` with 19 tasks in Linear → sees 19 tasks (not empty)
* Atomic claim: 6 agents simultaneously claiming → exactly 1 wins, others get next available
* `bd complete` → Supabase updated → [KEI-63](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-63/kei-61-explicit-deprecation-layer-bd-deprecate-command-environment) hook fires → next task injected within 2 seconds
* Linear stays updated as display adapter throughout
* Stale queue is structurally impossible — no sync lag

## Owner

Scout — start immediately. Everything else in the queue waits for this.

title: Migrate api_agent_cold_start creds to vault resolution (remove Phase 1 carve-outs)
description:
## Context

PRs #1358 and #1359 (2026-05-30, V1-battery unblock) added three credentials to `_TMUX_OPERATIONAL_PASSTHROUGH` in `src/dispatcher/main.py` as explicit Phase 1 carve-outs:

* `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` — read by the Anthropic SDK at spawn time in `api_agent_cold_start`
* `DATABASE_URL` — read by `_dsn()` for attribution INSERT connection
* `SUPABASE_DB_DSN` — fallback DSN also read by `_dsn()`

The dispatcher uses `env -i` to spawn tmux agent sessions, which scrubs the environment. The original design intent, stated in the code comment, was explicit: \*"Credentials (DATABASE_URL, *\_API_KEY, …) are deliberately NOT whitelisted → scrubbed."* The Phase 1 carve-out breaks that invariant so V1 chain hops can make Anthropic API calls and write attribution rows.

## Why this is load-bearing

Without `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` in the passthrough, every chain hop fails pre-API-call. Without `DATABASE_URL`/`SUPABASE_DB_DSN`, `insert_attribution` fails silently and the per-task A$10 ceiling gate (PR #1354) has nothing to SUM — Gate 1 is toothless.

## Acceptance criteria

1. `api_agent_cold_start` resolves `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` via a Vault agent / secret manager at spawn time — not from `os.environ` passthrough.
2. DB connection string is similarly resolved via Vault or injected via a credential broker at spawn time.
3. All three entries (`ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `DATABASE_URL`, `SUPABASE_DB_DSN`) are removed from `_TMUX_OPERATIONAL_PASSTHROUGH`.
4. The code comment is restored to its original intent: credentials scrubbed, not passed through.
5. Existing V1 chain battery tests pass end-to-end with vault-resolved creds.

## References

* PR #1358 — ANTHROPIC_API_KEY carve-out
* PR #1359 — DATABASE_URL + SUPABASE_DB_DSN carve-out
* `src/dispatcher/main.py` — `_TMUX_OPERATIONAL_PASSTHROUGH` tuple
* `src/keiracom_system/vault/api_agent_cold_start.py` — `_dsn()` + Anthropic SDK call site

title: V1 chain role-substitution: ROLE_FOR_STEP ordered fallback (post-battery)

Fix: fan each step to an ordered fallback list; route to first available spawn target. Depends on spawn-target availability signal (build TBD). This is Option B deferred from Agency_OS-yvlr51 decision 2026-05-30. Do not start until V1 battery completes.

title: V1 chain ROLE_FOR_STEP ordered-fallback — chain-freeze fix (post-battery)
description:
Follow-up to Agency_OS-yvlr51 (PR #1353 Option A) — files Option B that the yvlr51 dispatch decision deferred until after the battery.

## Problem (yvlr51 Gap 3 — Atlas scrutiny 2026-05-30)

The V1 chain's actual specific-callsign-stuck mechanism lives in src/keiracom_system/chain/v1_chain_orchestrator.py:
- ROLE_FOR_STEP (line 56) hardcodes aiden_plan -> aiden, max_challenge -> max, nova_build -> nova, orion_spec -> orion, atlas_safety -> atlas.
- FROM_TO_STEP (line 75) mirrors the inverse.

When a hop's named callsign is at context cap / rate-limited / dead-tmux, /dispatcher/spawn fires at keiracom.dispatch.<callsign> with no available consumer; the chain freezes at that step.

Agency_OS-yvlr51 PR #1353 (Option A) fixes only the CONCUR-REQUEST signaling layer; release semantics were already any-peer. It does NOT touch the chain's role->callsign mapping, so deliberator-at-cap during a V1 chain run remains operational risk Elliot explicitly accepted for the battery.

## Fix (Option B)

Fan ROLE_FOR_STEP from single callsign to ordered fallback list per step:
- aiden_plan: [aiden, max, elliot]
- max_challenge: [max, aiden, elliot]
- nova_build: [nova, atlas, orion]
- orion_spec: [orion, atlas, nova]
- atlas_safety: [atlas, orion, nova]

dispatch() and advance_step() iterate the list, dispatching at the first available consumer. Availability signal source: TBD — options include:
1. /dispatcher/spawn returning a 'no consumer' status that triggers fallback iteration.
2. Polling tmux session liveness + last-message age (existing reset_all_handler.py:10 hints at this).
3. ceo:agent_health key when KEI-63-style polling loop ships (same dependency as yvlr51 future-filter path).

FROM_TO_STEP needs the inverse mapping: each callsign can complete any step it's in the eligible list for.

## Acceptance

- ROLE_FOR_STEP becomes dict[str, list[str]].
- dispatch + advance_step iterate fallback list at /spawn time.
- Availability signal wired (mechanism TBD as part of design).
- Test: aiden unavailable -> max receives aiden_plan dispatch; chain advances normally.
- Test: all eligible peers unavailable -> chain pauses with an escalation event, not silent freeze.

## Why deferred (Elliot 2026-05-30)

Battery imminent. Option B regression risk pre-battery outweighs the unblock value; deliberator-at-context-cap during the battery happy-path test is operational risk Elliot explicitly accepted. File for post-battery.

## Authoritative trace

- Elliot dispatch 2026-05-30 ~01:00 AEST — 'Option B (ROLE_FOR_STEP fan-out) is correct long-term but invasive pre-battery'
- Atlas scrutiny 2026-05-30 — outbox file /tmp/telegram-relay-elliot/inbox/atlas_yvlr51_scrutiny_1780103043134.json (Gap 3)
- PR #1353 (yvlr51 Option A merged this signaling fix)

title: fix(dispatcher): CHAIN_STEP env injection missing from NATS→scripts spawn path
description: scripts/dispatcher/_spawn.py (NATS→nats_tmux_bridge→inbox path) has ZERO envelope→env wiring. src/dispatcher/main.py has AGENT_* auto-injection (lines 441-443 + 513-515) but the consumer loop uses a DIFFERENT path. chain_step from envelope never becomes CHAIN_STEP in spawned agent env — nd3b suppress gate never fires for V1 chain steps. Fix: either (i) add envelope→AGENT_* auto-injection to _spawn.py mirroring FastAPI path, or (ii) migrate consumer to HTTP POST /dispatcher/spawn. Atlas discovered empirically via grep (zero env-from-envelope in _spawn.py). nd3b fallback (CHAIN_STEP or AGENT_CHAIN_STEP) already future-proofs for whichever fix lands.

comments.author: Aiden
comments.text:
[ORION research 2026-05-29] Read scripts/dispatcher/_spawn.py in full + traced caller + checked Atlas's #1339 actual spawn mechanism. Findings:

== THE 3-LINE LITERAL FIX DOES NOT WORK AS WRITTEN ==

`for k, v in envelope.items(): env.setdefault(f"AGENT_{k.upper()}", str(v))` cannot be dropped in for three reasons:

1. `envelope` is NOT in handle_envelope's signature (scripts/dispatcher/_spawn.py:40-50). The function takes callsign/db/repo_root/inbox_root/mode/claude_bin/resume_context/popen/clock — no envelope param.

2. NO `env` dict exists in the function. Lines 88-94 call `popen([binary], stdin=PIPE, stdout=PIPE, stderr=PIPE, text=True)` — there is no env construction, anywhere.

3. `popen` does NOT receive an `env=` kwarg. The subprocess inherits the parent process's full env by default; nothing in this function controls the child's env.

== ACTUAL MINIMUM VIABLE FIX (5 lines + caller change) ==

scripts/dispatcher/_spawn.py:
- line 23 area: add `import os`.
- line 40-50 (signature): add `envelope: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None,` parameter.
- BEFORE line 88 (popen call): insert
    env = dict(os.environ)
    if envelope:
        for k, v in envelope.items():
            if v is not None:
                env.setdefault(f"AGENT_{k.upper()}", str(v))
- line 88-94 (popen call): add `env=env,` kwarg.

scripts/dispatcher/dispatcher_main.py:108-115 (the call-site): add `envelope=envelope,` (envelope IS in scope per the `for claimed, envelope in _inbox_loop.iter_claimed_envelopes(...)` loop above).

== CRITICAL CAVEAT — does this fix the nd3b gate? PROBABLY NOT ==

This path's `popen` spawns `claude` directly (line 82-99), NOT `agent_cold_start.py`. The nd3b notify-suppression gate lives in `agent_cold_start.run()` (`os.environ.get("CHAIN_STEP", "")` after save_atoms) — that function is not invoked on this code path. `claude` itself does not read CHAIN_STEP / AGENT_CHAIN_STEP.

So injecting AGENT_CHAIN_STEP into this popen's env lands the var inside the spawned `claude` process… but nothing there reads it. The fix is functionally inert for the nd3b gate.

The two spawn paths today:
- Path A (scripts/dispatcher/_spawn.py): per-callsign inbox-watcher → popen(claude). Long-lived per-callsign claude session. Reads stdin prompt. Does NOT run agent_cold_start.
- Path B (src/dispatcher/main.py): work-loop ephemeral spawn → tmux/container with DEFAULT_AGENT_COMMAND="python3 -m src.keiracom_system.vault.agent_cold_start". This IS the path where the nd3b gate fires (Atlas's #1339 + Nova's nd3b AGENT_CHAIN_STEP fallback closes the loop).

== ATLAS #1339's ACTUAL CHAIN SPAWN PATH ==

Inspected origin/atlas/oevr-chain-consumer-loop: the consumer (`run_consumer`) subscribes to NATS keiracom.agent.handoff (orion zr7e.9), calls `_advance_step_async(chain_id, completed_step, atom_id)`, which `await _publish_async(env, role)` publishes the next envelope to NATS (line 260). NO HTTP /dispatcher/spawn call, NO inbox file write. Pure NATS publish.

The NATS dispatch then lands via `scripts/orchestrator/nats_tmux_bridge.py` (verified — it `write_inbox_file(envelope, subject, inbox_dir)` at line 42) → so eventually it DOES reach this scripts/dispatcher/_spawn.py path via the per-callsign inbox-watcher. So 0bgt's path IS reached.

But — the spawn there is a fresh popen(claude) on the persistent callsign session, not agent_cold_start. The nd3b gate is on Path B (work-loop), not Path A (per-callsign chain).

== RECOMMENDATION FOR ATLAS BEFORE BUILDING ==

Confirm intent with Elliot/architecture:
- (i) Is the goal "match src/dispatcher/main.py's AGENT_* parity in scripts/dispatcher/_spawn.py for future use"? → ship the 5-line fix above. Harmless, parity-friendly.
- (ii) Is the goal "make the nd3b gate fire on chain hops dispatched via NATS→bridge→inbox"? → the fix above alone won't do it. Either (a) make _spawn.py spawn agent_cold_start instead of claude direct (significant refactor — changes the per-callsign session model), OR (b) have nd3b also gate on something the persistent claude session controls (e.g. a prompt marker or a state file).

(i) is small and reversible; (ii) needs an architecture call. Current state is that V1 chain notify-suppression already works end-to-end on Path B via #1337 + #1339 + qjl7-fix (Nova's AGENT_CHAIN_STEP fallback in nd3b). Whether Path A also needs it depends on whether chain hops ever run through the per-callsign claude sessions vs only through ephemeral work-loop agent_cold_start spawns.

Posting this as research; no code written. Atlas — over to you with the heads-up.

title: feat(dispatcher): inject CHAIN_STEP env var at spawn from chain envelope
description: Dispatcher spawn handler must inject CHAIN_STEP=<chain_step> into agent env when envelope carries chain_step key. Required for nd3b notify suppression gate to work end-to-end.

comments.author: Aiden
comments.text:
# Agency_OS-qjl7 — second opinion: CHAIN_STEP env injection in dispatcher spawn path

**Reviewer:** scout 2026-05-29 (research-only second opinion, per Elliot dispatch). Owner = Orion.

---

## Verdict — the injection mechanism already exists. No new dispatcher code required if the AGENT_* convention is acceptable.

Both spawn-backend translators in `src/dispatcher/main.py` already auto-inject every leftover `spawn_kwargs` field as an `AGENT_<KEY>` env var. Same exact pattern, twice:

**Container backend — `_container_spawn_kwargs`, `src/dispatcher/main.py:441-443`:**
```python
for meta_key, meta_val in sk.items():
    if meta_val is not None:
        env.setdefault(f"AGENT_{meta_key.upper()}", str(meta_val))
```

**Tmux backend — `_tmux_spawn_kwargs`, `src/dispatcher/main.py:513-515`:**
```python
for meta_key, meta_val in sk.items():
    if meta_val is not None:
        env.setdefault(f"AGENT_{meta_key.upper()}", str(meta_val))
```

These run AFTER the translator pops the structural keys (image/name/port/env/health_path/extra_args for container; session_name/working_dir/command/env for tmux). Everything else in `sk` — chain_step, task_id, atom_id, brief, tenant_id, callsign, … — auto-becomes `AGENT_<UPPERCASE_KEY>` in the spawned agent's env. `setdefault` means an explicit caller-supplied `env["AGENT_CHAIN_STEP"]` wins.

So if Orion's chain consumer calls `/dispatcher/spawn` with
```json
{"backend": "tmux", "key": "...", "spawn_kwargs": {"chain_step": "aiden_plan", "task_id": "X", "atom_id": "Y", "brief": "..."}}
```
the agent is spawned with `AGENT_CHAIN_STEP=aiden_plan`, `AGENT_TASK_ID=X`, `AGENT_ATOM_ID=Y`, `AGENT_BRIEF=...` already in env. Zero new code in `dispatcher/main.py`.

## End-to-end flow validated against origin/main

1. `@app.post("/dispatcher/spawn")` at `src/dispatcher/main.py:724-731` (`dispatcher_spawn`). `SpawnRequest.spawn_kwargs` is a free-form dict — no Pydantic restriction on the chain field.
2. Pre-spawn gates: idempotency (744-766), cost breaker (770-797), budget ceiling (801+), governance/spend/rate. None of these inspect or filter `chain_step`.
3. Translator dispatch (`src/dispatcher/main.py:855-862`):
   ```python
   if backend is Backend.CONTAINER:
       spawn_kwargs_effective = _container_spawn_kwargs(req.key, spawn_kwargs_effective)
   elif backend is Backend.TMUX:
       spawn_kwargs_effective = _tmux_spawn_kwargs(req.key, spawn_kwargs_effective)
   ```
4. Inside the translators (442 / 515), the AGENT_* loop fires.
5. For tmux: `_tmux_scrubbed_command` (line 491) wraps `env -i <K=v K=v …> sh -c '<agent_command>'` — so the full env dict (Vault bootstrap + operational passthrough + AGENT_* metadata) reaches the agent process. **No scrubbed-tmux allowlist excludes `AGENT_CHAIN_STEP`** (confirmed: only `_TMUX_OPERATIONAL_PASSTHROUGH` re-adds inherited host vars; the AGENT_* dict is built independently and merged into the env dict that env -i passes).
6. For container: the env dict (Vault bootstrap + AGENT_* metadata) is the entire credential surface (P10 cold-start, Agency_OS-8dvl) — Docker isolation means the container sees ONLY this env. AGENT_CHAIN_STEP is preserved.

## Naming decision Orion needs to make

The KEI title names the env var as **`CHAIN_STEP`** (bare). The existing dispatcher convention produces **`AGENT_CHAIN_STEP`** (prefixed). Pick one:

- **(a) Follow convention → `AGENT_CHAIN_STEP`** (recommended). Consistent with `AGENT_TASK_ID`, `AGENT_TENANT_ID`, `AGENT_BRIEF`, `AGENT_CALLSIGN`. Dispatcher diff = **zero lines**. The change is on the **agent side**: whoever reads chain_step (likely `src/keiracom_system/vault/agent_cold_start.py` for spawn-time chain context, or `src/dispatcher/main.py:dispatcher_task_complete` for nd3b's intermediate-step suppression) reads `os.environ.get("AGENT_CHAIN_STEP")` instead of `os.environ.get("CHAIN_STEP")`.

- **(b) Bare `CHAIN_STEP`** — requires a special-case carve-out in both translators. ~3 lines per translator (~6 total):
  ```python
  cs = sk.pop("chain_step", None)
  if cs is not None:
      env.setdefault("CHAIN_STEP", str(cs))
  ```
  inserted BEFORE the AGENT_* loop so chain_step doesn't double-emit. This breaks the convention but matches the KEI's literal naming.

**Recommend (a).** It produces zero dispatcher diff and keeps the AGENT_* contract clean — every metadata field becomes an `AGENT_*` env. The only callers that touch chain_step (the agent + the suppression branch in `dispatcher_task_complete`) follow the convention.

## Caveats / things to confirm

1. **Caller responsibility — who calls `/dispatcher/spawn` with `chain_step` in `spawn_kwargs`?** On origin/main today the work-loop consumer (`src/keiracom_system/work_loop/consumer.py`) is the only consumer calling `/dispatcher/spawn`. The V1 chain envelope is published by `v1_chain_orchestrator.dispatch / advance_step` to NATS `keiracom.dispatch.<role>`. Atlas's `5b4b7a819 [ATLAS] feat(chain): v1_chain_orchestrator consumer loop — handoff → advance_step` (`Agency_OS-oevr`, NOT yet on origin/main) is the consumer that translates NATS dispatch envelopes → `/dispatcher/spawn` calls. qjl7's wiring presumes oevr's consumer puts `chain_step` (+ task_id / atom_id / brief) into `spawn_kwargs` when calling `/dispatcher/spawn`. Confirm with Atlas that oevr does this; if it does not, that one-line addition lives in oevr's consumer, not in `dispatcher/main.py`.

2. **`env.setdefault` semantics.** An explicit caller-supplied `env["AGENT_CHAIN_STEP"] = "..."` wins over the AGENT_* loop (setdefault no-ops if the key is already set). That's the correct ordering — gives the orchestrator an override knob if ever needed — but worth noting.

3. **nd3b suppression coordination.** Nd3b (PR #1337, merged as `658371996`) suppresses `/dispatcher/task_complete` → #ceo when `chain_step` is set AND `!= "complete"`. nd3b's suppression branch must read whichever form qjl7 ships — `AGENT_CHAIN_STEP` if (a), `CHAIN_STEP` if (b). Worth a cross-check between qjl7 PR + nd3b's existing reader in `dispatcher_task_complete`. (Path of least friction: keep AGENT_* convention end-to-end.)

## Acceptance for qjl7 (suggested)

- A unit test in `tests/dispatcher/` that POSTs to `/dispatcher/spawn` with `spawn_kwargs={"chain_step": "aiden_plan", "task_id": "T-1"}` and asserts the spawned env contains `AGENT_CHAIN_STEP=aiden_plan` + `AGENT_TASK_ID=T-1`. Use the existing tmux backend test harness or stub `session_manager.spawn`.
- If (b) chosen: same test but assert bare `CHAIN_STEP=aiden_plan`.
- Confirm `dispatcher_task_complete` (the nd3b suppression branch) reads the same env-var name the agent sets at exit.

## Files to touch for qjl7 (if any)

- **Option (a) — convention:** **NO changes to `src/dispatcher/main.py`**. One-line acceptance test. Possibly a one-line addition to the nd3b reader in `dispatcher_task_complete` if it currently expects bare `CHAIN_STEP`.
- **Option (b) — bare:** ~6 lines across the two translators (carve-out before the AGENT_* loop) + the same one-line test/reader checks.

---

**End of second opinion.** I'd lead with (a) and propose to Orion. If Orion has already committed to (b) in a draft, the bare-CHAIN_STEP carve-out is a known-small patch and is also fine — the substantive point is that whichever name is chosen, the wiring is a ~6-line change at most, and `_tmux_scrubbed_command` + container env handling already preserve it through to the agent.

comments.author: Aiden
comments.text:
# Agency_OS-zqni — spec: final #ceo result post on chain completion

**Authored by:** scout 2026-05-29 (research-only, per Elliot dispatch). Implementer assignment TBD by Elliot (likely Aiden/Nova).
**Depends on:** Agency_OS-nd3b (notify_complete suppression for intermediate chain steps) — must land first, else this post double-fires.
**Related code on origin/main:** `src/keiracom_system/chain/v1_chain_orchestrator.py`, `src/dispatcher/main.py` (`/dispatcher/task_complete`, `TaskCompleteRequest`), `src/keiracom_system/vault/agent_cold_start.py` (`notify_complete`), `src/keiracom_system/attribution/logger.py` (cost source-of-truth).
**Coordination point:** Atlas's PR (`5b4b7a819` `[ATLAS] feat(chain): v1_chain_orchestrator consumer loop — handoff → advance_step`, **not yet on `origin/main`**) wires the consumer that drives `advance_step`. zqni's post is wired AFTER `advance_step` records completion — so it composes cleanly with Atlas's PR regardless of merge order.

---

## 1. How `advance_step` signals completion (mapped from origin/main)

`CHAIN_STEPS = (aiden_plan, max_challenge, nova_build, orion_spec, atlas_safety, complete)`
Flow: sequential through nova_build, fan-out to `orion_spec + atlas_safety` (PARALLEL_AFTER_STEP), then complete when both parallel partners finish.

`advance_step(chain_id, completed_step, atom_id)` body (lines ~200-296):
1. Loads state, idempotency-guards on `steps_done`.
2. Records completion: appends `completed_step` to `entry["steps_done"]`, sets `entry["atom_ids"][completed_step] = atom_id`, removes from `entry["pending"]`.
3. Branches: `PARALLEL_AFTER_STEP` (fan-out) | `entry["pending"]` non-empty (waiting on parallel partner — no dispatch) | else `_SEQ_NEXT` lookup OR completion branch.
4. **Completion branch (the hook for zqni):**
   ```python
   parallel_steps_done = set(entry["steps_done"])
   all_parallel_covered = all(
       s in parallel_steps_done for group in PARALLEL_AFTER_STEP.values() for s in group
   )
   if all_parallel_covered and any(
       completed_step in group for group in PARALLEL_AFTER_STEP.values()
   ):
       # Last parallel step completed → chain is complete.
       entry["current_step"] = "complete"
       entry["pending"] = []
   ```
   Currently NOTHING else happens here — `entry` is saved via `_save_state(state)` and the function returns `[]` (empty dispatched list). This is the empty hook zqni fills.

## 2. Where the post should live — RECOMMENDED

**Insert in `advance_step` at the completion branch**, right before `_save_state(state)`, by calling a new fail-open helper:
```python
if all_parallel_covered and ...:
    entry["current_step"] = "complete"
    entry["pending"] = []
    _post_chain_complete(entry)  # NEW — fail-open
```

**Why advance_step (not the consumer loop):** advance_step already does I/O (`_publish_envelope` → `asyncio.run` → NATS publish). One more outgoing call is consistent. Keeping the post here means the consumer loop (Atlas's oevr) needs **zero changes** to wire zqni — it just keeps calling `advance_step` and the post fires automatically.

**Helper shape (recommended):**
```python
def _post_chain_complete(entry: dict) -> None:
    """Fire-and-forget final #ceo post on chain completion. Fail-open."""
    try:
        cost_aud = _aggregate_chain_cost(entry["task_id"], entry["atom_ids"])
        msg = _format_chain_complete_message(entry, cost_aud)
        # Loopback POST to dispatcher (only the dispatcher process has SLACK_BOT_TOKEN
        # in env; spawned agents + this library invocation cannot post directly).
        urllib.request.urlopen(
            urllib.request.Request(
                f"{DISPATCHER_URL}/dispatcher/chain_complete",
                data=json.dumps({
                    "chain_id": entry["chain_id"],
                    "task_id": entry["task_id"],
                    "brief": entry["brief"],
                    "atom_ids": entry["atom_ids"],
                    "cost_aud": cost_aud,
                    "message": msg,
                }).encode(),
                headers={"Content-Type": "application/json"},
                method="POST",
            ),
            timeout=10,
        )
    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — chain completion must never block on Slack
        log.warning("v1_chain: chain_complete post failed for chain=%s", entry.get("chain_id"), exc_info=True)
```

## 3. New dispatcher endpoint — RECOMMENDED

Add `/dispatcher/chain_complete` to `src/dispatcher/main.py` (mirror of `/dispatcher/task_complete` shape):
```python
class ChainCompleteRequest(BaseModel):
    chain_id: str
    task_id: str
    brief: str = ""
    atom_ids: dict[str, str] = {}     # step -> atom_id
    cost_aud: float = 0.0
    message: str                        # pre-formatted msg (or compose dispatcher-side)

@app.post("/dispatcher/chain_complete")
async def dispatcher_chain_complete(req: ChainCompleteRequest) -> dict[str, Any]:
    # mirrors task_complete: subprocess slack_relay -c ceo with CALLSIGN=elliot env
    ...
```

**Why a NEW endpoint (not reuse `/task_complete`):** `task_complete` is per-spawn, suppressed by nd3b for intermediate chain steps. The final post is *per-chain*, not per-spawn. Separate endpoint keeps the suppression rule clean (every `task_complete` is suppressed when the task carries a `chain_step != "complete"`; the final post lives in its own lane).

## 4. Message format — RECOMMENDED

```
✅ Chain complete — {task_id}
**Brief:** {brief}
**Steps:** aiden_plan → max_challenge → nova_build → orion_spec + atlas_safety
**Cost:** A${cost_aud:.4f}  (per-step in chain log)
**chain_id:** {chain_id}
```

Plain English (Dave-facing). One emoji icon. Per `feedback_dave_plain_english_strict_2026-05-24`: no jargon. The per-step cost breakdown stays in `#execution` / chain log; #ceo gets the rolled-up number.

## 5. Cost aggregation — DECISION FOR IMPLEMENTER

Two reasonable paths (no Hindsight per-atom cost query function exists today — confirmed by grep):

**Path A (recommended — uses existing source of truth):** query `src/keiracom_system/attribution/logger.py` data store by `task_id` (cost field is the Anthropic-authoritative `total_cost_usd` per attribution/logger.py:34). One DB query at chain_complete time; sum all rows where task_id matches. Convert USD → AUD with the project rate (1 USD = 1.55 AUD per CLAUDE.md). Pro: zero coupling to chain orchestrator; works for non-chain tasks too. Con: requires attribution writes to be in by the time advance_step fires (race risk — mitigated by a small retry/wait or accepting a momentarily-stale figure).

**Path B (extends the chain envelope):** extend `TaskCompleteRequest` with `chain_step: str | None`, `chain_id: str | None`, `cost_aud: float | None`; have the consumer loop accumulate cost into `state[chain_id]["cost_aud"]`; zqni reads from state. Pro: no DB query at completion (state already in hand). Con: requires nd3b + consumer-loop changes to thread cost through; agent_cold_start must source cost from its own run (not free — agents would need to capture and forward).

**Recommend Path A** — fewer moving parts, single source of truth, no schema changes outside the new endpoint.

## 6. Dependency contract with nd3b

nd3b modifies `dispatcher.task_complete` (existing endpoint) to **suppress** the #ceo post when the task is an intermediate chain step. The taxonomy:
- `chain_step` is **NOT** present OR `chain_step == "complete"` → post (existing behaviour, single-task or final-step).
- `chain_step` present AND `chain_step != "complete"` → **suppress** the slack_relay call (still log + return `{"notified": false, "reason": "intermediate_chain_step"}`).

That makes zqni's final post (via the NEW `/dispatcher/chain_complete` endpoint) the **only** #ceo signal for chain runs. Any double-post in the wild is a bug.

**Ordering enforcement:** zqni MUST NOT land before nd3b. Reviewers should HOLD zqni on missing nd3b PR. The bd dep (Agency_OS-zqni blocked-by Agency_OS-nd3b) carries this; reflect in the PR description.

## 7. Test plan (recommended for the implementer)

- **Unit (v1_chain_orchestrator):** completion branch fires `_post_chain_complete` exactly once when the second parallel step completes; idempotency guard prevents double-fire on duplicate `advance_step` calls (same `steps_done` guard already there); `_post_chain_complete` is fail-open (urllib raise → log warn → no exception bubbles).
- **Unit (dispatcher):** `/dispatcher/chain_complete` returns 200 with `{notified: true}` on success; slack_relay non-zero rc → `{notified: false}` (mirror of `task_complete` test).
- **Integration (with nd3b shipped):** drive `advance_step("aiden_plan", ...)` through to `advance_step("atlas_safety", ...)`; assert ONE #ceo post (the final), no intermediate posts.
- **Cost-aggregation:** Path A — mock attribution data store with 5 task_id rows summing to known total; assert `cost_aud` matches (USD→AUD at 1.55).

## 8. Open decisions for the implementer (NOT decided here)

- Path A vs Path B cost aggregation (recommend A; final call: implementer + Aiden).
- Should `/dispatcher/chain_complete` be authn-gated (loopback-only — match existing internal endpoints)? Recommend match existing pattern; no auth, loopback-only.
- Per-step cost breakdown destination (#execution chain log? `chain_complete.log`?). Recommend chain_complete log on dispatcher; #ceo sees the rolled-up A$.
- Whether `_post_chain_complete` should retry on transient HTTP failure (recommend NO — fail-open mirror of `_publish_envelope`; one log warn, never blocks state save).

## 9. Files this lands in (when zqni is implemented)

- `src/keiracom_system/chain/v1_chain_orchestrator.py` — `_post_chain_complete()` helper + 1-line call in the completion branch.
- `src/dispatcher/main.py` — `ChainCompleteRequest` model + `/dispatcher/chain_complete` endpoint.
- `tests/keiracom_system/chain/test_v1_chain_orchestrator.py` — completion → post hook test.
- `tests/dispatcher/test_chain_complete_endpoint.py` (new) — endpoint test.

Estimated diff: ~80-100 lines including tests.

---

**End of spec.** Implementer: Aiden or Nova (per Elliot dispatch routing). Hold zqni PR review until nd3b ships.

description: Staged Part 1 from Agency_OS-rlfh (Atlas 2026-05-29 blast-radius analysis). Current NATS: NO-AUTH. Every client connects bare. Adding per-callsign accounts+ACLs requires coordinated cutover: (1) enumerate all NATS clients; (2) provision per-callsign NATS creds via Vault (nkey or user/pass) — store at secret/keiracom/nats/<callsign>; (3) update every nats.connect() to pass callsign creds; (4) add accounts{}/authorization{} + subject ACLs to nats-server.conf (workers: pub dispatch.<self>+agent.status.<self>+pair.<own_pair>, sub dispatch.<self>; elliot broad); (5) coordinated cutover — deploy creds to ALL clients FIRST, THEN reload nats-server with auth enabled. DEPENDENCY: Vault must be provisioned with prod secrets first (8dvl gap + ceo:v1_chain_architecture). BLAST RADIUS: reloading nats-server without pre-credentialing ALL clients breaks all fleet comms instantly. Cannot bundle with other PRs. Separate milestone post-Vault-provisioning.

title: V1 chain: NATS AtomV1 handoff transport not wired
description: ceo:v1_chain_architecture specifies handoff as 'AtomV1 pointer: exiting agent writes atom to Hindsight fleet_decisions; NATS carries task_id + atom_id; next agent recalls atom at spawn via L2'. There is no NATS publish of (task_id, atom_id) anywhere in the worker flow. The inbox-file/outbox-file system exists for human-relay messages; the Valkey pub/sub handles task queue events; but there is no NATS subject publish for inter-agent AtomV1 pointer passing. Without this, each agent in the chain cannot receive the prior agent's output atom reference. As a minimum viable fallback: the atom_id could be passed via the task row (tasks.last_atom_id) or a Valkey key, but neither exists either.

title: ceo_memory call-site → wrapper migration — pre-requisite for KEI-87 guard enforcement

The KEI-87 write-guard trigger (allowlist: elliot, dave, john) is NOT applied in prod Supabase and must NOT be applied until all ceo:\* write call-sites have been migrated to use the canonical ceo_memory_writer.upsert_ceo_memory_key wrapper (which sets the required SET LOCAL agency_os.callsign session variable). Applying the guard before this migration is complete would be a fleet-wide incident — every raw SQL write to ceo_memory not using the wrapper would be rejected.

Scope: audit all call-sites that write to public.ceo_memory directly (not via the wrapper). Migrate each to use ceo_memory_writer. When all call-sites are on the wrapper, apply the guard trigger to prod Supabase AND include it in the Vultr Postgres [apply.sh](<http://apply.sh>) cutover sequence.

Sequencing gate: this KEI must complete BEFORE the KEI-87 guard is applied in prod, and BEFORE the Vultr Postgres migration (PR #1272 002_triggers.sql) goes live with the guard enabled. The Vultr migration currently includes the trigger — ensure it is gated behind this KEI's completion in [apply.sh](<http://apply.sh>).

Nova verified: as of 2026-05-28 the guard is unapplied in prod (no trigger on ceo_memory, no write_guard function in pg_proc). PR #1274 corrects the migration source file + adds a banner recording this state.

title: Infra auto-scaling — 70% provision / 20% drain policy
description: Implement node auto-scaling policy. Provision new Vultr node when active tenant count hits 70% of node capacity (\~19/27 for Solo vc2-4c-8gb nodes). Drain node when count drops below 20%. State machine: active → draining → decommissioned. Cannot hard-delete a node with in-flight tasks — drain waits for completion. Valkey keys: keiracom:nodes:<id>:state, keiracom:nodes:<id>:tenant_count. Trigger: tenant onboarding checks fill level before assignment. auto_provision_threshold = 0.70 as configurable constant.
