title: Work loop consumer — Postgres trigger → Valkey pub/sub → dispatcher spawn
description:
Viktor+Dave ratified 2026-05-28. The work loop consumer is the commercial architecture — tier-gated concurrency.

Design:
1. Postgres trg_tasks_unblock_dependents fires → publish task_id + callsign + tenant_id to Valkey keiracom:tasks:available
2. Consumer checks per-tenant live spawn counter: Valkey keiracom:tenant:active_spawns:{tenant_id}
3. If counter < tier ceiling (from keiracom_tenants): call POST /dispatcher/spawn → increment counter
4. If counter >= ceiling: push to per-tenant overflow queue keiracom:tenant:overflow:{tenant_id} (not dropped)
5. Agent exits → decrement keiracom:tenant:active_spawns:{tenant_id} → consumer checks overflow queue → spawns next if slot free

Per-tenant spawn counter (FIRST-CLASS design requirement per Viktor):
- Key: keiracom:tenant:active_spawns:{tenant_id}
- Increment: on successful dispatcher spawn
- Decrement: on agent exit (exit_cycle completion callback)
- This counter is also the hook for spend-rate limiting beyond concurrency

Tier concurrency ceiling map:
- Solo (9 AUD/mo): 2 concurrent spawns
- Pro (49 AUD/mo): 6 concurrent spawns
- Team (49 AUD/mo): 20 concurrent spawns
- Distributor: provisioned per wholesale allocation

Upgrade path: customer upgrades tier → ceiling raised in keiracom_tenants → consumer reads new ceiling → next queued task spawns immediately. No ops intervention.

Backpressure: per-tenant overflow queue (requeue, not drop). Valkey distributed lock (prevent duplicate spawns on same task_id). Poison message handling: dead-letter after 3 failed attempts.

~150 lines Python. Single dedicated service with systemd unit.

title: Post-cutover #ceo listener replacement — lightweight capture watcher

title: Hindsight Postgres shm_size too small — mirror writes fail with No space left on device
description:
Enabling INDEXER_HINDSIGHT_MIRROR=on (Elliot dispatch 2026-05-28) surfaced that every mirror write to Hindsight fails. Discovered by ORION while wiring the live dual-write mirror; Aiden ratified Option B (leave flag on, file this P1).

ERROR (verbatim, from direct write probe + indexer logs):
  HTTP 500: could not resize shared memory segment "/PostgreSQL.3543089562" to 533794784 bytes: No space left on device

ROOT CAUSE:
- Hindsight's embedded Postgres needs ~534MB of shared memory for inline (async:false) embedding/extraction on a write.
- The container's /dev/shm defaults to Docker's 64MB (no shm_size set), so the PG shared-memory segment cannot grow to ~534MB -> 'No space left on device'.
- NOT host disk: host / is 35% used (188G free), /dev/shm 0% (7.9G free). This is the CONTAINER shm limit, not the host.
- async:true returns HTTP 200 but only queues an operation_id; deferred processing hits the same PG shm wall, so writes still do not land.

EVIDENCE:
- Mirror path fires post-restart but logs 1x HTTP 500 + 3x timeout, 0 successes (ceo-memory + elliot-memories indexers, 2026-05-28 04:43+).
- Direct probe POST /v1/default/banks/<bank>/memories async:false -> HTTP 500 (shm error); async:true -> HTTP 200 (queue only).

IMPACT: corpus drift continues — readers were cut over to Hindsight (PR #1175) but new writes are NOT landing in Hindsight (only Weaviate). 8 of 10 fleet banks already empty (see docs/retrieval/flashrank_baseline_2026-05-28.md). Mirror is fail-open so no indexer breakage; INDEXER_HINDSIGHT_MIRROR left ON intentionally so it self-heals once this is fixed.

FIX (devops — requires docker access ORION does not have; likely Elliot):
- Add 'shm_size: "1g"' to the 'hindsight' service in keiracom_system/fleet/hindsight/docker-compose.yml (single-service compose; Postgres is embedded in the hindsight container — no separate pg service). Then recreate the container (docker compose up -d --force-recreate hindsight).
- Alternatively tune PG work_mem / max_parallel_workers down if shm bump is not viable.

ACCEPTANCE:
- POST /v1/default/banks/<bank>/memories with async:false returns 2xx, AND
- a mirror write is confirmed present in BOTH Weaviate and Hindsight (recall the written id back from Hindsight).

LABELS: infra, P1, corpus-health

Added shm_size: 1g to keiracom_system/fleet/hindsight/docker-compose.yml; recreated live container (docker compose -p hindsight up -d --force-recreate, volume preserved). Verified: async:false write -> HTTP 200 (was 500), no shm error in docker logs, corpus intact, fleet_decisions grew 2->3. PR #1247. title: Retrieval eval harness — golden query set + precision@K framework for FlashRank vs sidecar A/B
description:
Option A from Orion's FlashRank baseline scrutiny (2026-05-28). Build a REUSABLE labelled evaluation harness so the FlashRank-vs-Nova-sidecar A/B (Wave 3) has a defensible quality metric, not just the behaviour characterisation in docs/retrieval/flashrank_baseline_2026-05-28.md.

Scope:
- Curate a golden query set (start ~20-30 queries) spanning governance, past decisions, code patterns, discoveries.
- Hand-label ground-truth relevance (binary or graded) for the recall pool per query.
- precision@K + recall@K + nDCG@K framework that can run FlashRank vs sidecar and emit a comparison table.
- Persist the labelled set as a versioned artifact (not throwaway) so re-runs are reproducible.

Blockers/notes surfaced by the Option-B characterisation run:
- 8 of 10 fleet Hindsight banks are EMPTY (only fleet_global_governance_patterns=19, fleet_decisions=2). A quality A/B is meaningless until banks are populated — sequence indexing first or scope the golden set to populated banks.
- At production-default 200ms rerank budget, FlashRank bypasses on a 21-doc pool (median 336ms measured). Budget is a tuning knob.

Research-heavy; assign ATLAS or SCOUT. Do NOT block Wave 3 on this.

title: [ENGINEER-TIER] Auto-claim dispatcher pre-check — skip brief if PR already merged
description: Aiden observation 2026-05-26 ~11:42 UTC on PR #1177: auto-claim dispatcher races with admin-merge. Pattern verified across 5 PRs (#1161, #1162, #1171, #1175, #1177) — deliberators receive auto-claim review briefs for PRs already merged. Substance reviews preserved in audit trail but operationally redundant. Correlation: doc-only and small PRs prone (fast merge); large code PRs not. Mechanical fix per Orion proposal: dispatcher pre-checks 'gh pr view N --json state' returns 'OPEN' AND 'mergeable' is 'MERGEABLE' AND CI is not pending-complete before posting auto-claim brief. If MERGED, skip dispatch. Bounded scope ~50-100 LoC. Memory anchor: feedback_auto_claim_race_condition_2026_05_26.md (Aiden authored). Phase 2 ops KEI.

title: [ATLAS] promote NATS PUBLISH success log from DEBUG to INFO in fleet_supervisor.py:134
description: Empirical finding from Phase A6 dual-publish investigation 2026-05-26. fleet_supervisor.py line 134: 'log.debug("[%s] NATS PUBLISH %s → %s", callsign, subject, state)' — emits at DEBUG level, which production logging filters out. Nova's a6_observation_check.sh verifier (PR #1162) greps for 'NATS PUBLISH' lines and never finds them because they don't reach the log file. Promote to log.info (single line change). Symmetric with line 67 (_temporal_signal_state) which already logs at INFO via log.info on success path — that's why the verifier finds Temporal signals but not NATS. After fix, Nova's verifier sees both halves and dual-publish observability is real. ~5 min. After PR opens: bd close Agency_OS-(this), Aiden + Max review.

title: Phase A7 build — cache architecture implementation (constants + Valkey + token budget + LiteLLM + observability + 48h baseline)
description:
Phase A7 build dispatch after PR #1156 design merged 2026-05-26T01:00:35Z. Engineer-tier work; ~500-600 LoC per Max re-estimate (design doc said ~400; honest scope is higher per sub-tasks 1+2 LoC re-estimate).

PREREQUISITE design clarifications (Max 5 concrete-blockers — resolve in build-dispatch design pass BEFORE code):

  1. Sub-task 1: name Valkey Python client lib + version pin (redis-py vs valkey-py) — avoid PR #1154 requirements.txt-gap repeat
  2. Sub-task 1: name TEI sidecar integration shape — how tei_client is constructed in canonical_cache_key (injected? lazy-imported? per PR #1133 pattern?)
  3. Sub-task 2: resolve keiracom_tenant_budgets schema contradiction — PRIMARY KEY (tenant_id) allows one row vs effective_from/until implies time-travel history. Pick (a) point-in-time only OR (b) composite PK (tenant_id, effective_from) + index WHERE effective_until IS NULL
  4. Sub-task 3: verify cache_control_enabled: true is real LiteLLM YAML option OR rename + add caller-side code injecting cache_control markers per Anthropic API spec (would grow sub-task 3 by 30-50 LoC + invalidate 0-code-change claim)
  5. Sub-task 4: name Anthropic prompt cache hit rate metric for Better Stack parallel to Valkey metric (e.g. keiracom.cache.anthropic.input_tokens{type=create|read|standard, tenant_id, model})

QUALITY-OF-LIFE refinements (Max 5 non-blocking but fold-in):

  6. Update LoC estimates: sub-task 1 ~250 not ~150; sub-task 2 ~150 not ~100
  7. LAW II currency: §5 LiteLLM virtual key shows $50 USD daily / $1000 USD monthly — add $AUD translation per Daves $AUD-always rule (= $77.50 AUD daily / $1550 AUD monthly)
  8. §7 48h baseline: name <10% Valkey hit rate as BLOCKING-V1 (re-tune buckets + re-baseline before paying customer) OR explicit TRACK-AND-IMPROVE framing
  9. Schema: add CHECK (tier IN (sandbox,solo,pro,team,enterprise)) constraint to prevent typos
  10. §4 hard rule: all Valkey key construction goes through canonical_cache_key() — no direct redis.set/get outside valkey_client.py module + PR-linter grep pattern test

ATLAS NIT (already noted PR #1156): defense-in-depth tenant_id prefix guard at read/write boundary in valkey_client.py — rejects any key not matching v1:{expected_tenant_id}:* (~5-10 LoC)

SUB-TASK SEQUENCING per design §12:
  Sub-task 1 (~250 LoC): constants.py + valkey_client.py core + tests — independent
  Sub-task 2 (~150 LoC): token_budget_policy.py + Postgres migration + tier seeds + tests — independent
  Sub-task 3 (~20-30 LoC config + maybe ~30-50 LoC code if cache_control verify fails): LiteLLM config for Daves tenant
  Sub-task 4 (~50 LoC): Better Stack instrumentation hook + chart setup — needs Layer 12 alignment
  Sub-task 5 (~50 LoC): 48h baseline observation script + report template — runs post-A9

BUILD WAIT GATE: contract V2 amendment 3 (Agency_OS-ucf8 cache-hit short-circuit re-ordering) ratification — A7 build cleaner if cache_check ordering is canonical V2; if V2 stalls, build under V1 sequence with known token_gate-wasted-on-cache-hit inefficiency + refactor later. Both paths in design §12.

DOWNSTREAM CONSUMER: LLM-call workflow #2 (separate dispatch) imports TenantBudgetPolicy from this bd build + enforces token_gate against it.

description:
Atlas Cat 16 spot-check HOLD anchor (2026-05-25 ts ~1779715000): Dave directive 2026-05-25 decision #3 ratified one-Composio-account-per-customer architecturally; Atlas's HOLD is on empirical verification that this actually works as designed.

Scope:
1. Read Composio docs on per-tenant OAuth segregation patterns (account-per-tenant vs API-key-partitioning)
2. Build small POC: provision 2 separate Composio accounts, validate OAuth flows segregate cleanly, no cross-tenant data leakage
3. Verify pricing/billing model for per-customer accounts (cost projection for V1 launch volume)
4. Document findings + recommend wiring approach for KeiracomTenantExtension integration

Acceptance:
- Empirical proof segregation works as ratified (OR documented why it doesn't, with mitigation)
- Cost projection per customer per month
- Wiring spec for tenant.extension integration

Anchor: ceo:keiracom_architecture_v2 Cat 11 gov.composio_per_customer_segregation (RATIFIED-CEO hard gate); Cat 16 infra.secrets_management Phase 2 sub-deliberation item (d) Composio OAuth per-tenant segregation HARD-GATE-WITHIN-HARD-GATE.

Atlas verdict: feasibility verdict can't be CONCUR until empirical proof. ~1d eng-tier work.

title: V1 CRITERION 4 — Memory + governance on API token spend + cache (cost-aware by design)
description:
Dave-ratified v1 completion criteria #4 (2026-05-25). Every memory read/write and every governance decision tracked against real API cost. Caching built in.

PARTIAL coverage already in flight:
- PR #1137 (metering pipeline) covers per-tenant LLM spend tracking from Hindsight log field — Phase 2 build wave 2 item 3
- Phase 2 build metering pipeline lands the substrate; integration into governance is the gap

Remaining work:
- Memory layer reads cost-tracked at the primitive level (Ingest/Recall/Synthesize each tag cost-attribution)
- Governance decisions cost-tracked (deliberator concur calls, audit-dispatch reviews, canonical-key queries — all token-costed)
- Cache-aware patterns: the Claude prompt cache has a 5-minute TTL; agents should be designed to hit the cache deliberately (e.g. consistent system prompt prefix, batched calls within window) rather than incidentally
- Cost-attribution dashboard showing per-tenant + per-callsign + per-agent-role spend over time

Sub-pieces (to scope):
- Memory primitive cost-attribution wrapper
- Governance decision cost-attribution wrapper
- Cache-pattern enforcement (linter? runtime-warn on cache-bust events?)
- Cost dashboard (consumes metering pipeline output)

Priority: P0 — blocks v1 shipping per Dave criteria
Sequencing: in flight on metering substrate; rest can dispatch as Phase 2 build pieces land

Dave-ratified v1 completion criteria #3 (2026-05-25). Each agent role properly defined — right identity, right capabilities, right behaviour profile — so the system gets best performance out of each agent.

Current state: callsigns (Elliot/Aiden/Max/Atlas/Orion/Scout/Nova/Viktor) evolved organically. Not purpose-built for the ephemeral runtime.

Dave-gated mode choice (pending): does Dave design the personas himself (vision call), or does the team propose ideal candidates for his review?

Sub-pieces (to scope after mode-choice):
- Persona role taxonomy aligned with ephemeral system (deliberator / orchestrator / worker / specialist)
- IDENTITY templates per role with capability + behaviour profiles
- Spawn-time persona-loading mechanism
- Migration path from current callsigns to new persona set (or keep callsigns + add personas; depends on Dave choice)

Priority: P0 — blocks v1 shipping per Dave criteria
Sequencing: BLOCKED on Dave persona-design-mode decision; then 1-2 weeks design work + iterative refinement
notes:
Dave additions 2026-05-25 ts ~1779676750:
- Chat agent identity needs separate research track (customer-facing chat persona is its own design question, distinct from internal agent roles)
- Each agent identity should OPTIMISE its role — not generic
- Deliberators (Elliot/Aiden/Max) need COMPLIMENTARY roles to each other — current overlap is too high

Still blocked on Dave persona-design-mode choice: (a) Dave designs personas himself (vision call) OR (b) team proposes ideal candidates for Dave review.

title: V1 CRITERION 2 — Whole system modelled, built, wired (end-to-end coherence)
description:
Dave-ratified v1 completion criteria #2 (2026-05-25). Not pieces in isolation — the full system designed as a coherent model and wired end-to-end.

What this means concretely:
- An architectural model document showing how memory + agents + UI/UX + governance fit together as ONE system, not adjacent components
- Each piece wired such that customer journey works end-to-end without manual stitching
- UI/UX surfaces from earlier ratifications (chat + workforce + dashboard) actually present and consuming the substrate
- Governance layer (orchestrator-merge pattern, audit-dispatch checklist, canonical-key discipline) embedded into the runtime, not just docs

Sub-pieces (to be scoped):
- System architecture diagram with all surfaces named and wired
- Chat surface implementation (user-facing conversation interface)
- Workforce surface implementation (ephemeral agents executing tenant tasks)
- Dashboard surface implementation (admin/observability)
- Governance-as-runtime (rules enforced at execution, not just documented)
- End-to-end test scenarios that exercise the whole system

Priority: P0 — blocks v1 shipping per Dave criteria
Sequencing: substrate work (Phase 2 build) lands first; system-coherence work composes on top

title: V1 CRITERION 1 — Ephemeral agent system replaces tmux (architectural backbone)
description:
Dave-ratified v1 completion criteria #1 (2026-05-25). Migrate from persistent tmux-pane runtime to ephemeral spawn-and-die agents with memory carrying state between invocations.

Architectural shift: agents currently live in long-running tmux panes (elliot, aiden, atlas, orion, scout, max, nova). The MAL V1 substantive_lock framing assumes ephemeral agents — short-lived, stateless, with persistent memory layer.

Sub-pieces (to be scoped + dispatched):
- Agent spawn mechanism (how does an ephemeral agent start with the right context loaded from memory?)
- Agent termination + state-flush (how does context flush to memory at the boundary?)
- Inter-agent dispatch in ephemeral world (currently NATS → tmux inbox; new model: NATS → spawn-receiver?)
- Replacement for tmux capture-pane liveness checking
- Cache-aware spawn pattern (5-min Claude prompt cache window should be respected, not invalidated unnecessarily)

Priority: P0 — blocks v1 shipping per Dave criteria
Sequencing: parallel with criteria 2-4; foundational refactor that affects everything else

This is a major work bucket. Scoping phase first; then atomic dispatch units.

title: Viktor CARA citation reconciliation — what is CARA in Hindsight?
description:
BLOCKING question for Phase 2 build, surfaced by Atlas PR #1129 (Phase 2.1 spike item vi). The MAL V1 substantive_lock cites Hindsight TEMPR + CARA as the engine layer the V1 primitives wrap. TEMPR is solidly documented in public Hindsight developer docs. CARA is NOT — Atlas's empirical search of vectorize-io/hindsight found no documentation. Three possibilities:

1. CARA is an internal-only Hindsight module (not in public docs) — needs confirmation it actually exists in the deployed instance.
2. CARA is a synthesis term Viktor used for the reflect-disposition path that already exists under another name in Hindsight (Reflect plus belief-update plus disposition traits per reflect.mdx). Naming reconciliation.
3. CARA is a planned subsystem not yet shipped — would be a Phase 2 build risk.

Phase 2 build cannot commit to the Reflect / belief-update layer until this resolves. P0 blocking.

Owner: Viktor (the roadmap-architect deliberator). Filing for Aiden to relay on next deliberation cycle since Viktor doesn't have an inbox watcher today.

Source PR: https://github.com/Keiracom/Agency_OS/pull/1129 (merged 2026-05-24T20:04Z)

title: Revive fleet-supervisor service — Max stalled 1.5hr on pane question undetected
description:
Anchored 2026-05-24 wake-50 incident. Max was paused on a (a)/(b) question for ~1.5hr during fi4u authoring; the supervise-wake loop did not detect it because fleet-supervisor.service has been inactive since 2026-05-20.

Per feedback_orchestrator_stall_visibility (Dave 2026-05-20): 'Don't assume dispatched agents are progressing — they silently stall on rate-limits/errors. Stagger multi-agent dispatches; verify liveness, don't blind-wait.'

The supervisor-wake loop catches PRs + inbox events but does NOT scan worker tmux panes for blocking prompts. Orion's PR #1112 fleet-supervisor stall detector (just merged today) was supposed to address this — but the systemd service is inactive.

Tasks:
1. Diagnose why fleet-supervisor.service is inactive (was Type=oneshot timer-driven; timer may not be enabled).
2. Re-enable + verify periodic firing.
3. Extend supervise routine to scan worker pane prompts via tmux capture-pane for '❯' prompt with unanswered question — surface as a wake event to Elliot.
4. Test against synthetic offender (worker pane with stuck prompt).

P1 — this is a real orchestration gap revealed by an actual incident today.

title: NATS publish HMAC signing — must close before multi-tenant
description:
Aiden architecture-lens recommendation point 6 from 2026-05-24 bootstrap-authority deliberation (ratified by Dave 2026-05-24).

Threat surface: current direct-push pattern means Elliot can be tricked into pushing whatever passes NATS concur. A hostile peer publishing a fake `[REVIEW:approve:<callsign>]` message to NATS is the threat — any callsign with NATS publish capability can fabricate a concur from another callsign.

Mitigation: require NATS publishes to be signed with per-callsign HMAC. Drain daemon Agency_OS-q0jr has scoping for this; signing is a separate concern.

Tasks:
1. Per-callsign HMAC secret generation + storage (~/.config/agency-os/.callsign_secrets or equivalent secure store).
2. NATS publish wrapper signs envelope payload with per-callsign HMAC before publish.
3. Subscriber verification: reject envelopes with invalid HMAC; emit governance debt alert on signature failures.
4. Integration with the orchestrator-merge-after-NATS-concur pattern: merge gate verifies HMAC signatures on the cited concur messages before merging.
5. Per-callsign secret rotation policy + emergency-rotation procedure.

Sequencing: bootstrap-acceptable to defer. Must close BEFORE multi-tenant launch (any tenant whose data is governed by these concurs needs the signing layer).

title: Provision per-callsign GitHub bot accounts (deliberator-aiden-bot / deliberator-max-bot / deliberator-elliot-bot)
description:
Aiden architecture-lens recommendation point 5(c) from 2026-05-24 bootstrap-authority deliberation (ratified by Dave 2026-05-24).

Current state: all callsigns authenticate to GitHub as the shared 'Keiracom' account. `gh pr review --approve` fails with 'Can not approve your own pull request' because GitHub sees author + approver as same identity. Branch protection counts identities, not callsigns — two callsigns approving via the same token = ONE review for branch protection.

Goal: provision separate GitHub bot accounts for each deliberator with their own OAuth tokens, so `gh pr review --approve` becomes the branch-protection-counted approval signal natively.

Tasks:
1. Provision three GitHub bot accounts (deliberator-aiden-bot / deliberator-max-bot / deliberator-elliot-bot) with separate emails + tokens.
2. Configure per-callsign `GH_TOKEN` env var override in each callsign's environment (CALLSIGN-aware token loader).
3. Update PR review tooling: deliberators use their own bot token for `gh pr review --approve`; merge action still uses Elliot admin token (orchestrator-merge pattern).
4. Re-enable native PR review-approval as the primary concur signal; NATS substance review still required + cited in approval body.
5. Branch protection: once per-callsign accounts exist, can re-evaluate flipping `enforce_admins=true` (currently false to preserve orchestrator-merge bootstrap pattern).

Sequencing: POST first-paying-tenant (per Aiden recommendation). Bootstrap authority covers the current window via orchestrator-merge-after-NATS-concur pattern.

title: ceo_memory.context column NOT NULL constraint + backfill migration
description: Discovered by Viktor 2026-05-24 in the write-gate inventory (item 7 in the soft-gated set). Aiden + Max also named this in MAL V1 CONCUR as G1. Agents can currently write ceo_memory entries without a context tag, making them unqueryable by the planned MAL V1 architecture (which routes recall by context). Fix sequence: (1) backfill migration — classify all existing ceo_memory rows into 'fleet' / 'product' / 'archive' / 'both' values; (2) add CHECK constraint on the value enum; (3) add NOT NULL constraint to the context column. Test path - positive: INSERT with valid context value lands; negative: INSERT without context expects SQL constraint violation; INSERT with invalid context value expects CHECK constraint violation. Engineer-tier work, ~2-3 hours (backfill is the longest). Closes write-gate inventory item 7 + MAL V1 G1. Sequenced before Phase 2.0 namespace work.

title: Weaviate ingest gate — reject ingest calls without explicit target_collection arg
description: Discovered by Viktor 2026-05-24 in the write-gate inventory (item 4 in the ungated high-priority set). Any agent can currently ingest to any Weaviate collection without a collection-scoping gate. Not critical until the Keiracom V1.0 product memory collection is created — at that point, namespace pollution would be silent (wrong answer on recall, no error fires). Fix: at the ingest helper layer, reject calls that do not explicitly declare target_collection. Test: positive path - ingest with explicit target_collection lands; negative path - ingest without target_collection raises with a clear error message. Engineer-tier work, decoupled, ~1-2 hours. Critical-path BEFORE the product collection is created in Phase 2.0; nice-to-have until then but worth shipping now to lock the discipline.

title: Phase 2.2 — product code migration via clean PRs subject to dynamic-exclusion manifest
description: Per ratified separation directive 2026-05-24. Final phase: migrate V1.0 product code (Memory Abstraction Layer + Hindsight wrappers + Go sidecar + MCP server + tenant onboarding + install script + CLI) from current monorepo to fresh product repo. Each migration is a clean PR (not subtree, not history split — additive write). Subject to migration manifest with dynamic-exclusion: original-repo CI fails any PR touching a migrated path; product-repo CI fails any PR adding paths not on manifest; PATHS WITH ACTIVE PR OR ACTIVE KEI WORK EXCLUDED FROM MANIFEST until in-flight work lands (Elliot extension). CI gates: product repo imports nothing from fleet or archive (Aiden G4 — grep returns zero). MCP bridge refuses wrong-project-id Supabase calls; per-repo .env enforced at bridge layer (Aiden G8). Loop-nudge classifier already extended to N-repo (Aiden G7) before this phase. Negative-path tests on namespace boundaries pass first (Max C2). Skills directory ownership manifest drafted (Max R1). Migration runner scope spans 3 repos + shared Supabase (Elliot expansion to MAL Gate F). BLOCKED on Phase 1.2.5 + Phase 2.0 + Phase 2.1 all landing. Estimated 1-2 weeks elapsed engineering. Engineer-tier with deliberator review per PR.

title: Phase 2.1 — Hindsight verification spike (6 items, 1-2 days, gates Phase 2.2 build start)
description: Per ratified MAL V1 + ratified separation directive 2026-05-24. Verification spike on Vectorize.io Hindsight self-hosted as Memory Abstraction Layer engine. Six items, all must clear with verbatim findings posted to #ceo per LAW XIV before Phase 2.2 (product code migration) fires: (1) repo health — confirm Hindsight repo at vectorize-io/hindsight is MIT-licensed, actively maintained, contributor base; (2) benchmark validity — does LongMemEval 91.4% claim reflect agentic software-engineering memory or only conversational; (3) multi-tenancy under Solo/Pro/Scale tier model via memory banks (Viktor's named 1-day spike on quotas + audit logging); (4) fastembed pluggability as embedding provider, not locked to specific model; (5) BYOK LLM routing through customer's key for Reflect + belief-update operations; (6) domain mapping (Decision→World, Artifact→Experience, AntiPattern→Opinion, TaskContext→Observation) preserves Trace + audit semantics with real examples. Outcome: pass → adopt Hindsight + V1 primitives become thin wrappers; fail on item (iii) or (v) → architecture inverts (build primitives ourselves OR sovereignty compromise) and Phase 2.2 plan revisits. No build dollars committed until spike-pass posted. Engineer-tier with deliberator review (Aiden + Max gate the spike outcome).

title: Phase 2.0 — repo creation: carve fleet repo, create product repo, confirm archive state, namespace memory stores
description: Per ratified Agency OS / Keiracom separation directive 2026-05-24. Repo topology: internal fleet repo (working name keiracom-fleet, carved from current monorepo), product repo (fresh, working name TBD-rename-ready), archive repo (existing URL preserved, marked inactive). Includes: (a) GitHub: create keiracom-fleet repo + new product repo (working name TBD); add bot access; configure CI matrices (stripped product-only with 3 non-negotiable tests per Item 2); (b) Supabase: add context column to ceo_memory with DB trigger enforcement (values fleet/product/archive/both, rejects NULL or unknown); (c) Weaviate: create new product collection (target name configurable per rename-ready); update backup scripts to cover both collections (Max G5); (d) Drive Manual: section enum at skill level (values FLEET/PRODUCT/ARCHIVE, skill rejects unknown); (e) Vultr: logical /opt/<fleet>/ vs /opt/<product>/ directory split with separate .env files. All additive — original monorepo untouched until Phase 2.2 migration. BLOCKED on Phase 1.2.5 (Agency_OS-yy8f architecture doc rewrite + Agency_OS-eoxg product-framing files + Agency_OS-fwdb identity refresh + Phase 1.2.5 bundle 7 artefacts) all landing first.

title: Phase 1.2.5 — pre-migration artefact bundle (7 artefacts gate first product-migration PR)

title: Next.js frontend build regression — route conflict on / (root + (marketing) page collision)
description:
Every Vercel frontend deploy since 2026-05-19 22:19 UTC fails identically:

errorCode: ENOENT
errorMessage: ENOENT: no such file or directory, lstat '/vercel/path0/frontend/.next/server/app/(marketing)/page_client-reference-manifest.js'
errorStep: buildStep

Empirical scope: 50+ consecutive failed deploys across ALL branches (ATLAS/ORION/SCOUT/ELLIOT/MAX) AND production main. Last green deploy was sha 63cda3650e at 2026-05-19 22:21 UTC. PRs cannot achieve green Vercel — main itself is failing.

Likely root cause: frontend/app/page.tsx (39KB, 2026-05-17, original root landing) AND frontend/app/(marketing)/page.tsx (6KB, 2026-05-20 00:07) both resolve to / — Next.js route group conflict. (marketing) was added in MAX's PR #965 (KEI-119 keiracom.com landing page scaffold) but the original root page was never removed.

Verification command: gh api repos/keiracom/Agency_OS/commits/main/status (Vercel – frontend: failure on main).

Blocks: cannot achieve clean CI on any open PR. Dave-gated: which root page is canonical (legacy app/page.tsx or new (marketing)/page.tsx).

Surfaced from PR #1112 investigation (orion agent stall detector — Python-only, inherited this on day-old failing main).
notes: next_work_prompter loop-fired again on PR #1112 at 2026-05-23 13:31 UTC despite first outbox to Aiden + this bd issue being filed. Concrete second-cycle demonstration of Agency_OS-sg29 in its infra-blocked subcase: PR CI red, but red is repo-wide pre-existing (not author-caused, not author-fixable). Prompter should detect: author's latest commit (d1fd3e2d5) is newer than the FIRST Vercel failure on this PR's branch AND the same Vercel failure exists on origin/main HEAD. When both hold → INFRA-blocked → suppress author nudge.

title: Agent stall-detector: fast error-aware detection of rate-limit/API-error stalls
[ATLAS] cognee_auto_ingest --once never triggers cognify + cognify unbatched OOM-throttles Cognee
description:
Two linked defects found during the 2026-05-20 memory-content refresh.

DEFECT 1 — no cognify trigger. scripts/cognee_auto_ingest.py ingest() only POSTs /api/v1/add. It never POSTs /api/v1/cognify. /add stores raw dataset content; only /cognify rebuilds the knowledge graph that GRAPH_COMPLETION recall reads. Result: 'cognee_auto_ingest.py --once' can NEVER clear recall drift — it adds data the graph never sees. Both the --once path and the --watch path are affected.

DEFECT 2 — unbatched cognify OOM-throttles the server. Manually triggering /api/v1/cognify on the full 52-file governance dataset pushed cognee.service cgroup memory to MemoryCurrent=2703MiB vs MemoryHigh=2700MiB (100.1%). Kernel throttle engaged ~12:37:39Z; Cognee /health + /search unresponsive 45+ min; process alive but paralysed (1.6% CPU, log frozen). MemoryMax=3072MiB not hit so no OOM-kill, but MemoryHigh throttle is functionally equivalent to a wedge.

FIX DIRECTION:
1. cognee_auto_ingest.py: after /add, trigger /api/v1/cognify so the graph actually rebuilds.
2. Cognify in memory-safe batches (~5-10 files per call, settle/await between) so peak cgroup memory stays under MemoryHigh=2700M. Do NOT cognify a 50+ file dataset in one call.
3. Consider raising MemoryHigh/MemoryMax for cognee.service if batching alone is insufficient — coordinate with whoever owns the 3G cap (Max's cgroup acceptance test).

Anchored: 2026-05-20 memory-content-refresh directive. Discovery logged separately.
notes: Code-fix PR #1114 opened 2026-05-24. End-to-end blocked on pre-existing cognee.service Kuzu WAL corruption (2026-05-20 incident, not recovered). Code-level validation clean: ruff, format, OpenAPI contract, negative-path TimeoutError. Awaiting dual approval (elliot+aiden) per LAW; awaiting Dave/elliot authorisation on WAL recovery (Option A: mv corrupt WAL + restart, lose ~7d graph deltas — reconstructable).

title: [ATLAS] tool-call-log-indexer frozen — DB connect/query hang, no timeout
description: ROOT CAUSE: scripts/orchestrator/tool_call_log_indexer.py run() loop does psycopg.connect(_dsn(), prepare_threshold=None) with NO connect_timeout/statement_timeout and no try/except. When the Supabase pooler/network stalled, the indexer hung indefinitely in do_poll on the DB socket (verified /proc/338095/wchan=do_poll). Log frozen cleanly since 2026-05-17 12:40 (last line a successful batch, no traceback), service active(running) 3 days. FIX: add connect_timeout + statement_timeout so a stall fails fast and systemd Restart recovers. Audit 2026-05-20.

title: [ATLAS] indexing-queue-worker frozen — missing prepare_threshold=None + no DB timeout
description: ROOT CAUSE: scripts/indexing_queue_worker.py run() opens psycopg.connect(_dsn()) WITHOUT prepare_threshold=None. Against Supabase transaction-mode pooler, psycopg3 auto-prepares after 5 executes -> DuplicatePreparedStatement (verbatim traceback in log 2026-05-17 12:52). Worker then hung in do_poll on the DB socket (no connect/statement timeout) - service shows active(running) PID 1093662 for 3 days, log frozen. FIX: add prepare_threshold=None + connect_timeout + statement_timeout. Audit 2026-05-20.

title: [ATLAS] KEI-239 — `bd complete` becomes the canonical completion gesture: auto-writes verification + propagates to Linear mirror
description:
## Policy (Dave directive 2026-05-19)

bd is the ideal agent interface. `bd complete <id>` should be the one-shot agents use to mark work done — no separate calls to write `task_verifications` rows, no manual Postgres mutation.

## Fix

In `scripts/tasks_cli.py` `cmd_complete`:

* Accept (existing) `--evidence` flag from KEI-89 Gate 2
* If evidence is provided: write the `task_verifications` row from evidence (existing path)
* If evidence is NOT provided AND `--auto-verify` flag passed: write a synthetic verification row attributed to the calling callsign with behavioral_test='bd complete auto-verify' + test_output='<verbatim PR merge commit SHA + Linear KEI URL>'
* Then proceed with the existing UPDATE `status='done'` flow
* Trigger passes because verification exists; sync_event fires from Postgres trigger; orchestrator mirrors to Linear

## Acceptance

- [ ] `bd complete <id> --auto-verify` writes synthetic verification + sets done
- [ ] `bd complete <id>` (no flags, no evidence) fails with hint: "pass --evidence or --auto-verify"
- [ ] Tests: auto-verify path, evidence path, no-flag failure
- [ ] Depends on KEI-235 (orchestrator-side verification fix already in place)
- [ ] Verbatim live test: `bd complete <test-KEI> --auto-verify` → Postgres status=done → orchestrator → Linear=Done within 30s
- [ ] Lint clean

## Source

Dave directive 2026-05-19: "bd is the ideal."

title: [ATLAS] KEI-238 — Linear webhook ignores echo from orchestrator's own writes
description:
## Problem

Defense-in-depth against the loop pattern that caused the 59-KEI downgrade incident: even with KEI-236 locking the orchestrator to one-way pushes, the Linear webhook currently accepts ALL state changes — including the ones echoed back from the orchestrator's own `issueUpdate` calls. If we ever re-introduce a non-postgres write path, this would re-create the loop.

## Fix

In `src/api/webhooks/linear.py`:

* Parse `data.actor.id` from the Linear webhook payload
* Compare to `LINEAR_VIEWER_ID` env var (the API key's user id)
* If `actor.id == viewer.id` AND `action='update'` AND we're handling a state-change: SKIP (don't write to Postgres, don't emit sync_event)
* Still log + heartbeat as `ignored_self_echo` so the monitor can see the protection working

## Acceptance

- [ ] `_normalise_event` returns None for `action='update'` with `actor.id == LINEAR_VIEWER_ID`
- [ ] LINEAR_VIEWER_ID env var documented + set via `scripts/sync_linear_viewer_env.py` helper (one-line GraphQL `{ viewer { id } }`)
- [ ] Tests: self-echo skipped; other-actor preserved
- [ ] Heartbeat status='ignored_self_echo' on the skip path
- [ ] Live --apply verify: orchestrator-triggered Linear updates don't loop back to Postgres
- [ ] Lint clean

## Source

Dave directive 2026-05-19 — defense in depth.

title: [ATLAS] KEI-237 — reconciler stops emitting Linear→Postgres field_drift events
description:
## Policy (Dave directive 2026-05-19)

Linear is a read-only mirror. The reconciler's `field_drift` bucket — added in KEI-233 — propagates Linear's canonical state DOWN to Postgres. Under the new policy this direction is wrong: Postgres is canonical, Linear is downstream.

## Fix

In `reconcile_three_stores.detect_drift`:

* Keep the `field_drift` bucket detection (still useful for visibility)
* In `_emit_events`, change the emission policy:
  * field_drift entries now emit `origin=postgres, event_type=update` with the Postgres-side payload
  * The orchestrator then dispatches to bd + linear (Linear gets the mirror update)
  * Effect: Postgres's truth flows OUT to Linear, never the reverse

## Acceptance

- [ ] `_emit_events` field_drift branch reads pg_row (not linear_iss) for canonical payload
- [ ] Origin tag flipped to `postgres`
- [ ] Tests: assert emitted origin = postgres
- [ ] Live --apply verify: any remaining field_drift cases push Postgres-state to Linear (not vice versa)
- [ ] Depends on KEI-236 (Linear-as-mirror policy must be locked first)
- [ ] Lint clean

## Source

Dave directive 2026-05-19 + KEI-236 policy lock.

title: [ATLAS] K4-followup — reconciler detects field-level drift, not just missing-store drift
description:
## Problem

Cross-store audit 2026-05-19 revealed **45 stale state mismatches + 4 NULL-status rows** between Linear and Postgres. Linear says `completed`, Postgres still says `available` (etc.). The K4 reconciler (`scripts/reconcile_three_stores.py`) does NOT catch this drift — it only classifies issues into 4 buckets (`in_all_three` / `missing_postgres` / `missing_bd` / `missing_linear`) and only emits sync_events for the three "missing" buckets. KEIs present in all three stores but with diverging field values get no event raised.

## Sample of the drift

```
KEI-1    Linear=canceled   pg=available     "Get familiar with Linear"
KEI-10   Linear=completed  pg=available     "System Health Monitoring Layer"
KEI-209  Linear=completed  pg=available     "[WORKER] Dispatcher — auth_minter"
KEI-218  Linear=started    pg=available     "fleet-supervisor crash-loop fix"
... (49 total)
```

## Fix

Extend `detect_drift` to add a 5th bucket — `field_drift` — for KEIs present in all 3 stores but with diverging `status` (and optionally `title` / `priority`). Treat Linear as canonical (it's the human board). For each row in the field_drift bucket, `_emit_events` raises `sync_events(origin='linear', event_type='update')` with the canonical payload. K3 sync_orchestrator drains and dispatches the corrections to Postgres + bd.

## Acceptance

- [ ] `detect_drift` adds `field_drift` bucket; existing 4 buckets unchanged
- [ ] `_emit_events` emits `update` events from `origin=linear` for each field-drift row
- [ ] Unit tests for `field_drift` classification (status differs, title differs, all-match, ignored statuses)
- [ ] Live `--apply` run on current production state → ≥49 update events emitted → orchestrator drains them → audit re-runs clean
- [ ] `ruff check && ruff format --check` clean
- [ ] KEI-108 gate (no new service files)
- [ ] Dual-approve / Dave-authorised single-merge

## Source

Atlas cross-store audit 2026-05-19. Dave directive: "ship pr".

title: [ATLAS] External Knowledge Ingester — Weaviate ExternalKnowledge + 14-source weekly
description: Generic external-knowledge ingester. Crawl 14 docs sources + GitHub repos, rule-chunk, upsert to new Weaviate ExternalKnowledge collection via text2vec-openai. Weekly systemd timer. title: [ATLAS] External Knowledge Ingester — Weaviate ExternalKnowledge collection + 14-source weekly refresh
description:
## Why

Agents reimplement third-party patterns from scratch every session by reading external docs inline. Pre-loading external documentation into Weaviate gives "one brain, all arms" — any agent queries `ExternalKnowledge` and gets answers instantly.

## Build

`scripts/orchestrator/external_knowledge_ingester.py` — generic ingester accepting URLs or GitHub repos. Rule-based chunker (no LLM calls). Weaviate POST via the existing `indexer_base.py` helpers (`ensure_class`, `post_object`, `aggregate_count`, `deterministic_uuid`).

## Weaviate schema — `ExternalKnowledge`

* `url` TEXT
* `source_name` TEXT
* `section_title` TEXT
* `content` TEXT (vectorized via text2vec-openai)
* `chunk_index` INT
* `ingested_at` DATE

Class-level vectorizer: `text2vec-openai` (matches existing Codebase/Decisions/Keis pattern). Deterministic UUID via `deterministic_uuid("external_knowledge", f"{url}#{chunk_index}")`.

## First-batch sources (14)

| Domain | Type | Target |
| -- | -- | -- |
| NATS | docs | [https://docs.nats.io](<https://docs.nats.io>) |
| NATS | repo | [https://github.com/nats-io/nats.py](<https://github.com/nats-io/nats.py>) |
| NATS | repo | [https://github.com/nats-io/nats-architecture-and-design](<https://github.com/nats-io/nats-architecture-and-design>) |
| Valkey | docs | [https://valkey.io/docs](<https://valkey.io/docs>) |
| Valkey | repo | [https://github.com/valkey-io/valkey-py](<https://github.com/valkey-io/valkey-py>) |
| Weaviate | docs | [https://weaviate.io/developers/weaviate](<https://weaviate.io/developers/weaviate>) |
| Weaviate | repo | [https://github.com/weaviate/weaviate-python-client](<https://github.com/weaviate/weaviate-python-client>) |
| Cognee | docs | [https://docs.cognee.ai](<https://docs.cognee.ai>) |
| Cognee | repo | [https://github.com/topoteretes/cognee](<https://github.com/topoteretes/cognee>) |
| LiteLLM | docs | [https://docs.litellm.ai](<https://docs.litellm.ai>) |
| LiteLLM | repo | [https://github.com/BerriAI/litellm](<https://github.com/BerriAI/litellm>) |
| Supabase | docs | [https://supabase.com/docs/guides/auth](<https://supabase.com/docs/guides/auth>) |
| Supabase | docs | [https://supabase.com/docs/guides/database/postgres/row-level-security](<https://supabase.com/docs/guides/database/postgres/row-level-security>) |
| Supabase | docs | [https://supabase.com/docs/reference/python/introduction](<https://supabase.com/docs/reference/python/introduction>) |
| FastAPI | docs | [https://fastapi.tiangolo.com](<https://fastapi.tiangolo.com>) |
| asyncpg | docs | [https://magicstack.github.io/asyncpg/current](<https://magicstack.github.io/asyncpg/current>) |
| psycopg3 | docs | [https://www.psycopg.org/psycopg3/docs](<https://www.psycopg.org/psycopg3/docs>) |
| Docker | docs | [https://docs.docker.com/engine/install/ubuntu](<https://docs.docker.com/engine/install/ubuntu>) |
| Docker | docs | [https://docs.docker.com/config/containers/systemd](<https://docs.docker.com/config/containers/systemd>) |
| systemd | docs | [https://systemd.io](<https://systemd.io>) |
| systemd | docs | [https://www.freedesktop.org/software/systemd/man/systemd.service.html](<https://www.freedesktop.org/software/systemd/man/systemd.service.html>) |
| Linear API | docs | [https://developers.linear.app/docs](<https://developers.linear.app/docs>) |
| Better Stack | docs | [https://betterstack.com/docs/logs/getting-started](<https://betterstack.com/docs/logs/getting-started>) |
| Better Stack | docs | [https://betterstack.com/docs/uptime/webhooks](<https://betterstack.com/docs/uptime/webhooks>) |
| Claude Code | docs | [https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code/overview](<https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code/overview>) |

## Constraints

* No Claude/LLM calls during ingestion — rule-based chunker only (H2/H3 boundary + 2000-char size cap)
* Failed URLs **logged, not silently skipped** — verbatim per-source failure report
* Idempotent — same URL + chunk_index → same Weaviate UUID
* Same chunking + embedding + upsert pattern as existing indexers

## Deliverables

* `scripts/orchestrator/external_knowledge_ingester.py`
* `infra/systemd/external-knowledge-ingester.service` + `.timer` (weekly)
* `scripts/install_external_knowledge_ingester.sh` (KEI-108 compliance)
* `tests/scripts/test_external_knowledge_ingester.py`
* First-run backfill — verbatim chunk-count per source, all > 0

## Acceptance

- [ ] Collection `ExternalKnowledge` exists in Weaviate (verbatim create output)
- [ ] Backfill chunk count > 0 for each of the 14 sources (verbatim)
- [ ] Failed-URL log present + non-fatal
- [ ] Unit tests for chunker + URL classifier pass (verbatim pytest)
- [ ] `ruff check && ruff format --check` clean (verbatim)
- [ ] KEI-108 install-script gate passes
- [ ] Single PR open with dual-approval gate

## Governance

* LAW XIV: verbatim output throughout
* Do not mark complete until backfill chunk_count > 0 for all sources
* Dual approve before merge (Elliot + Aiden)

## Source

Dave directive 2026-05-19 — "External Knowledge Ingester. One brain, all arms."

title: [ATLAS] K3 — sync_orchestrator drain worker (origin-tagged loop-safe fan-out)
description:
## Problem

`completion-sync-worker.py` only handles the completion sink (`tasks.status='done'|'cancelled'`) and only fans out Postgres → Linear/ceo_memory/drive_manual. It cannot consume `sync_events` from `origin='bd'` or `origin='linear'` and cannot prevent loops (no origin tag honoured).

## Spec — Step 3 of 4

Replace `scripts/orchestrator/completion_sync_worker.py` with `scripts/orchestrator/sync_orchestrator.py`:

```python
def process_event(event):
    origin = event["origin"]
    targets = {"bd", "postgres", "linear"} - {origin}  # never echo to origin (loop prevention)
    for target in targets:
        _dispatch[target](event)  # _dispatch_bd / _dispatch_postgres / _dispatch_linear
```

Behaviour:

1. `SELECT FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED FROM public.sync_events WHERE processed=false AND attempts < 3` — batch 20
2. For each event, dispatch to the **OTHER two** stores (origin-tag determines skip)
3. Loop prevention is structural: no echo back to origin (the `sync_events` row created by the destination write must NOT trigger a second fan-out — destinations include an `origin_event_id` reference in their write to break the cycle)
4. Per-target writer:
   * `_dispatch_bd` — invoke `bd update/create/close` via subprocess
   * `_dispatch_postgres` — direct `INSERT … ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE` to `public.tasks`
   * `_dispatch_linear` — Linear GraphQL `issueUpdate` mutation (reuse existing `_sink_linear` from completion_sync_worker)
5. Idempotency: dedupe on `(task_id, payload_hash)` — duplicate events skipped
6. Backoff: existing ladder `(1s, 5s, 25s)` retained; abandons after 3 attempts with `error_message`
7. Heartbeat tick on each batch (KEI-91 Gate 4 compliance)

## Acceptance

- [ ] `sync_orchestrator.py` lands; `completion_sync_worker.py` deleted or aliased to backwards-compat shim
- [ ] systemd unit `infra/systemd/sync-orchestrator.service` (replaces completion-sync-worker.service)
- [ ] Origin-tag loop prevention test: insert event with origin=`bd`, observe writes to postgres + linear ONLY, no echo back to bd
- [ ] Idempotency test: re-emit same event, observe single dispatch (no double-write)
- [ ] All existing tests `tests/scripts/test_completion_sync_worker.py` ported and pass
- [ ] `ruff check && ruff format --check` clean
- [ ] PR open, dual-approval gate

## Depends on

* K2 (sync_events table) — must be merged first

## Blocks

None — K4 is independent.

title: [ATLAS] K2 — sync_events table + emit hooks (bd-Dolt + Postgres + Linear webhook)
description:
## Problem

No store currently publishes structured change events that the OTHER stores can consume. Existing sync paths are point-to-point and partial:

* `completion-sync-worker` (KEI-74) only fans out on `tasks.status='done'|'cancelled'` (no in_progress / open / label / priority / title)
* `linear_to_bd.py` webhook only handles `create` + `status` (no field updates)
* `bd linear sync --pull` is one-way (Linear → bd-Dolt)
* Nothing emits when bd-Dolt rows change

## Spec — Step 2 of 4

Create `public.sync_events` audit table — single source of truth for all cross-store changes:

```sql
CREATE TABLE public.sync_events (
  id            UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  origin        TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (origin IN ('bd', 'postgres', 'linear')),
  event_type    TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (event_type IN ('create', 'update', 'close', 'reopen', 'priority_change', 'title_change')),
  task_id       TEXT NOT NULL,       -- canonical Linear KEI-N (post-K1)
  bd_id         TEXT,                -- Agency_OS-xxx if known
  payload       JSONB NOT NULL,      -- full field snapshot
  payload_hash  TEXT GENERATED ALWAYS AS (encode(digest(payload::text, 'sha256'), 'hex')) STORED,
  processed     BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE,
  attempts      INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  created_at    TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);
CREATE INDEX idx_sync_events_unprocessed ON public.sync_events(created_at) WHERE processed = FALSE;
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX uq_sync_events_dedupe ON public.sync_events(task_id, payload_hash) WHERE processed = FALSE;
```

Three emit paths:

1. **bd-Dolt** → `.beads/hooks/post-write.sh` (if Beads supports git-style hook) OR a `bd remember`-driven shim that runs after `bd create/update/close` and inserts via psycopg
2. **Postgres** → generalise `trg_tasks_status_to_sync_queue` to `trg_tasks_to_sync_events` covering INSERT + UPDATE on any column (status / priority / title / description / labels)
3. **Linear webhook** → modify `src/api/webhooks/linear.py` to insert into `public.sync_events` (origin=`linear`) instead of calling `linear_to_bd.py` directly

## Acceptance

- [ ] Migration applied (verbatim)
- [ ] Three emit paths instrumented; each emits a synthetic row when its origin store is touched
- [ ] Unit tests for each emit path (mock the other side)
- [ ] Integration smoke: edit a row in each origin store, observe `sync_events` row landing with correct `origin` tag (verbatim DB query)
- [ ] `ruff check && ruff format --check` clean
- [ ] PR open, dual-approval gate

## Depends on

* K1 (bd_id column) — must be merged first; `sync_events.task_id` joins on the canonical KEI-N established by K1

## Blocks

* K3 (sync_orchestrator drain worker reads `sync_events`)

title: [ATLAS] K1 — ID canonicalisation: public.tasks.bd_id column + backfill (sync foundation)
description:
## Problem

Today's three-store drift between **bd-Dolt**, **Postgres** `public.tasks`, and **Linear** is rooted in an ID-key mismatch:

* bd-Dolt keys on `Agency_OS-xxx` short codes (236 issues)
* Linear keys on `KEI-N` per-team sequence
* Postgres `tasks.id` mixes both: **171** `KEI-*` **rows** (from `reconcile_linear_supabase.py`) + **1** `Agency_OS-*` **row** (from `tasks_cli.py` heartbeat/claim) — no join key between them

Direct evidence (atlas session 2026-05-19): `bd close Agency_OS-50hd4r --evidence -` failed with `psycopg.errors.ForeignKeyViolation: Key (task_id)=(Agency_OS-50hd4r) is not present in table "tasks"`. Falls back to `bd update --status=closed` which closes bd-side only; Postgres trigger never fires; completion never reaches Linear.

## Spec — Step 1 of 4 of bi-directional sync architecture

Add `public.tasks.bd_id` column to carry the bd-Dolt short-code paired with each Linear `KEI-N`:

```sql
ALTER TABLE public.tasks ADD COLUMN bd_id TEXT;
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX uq_tasks_bd_id ON public.tasks(bd_id) WHERE bd_id IS NOT NULL;
CREATE INDEX idx_tasks_bd_id ON public.tasks(bd_id);
```

Backfill script `scripts/backfill_tasks_bd_id.py`:

1. Iterate every `public.tasks` row WHERE `bd_id IS NULL AND linear_url IS NOT NULL`
2. Match against `bd list --json` rows by `external_ref` URL — bd's existing pairing key
3. UPDATE Postgres row SET `bd_id = '<Agency_OS-xxx>'`
4. Emit verbatim diff summary: matched / unmatched / dual-claims (one Linear URL → multiple bd IDs)

Update `scripts/tasks_cli.py` writers (lines 153, 525, 549, 698, 741):

* When writing heartbeat/claim/verify rows, write **Linear** `KEI-N` **as primary** `id` if a bd↔Linear pair exists; preserve `bd_id` column with the `Agency_OS-xxx`
* If no Linear pair yet, fall through to current behaviour (write `Agency_OS-xxx`) — `bd_id` field unused on that row
* All future readers join on `bd_id` OR `id` depending on which store they entered from

## Acceptance

- [ ] Migration applied to Supabase project `jatzvazlbusedwsnqxzr` (verbatim `apply_migration` output)
- [ ] Backfill script runs dry-run cleanly + paste verbatim summary (matched/unmatched counts)
- [ ] Backfill `--apply` run paste verbatim diff
- [ ] `tasks_cli.py` writers updated; existing tests `tests/scripts/test_tasks_cli.py` still pass (verbatim pytest output)
- [ ] New test `tests/scripts/test_backfill_tasks_bd_id.py` covers the matching + ambiguous-match cases
- [ ] `ruff check && ruff format --check` clean (verbatim)
- [ ] PR open with `[ATLAS]` tag, dual-approval gate

## Source

Atlas diagnosis 2026-05-19 — drift count: bd 147 active vs Postgres 3 active = 144-issue gap. ID-mismatch is root cause #1 of 5.

## Blocks

K2 (sync_events table), K3 (sync_orchestrator), K4 (reconcile_three_stores) — all downstream depend on stable join key.

title: [BUG] KEI-228 — Nova missing relay-watcher + inbox-watcher systemd services + scripts
description:
## Problem

Nova has nova-agent.service enabled+active but lacks the structured inbox/outbox dispatch channel that every other clone-tier callsign has. Verbatim systemctl inventory:

  atlas-inbox-watcher.service    enabled
  atlas-relay-watcher.service    enabled
  orion-inbox-watcher.service    enabled
  orion-relay-watcher.service    enabled
  scout-inbox-watcher.service    enabled
  scout-relay-watcher.service    disabled
  aiden-relay-watcher.service    disabled
  max-relay-watcher.service      enabled
  (NO nova-inbox-watcher.service)
  (NO nova-relay-watcher.service)

Effect: Nova receives no structured dispatch — only tmux send-keys, which is fragile (relies on cursor position, no acknowledgment, no replay-on-restart). Nova explicitly asked for this.

## Cause

The canonical per-callsign watcher scripts live at /home/elliotbot/clawd/scripts/ (host shared, outside all Agency_OS worktrees). Existing scripts:
  atlas_inbox_watcher.sh    (1954 bytes, 2026-04-26)
  atlas_relay_watcher.sh    (1176 bytes, 2026-04-26)
  orion_inbox_watcher.sh    (1895 bytes, 2026-04-26)
  orion_relay_watcher.sh    (1165 bytes, 2026-04-26)
  scout_inbox_watcher.sh    (2005 bytes, 2026-05-12)

Missing: nova_inbox_watcher.sh, nova_relay_watcher.sh, scout_relay_watcher.sh.

Systemd unit files at ~/.config/systemd/user/ are also host-local (not in repo).

## Fix

1. Copy atlas_inbox_watcher.sh → /home/elliotbot/clawd/scripts/nova_inbox_watcher.sh, sed atlas→nova.
2. Copy atlas_relay_watcher.sh → /home/elliotbot/clawd/scripts/nova_relay_watcher.sh, sed atlas→nova.
3. Copy ~/.config/systemd/user/atlas-inbox-watcher.service → nova-inbox-watcher.service, sed atlas→nova (ExecStart path + Description).
4. Same for relay-watcher.service.
5. systemctl --user daemon-reload && systemctl --user enable --now nova-inbox-watcher.service nova-relay-watcher.service.

## Verification

- systemctl --user status nova-inbox-watcher.service nova-relay-watcher.service: both active (running).
- Write a test dispatch JSON to /tmp/telegram-relay-nova/inbox/test.json; watcher injects into nova tmux pane within ~5s.
- journalctl --user -u nova-inbox-watcher.service -n 20: no errors.

## Out of scope

- Bringing the host-private scripts/units into the Agency_OS repo as canonical templates. Worth doing but separate KEI (cross-callsign structural refactor; would touch all 4 existing watcher pairs).
- scout-relay-watcher.service is currently disabled — separate question about whether it should be enabled or if scout uses a different channel.

## Source

Dispatch from Elliot via outbox 2026-05-19. Nova explicitly requested 'start the relay-watcher service for nova'.
close_reason: Duplicate of Agency_OS-pz58 (filed by Elliot first; same scope: Nova missing inbox+relay watcher scripts + systemd units). Working pz58 instead.

Same fleet-infra gap as the self-claim-loop unit (Agency_OS-wj94 / Linear KEI-226). Impact: Nova cannot receive structured inbox dispatch files from elliot/parent (must rely on tmux send-keys which is brittle); Nova cannot send outbound status posts via outbox→Slack relay (must use other paths). Fix scope: (1) Copy /home/elliotbot/clawd/scripts/atlas_inbox_watcher.sh → nova_inbox_watcher.sh, sed atlas→nova on paths + TMUX_TARGET=nova:0.0; (2) Same for atlas_relay_watcher.sh; (3) Copy /home/elliotbot/.config/systemd/user/atlas-inbox-watcher.service → nova-inbox-watcher.service, sed ATLAS→NOVA + atlas→nova; (4) Same for atlas-relay-watcher.service; (5) systemctl --user daemon-reload && enable+start both nova-*-watcher services; (6) Verify via 'ls /tmp/telegram-relay-nova/inbox' inbox dir exists + relay watcher consuming outbox files. Linear mirror tag: KEI-228.

(2) /home/elliotbot/clawd/scripts/nova_relay_watcher.sh created (sed atlas→nova, TMUX_TARGET=elliottbot:0.0 preserved — Elliot is Nova's parent). (5) Both services systemctl --user start: Active(running) since 2026-05-19T00:27:49Z, inotifywait subprocesses spawned on /tmp/telegram-relay-nova/{inbox,outbox}. Directories auto-created on script start. bash -n syntax check passed on both scripts before enable. Cross-repo follow-up Agency_OS-b7jv filed for bd↔Linear staleness probe.

title: [BUG] Nova missing from agent-self-claim-loop@<callsign>.service fleet — 6/7 coverage
description: Discovered 2026-05-19 via NATS readiness audit triggered by Dave 'is NATS working' check. systemctl --user list-units shows agent-self-claim-loop@<callsign>.service active for aiden/atlas/elliot/max/orion/scout but NOT nova. Nova was added to the fleet as the 7th callsign in earlier dispatcher work but the corresponding self-claim-loop unit was never instantiated/started/enabled. Impact: nova never publishes keiracom.agent.status.nova to NATS, so the supervisor v2 (KEI-221) routing cannot observe nova state transitions; nova falls back to legacy Slack [READY:nova] pings which are subject to Slack rate-limits. Fix scope: (1) systemctl --user enable agent-self-claim-loop@nova.service, (2) systemctl --user start agent-self-claim-loop@nova.service, (3) verify .env has AGENT_ROUTING_NOVA=v2 (confirmed present already), (4) confirm via pgrep -af 'agent_self_claim_loop.*nova', (5) verify NATS sub keiracom.agent.status.nova receives events on next nova busy→ready transition. (1)(2) executed inline at file time. Linear mirror tag: KEI-226.
close_reason: Nova self-claim-loop service confirmed active. Verbatim: systemctl --user status agent-self-claim-loop@nova.service → 'Active: active (running) since Tue 2026-05-19 00:14:22 UTC; 28min ago. Main PID: 1348329 (bash) /home/elliotbot/clawd/Agency_OS/scripts/orchestrator/agent_self_claim_loop.sh --callsign nova'. 7/7 fleet coverage restored. Closed per Elliot 2026-05-19 correction.

title: [WORKER] Composio OAuth handler + routes — post Dave-action vendor onboarding (KEI-124 follow-up)
description:
## What Implement the handler + routes for KEI-124 Composio OAuth. The data-layer migration `oauth_provider_tokens` + skill scaffold lands in PR (this session). Implementation requires Dave-action prerequisites to complete first. ## Dave-action prerequisites (BLOCKS this bd) Per skills/composio-oauth/SKILL.md §Dave-action prerequisites: 1. Composio.dev account creation (organisation: Agency_OS-scout owner). 2. OAuth app registration on Slack / Linear / GitHub via Composio dashboard with documented scopes. 3. Issue Composio master API key → seed env vars `COMPOSIO_API_KEY` (prod) + `COMPOSIO_API_KEY_DEV` (staging) → record in `elliot_internal.api_keys_ledger`. 4. Configure Composio callback URL: `https://api.keiracom.com/api/v1/composio/callback` (+ staging equivalent). 5. Confirm `CUSTOMER_KEY_ENCRYPTION_KEY` provisioned in both environments. ## Spec (post-unblock) - `src/auth/composio_oauth.py` — handler with `start_oauth_flow(tenant_id, provider) -> {oauth_url, state}` + `finalize_connection(state, composio_connection_id, db) -> UUID` + `store_tokens(...)`. Provider allowlist hard-coded: {'slack','linear','github'}. - `src/api/routes/composio.py` — `POST /api/v1/composio/connect?provider=

` + `GET /api/v1/composio/callback?state=...&composio_connection_id=...` + (optional) `POST /api/v1/composio/webhook` for vendor-side revocation events. - Settings: `composio_api_key`, `composio_base_url`, `composio_callback_url` (read from env, fail-fast if unset in prod). - Router registered in `src/api/main.py`. - Tests: `tests/auth/test_composio_oauth.py` + `tests/api/routes/test_composio.py`. Composio HTTP calls mocked via httpx mock. Cover: happy-path, state-replay, expired-state, vendor-503, provider-not-in-allowlist, revoke-cascade-on-customer-soft-delete. ## Acceptance criteria - [ ] All five Dave-action prerequisites confirmed live in `elliot_internal.api_keys_ledger` — paste verbatim query result before claiming. - [ ] `POST /api/v1/composio/connect?provider=slack` returns `{oauth_url, state}` with valid CSRF state stored in Redis (10-min TTL, mirrors crm.py KEI-309 pattern). - [ ] `GET /api/v1/composio/callback` with valid state + connection_id writes encrypted token row to `oauth_provider_tokens` then 302s to frontend. - [ ] Provider allowlist enforced — 400 on unknown provider. - [ ] Vendor 5xx → handler returns 503 (not 500), logs to `interceptor_events`, surfaces to #ceo via existing alert path. - [ ] Soft-revoke cascade trigger when matching `dispatcher_customers` row is soft-deleted. - [ ] Unit + integration tests pass (paste verbatim pytest output). - [ ] Sonar QG=OK, issues=0 on the PR per dual-Sonar AND-rule (KEI-189).

title: [DRIFT AUDIT] bd in_progress vs merged PR cross-reference — systemic close-on-merge gap
description:
## Problem

bd in_progress state systematically drifts from reality: KEIs remain in_progress for hours/days after the PR delivering them is merged. Observed 4 times in 2026-05-18→19 dispatch cycles alone:

- 2026-05-18 dispatch #1: 70st (PR #1046) + lsyd (PR #1060) — both already merged when proposed as in_progress P1 work.
- 2026-05-18 dispatch #2: hzk5 (PR #1053) + 6suajk (PR #983) — both already merged when proposed as P1 candidates.
- 2026-05-19 drift-audit (Elliot, manual pass): 8 more stale entries — tjgihu/PR#1033, v1e279/PR#1038, 50hd4r/PR#1030, p2b6/PR#1030, qiqvu1/PR#954, 5b0t/PR#1021, 524m/PR#1021, gpis/PR#1025+#994.

## Cost

Every stale entry wastes ≥1 redispatch cycle:
- Dispatch agent (Elliot) writes brief assuming the work is open.
- Worker agent (Atlas/Orion/Scout/Nova) does scrutiny pass + duplicate-work alert.
- Outbox traffic + context burn + no shipped work.

Two of yesterday's four dispatches collapsed to "scrutiny only" because every candidate was already shipped.

## Proposed fix

Cron-able audit script that flags in_progress bd KEIs whose KEI-ID appears in a merged-PR title in the same repo. Cross-tracker check, advisory output, recommends closure.

### Algorithm

```
for kei in bd list --status=in_progress --json:
    kei_ids = extract_kei_ids(kei.title + kei.description + kei.notes)
    for kei_id in kei_ids:
        merged_prs = gh api repos/Keiracom/Agency_OS/pulls?state=closed
                     | filter merged==true AND title contains kei_id
        if merged_prs:
            print(kei.id, kei_id, merged_prs[0].number, merged_prs[0].mergedAt)
            print("  → bd-original close", kei.id, "--reason=PR #N merged DATE")
```

### Scope

- One Python script: `scripts/drift_audit_bd_state.py` — runs locally, takes ~30s for ~50 in_progress KEIs.
- One systemd timer: `systemd/drift_audit_bd_state.timer` — daily 06:00.
- Output goes to `/home/elliotbot/clawd/logs/drift_audit.log` + posts a summary to #execution if any drift found.
- Does NOT auto-close. Advisory only; human ratifies via bd-original close.

### Out of scope

- Reverse direction (merged PR with no matching in_progress KEI — that's coverage gap, separate audit).
- Linear sync (bd↔Linear is a separate worker — Agency_OS-9kfy + Agency_OS-iosu).

## Acceptance

- [ ] `scripts/drift_audit_bd_state.py` exists + has unit tests.
- [ ] Dry-run on 2026-05-19 surfaces all 8 entries listed above.
- [ ] systemd timer installed; first run completes successfully on host.
- [ ] If drift > 0, posts a one-line summary to #execution outbox.

## Source

Pattern observed by Atlas across 4 redispatch cycles 2026-05-18→19. Proposed by Atlas to Elliot via outbox 2026-05-19; Elliot ratified filing this KEI as P1.

title: ceo_memory_indexer.py — same prepare_threshold=None bug as PR #1046
description: Decisions count stuck at 300 across multiple restarts even after PR #1046 (prepare_threshold=None for elliot_memories_indexer via indexer_base.run_db_indexer). Root cause: ceo_memory_indexer.py line 160 has its OWN psycopg.connect(_dsn(), autocommit=True) call, NOT routed through indexer_base.run_db_indexer. PR #1046's fix doesn't reach this file. Apply the same one-line fix at line 160: psycopg.connect(_dsn(), autocommit=True, prepare_threshold=None). Same pattern as PR #1046 + same reference_psycopg_supabase_pgbouncer memory. Add to existing PR #1046's regression test family OR write parallel test. Expected effect post-fix: Decisions count climbs 300 → real ceo_memory key count within ~3 min after restart.
notes: PR #1053 open — one-line fix at ceo_memory_indexer.py:160 (psycopg.connect prepare_threshold=None). Same pattern as PR #1046 at second site (ceo_memory has own main(), bypassed indexer_base.run_db_indexer). Empirically confirmed pre-fix: ceo-memory-indexer.log bleeding identical InvalidSqlStatementName loop as elliot-memories-indexer was. Regression test mirrors test_indexer_base_prepare_threshold_kei70st; negative-path verified. Post-merge: systemctl --user restart ceo-memory-indexer → Decisions count climbs off 300 within ~3min.
close_reason: already shipped — PR #1053 (atlas) merged 2026-05-18T23:12:47Z. ceo_memory_indexer.py line 160 now passes prepare_threshold=None per the bug spec. CI all green (SonarCloud, mypy, pytest, ruff). Empirical verify: cat scripts/orchestrator/ceo_memory_indexer.py | grep prepare_threshold.

title: Fix AgentMemories indexer 97% drift (192/7432)
description: elliot-memories-indexer.service active running but Weaviate AgentMemories=192 while Supabase public.agent_memories has 7432 non-archived rows (identity_fact=1600, decision=1357, lesson=997, milestone=978, pattern=713, ceo_instruction=385, daily_log=226...). Indexer emits zero log output in journalctl over last 30min. Likely: watermark logic broken, type filter too strict, or batch-fail silent-fail. Backfill remaining 7240 rows + add log emission on every batch (loud success/failure).
notes:
ROOT CAUSE (PR #1046): indexer_base.run_db_indexer missing prepare_threshold=None — Supabase pgbouncer txn-mode drops PREPARE between leases. psycopg3 auto-prepared after 5 executions, then DuplicatePreparedStatement → InvalidSqlStatementName loop for 20h. Pattern matches reference_psycopg_supabase_pgbouncer (KEI-54B PR #881); 12+ files in src/ already apply it.

DRIFT FRAMING CORRECTED:
- Spec said 192/7432 (97%) — two tables conflated
- elliot_internal.memories (what indexer reads): 1665 rows, 192 indexed → 88% drift, in-scope
- public.agent_memories: 7433 rows, 0 indexed → no indexer exists, OUT OF SCOPE for this fix

PR: https://github.com/Keiracom/Agency_OS/pull/1046
- One-line fix in indexer_base.py:262 (prepare_threshold=None)
- Regression test (negative-path verified)
- ruff clean
- Service restart deferred to post-merge (main worktree only; Atlas can't write to it)

FOLLOW-UP: Agency_OS-lsyd (P2) filed for public.agent_memories indexer + 7433-row backfill.

LOG ROUTING NOTE: original "no journalctl output" was diagnostic red herring — service routes to /home/elliotbot/clawd/logs/elliot-memories-indexer.log via StandardOutput=append:..., not journald. Log file has 30,961 lines of the error stream.
close_reason: PR #1046 (70st prepare_threshold fix) MERGED 2026-05-18 21:17Z; PR #1060 (lsyd public.agent_memories indexer + 7400-row backfill) MERGED 2026-05-18 23:20Z. Atlas correction — bd state was stale.

title: ENV schema validation hook — critical-5 env vars fail-fast at SessionStart
description:
## Problem

Misconfigured agent env (missing `DATABASE_URL`, `SLACK_BOT_TOKEN`, `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `OPENAI_API_KEY`, `CALLSIGN`) surfaces only when a downstream hook or the agent's first MCP call crashes. That's slow, obscure, and easy to miss when bringing up a new worktree on Vultr.

## Fix

`scripts/hooks/env_schema_validate.sh` checks the critical-5 at session start, prints a clear remediation message on miss, exits non-zero. Registered FIRST in the `SessionStart` `*` hook chain in `.claude/settings.json` so any miss surfaces immediately.

The hook auto-sources `$AGENCY_OS_ENV` (default `$HOME/.config/agency-os/.env`) when the calling shell hasn't already exported the vars — SessionStart contexts often start with a near-empty env.

## Acceptance

- [ ] `scripts/hooks/env_schema_validate.sh` exits non-zero when any of the 5 are missing, with a clear list naming them
- [ ] Empty-string env vars are treated as missing (catches botched rotations)
- [ ] `DATABASE_URL` OR `SUPABASE_DB_URL` satisfies the DB requirement
- [ ] Registered FIRST in `.claude/settings.json` `SessionStart` `*` chain (fail-fast)
- [ ] Bash syntax valid; `set -u` survives stripped-env callers (no HOME)
- [ ] 14 unit + integration tests pass

## Source

Dispatch from Elliot 2026-05-18 — STEP 0 PRE-CONFIRMED. Beads `Agency_OS-9erxio` P1.
