title: KEI-222: CI ghost-green — pytest passes in CI but fails locally
description:
Dave directive 2026-05-18 + Max empirical finding: PR #1027 Backend Tests (Pytest) reported SUCCESS in CI (1m55s) but Max ran locally on origin/pull/1027/head and got verbatim:

FAILED tests/test_slack_relay_callsign_fix.py::test_forbidden_channel_blocks
AssertionError: assert 'aiden-relay refuses post to C0B2PM3TV0B' in 'R_CEO_FORMAT_BLOCKED: #ceo post violates plain-English bullets-only convention...'

Aiden independently reproduced. CI false-green is systemic — every PR we've merged today on green pytest signal is suspect.

Investigation scope:
- CI workflow cached from previous green commit? Check workflow run logs for PR #1027.
- Test collection excludes the file? Check pytest.ini / pyproject.toml / conftest filters.
- CI runs different test set than local pytest? Compare CI command vs local invocation.
- Workflow filter excluding this branch? Check .github/workflows/*.yml for path/branch filters.

Verification: run a controlled experiment — submit a PR with a deliberately failing test, confirm CI marks RED. Then audit recent PRs merged today (#1026, #1023, #1022, #1021, #1020) for similar ghost-green risk.
acceptance_criteria: Root cause identified + fix in place that makes deliberately failing tests show RED in CI

title: [WORKER] Drive → Weaviate indexer — StrategicDocuments collection + bulk backfill + incremental sync
description:
## Why

Drive docs contain all ratified strategic decisions — roadmap, team structure, deliberation floor spec, architecture. Agents cannot semantically query Drive. Everything in Drive needs to be in Weaviate so agents can retrieve context without re-explaining it every session.

## What

Build a Drive → Weaviate indexer. Same pattern as [KEI-201](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-201/scout-slack-historical-extraction-to-weaviate-bulk-backfill) (Slack → Weaviate).

**Phase 1 — Create StrategicDocuments collection in Weaviate**

```python
{
  "class": "StrategicDocuments",
  "vectorizer": "text2vec-openai",
  "properties": [
    {"name": "doc_id", "dataType": ["text"]},
    {"name": "doc_url", "dataType": ["text"]},
    {"name": "title", "dataType": ["text"]},
    {"name": "section", "dataType": ["text"]},
    {"name": "content", "dataType": ["text"]},
    {"name": "updated_at", "dataType": ["date"]},
    {"name": "ratified_by", "dataType": ["text"]},
    {"name": "ratified_at", "dataType": ["date"]}
  ]
}
```

**Phase 2 — Bulk backfill these docs immediately**

```
1Br6SsCKizvyNk6dOvm9EQE_8drut1WRId7WJNddilDA  Roadmap (ratified 2026-05-18)
1U5uet-IxJSNhE-iWlVdW3eZpNR8obf-7j3BzvaEWdH4  Deliberation Floor Flow Architecture v2
113Ej0n62uS_qmyMwvase54kFI1eCCwHHwwrMN9GDlCc  Team Structure (ratified 2026-05-18)
1NNoA-6MgXZS6mk35QCeer3mmEnqNVRSD7NBmu0fzfHw  Architecture FINAL (Parts 1-16)
1kf-MVHeHaViwlMuy3ti_pZCS1DJE9ox8vIa3aZdspkY  Team Structure Brief v2
1Br6SsCKizvyNk6dOvm9EQE_8drut1WRId7WJNddilDA  Official product roadmap
```

Chunk strategy: split by heading (h1/h2/h3 boundaries), not fixed token size. Each section becomes one Weaviate object. Preserves semantic coherence.

**Phase 3 — Incremental indexer**

* Poll Drive API every 6 hours for modifiedTime changes on target docs
* On change: re-fetch, re-chunk, upsert (overwrite by doc_id + section)
* Add new docs by appending their ID to a config file: `config/drive_index_targets.json`
* No hardcoded doc IDs in the indexer code — all driven by config

**Phase 4 — Validation**
Run semantic query: "what did Dave decide about team structure" 
Confirm top result returns from StrategicDocuments collection with certainty > 0.70
Paste verbatim result.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] StrategicDocuments collection exists in Weaviate with OpenAI vectorizer
- [ ] All 5 target docs chunked and indexed (row count per doc posted)
- [ ] Incremental indexer running as systemd service or cron
- [ ] Semantic validation query returns relevant hit > 0.70 certainty
- [ ] drive_index_targets.json config file exists for adding new docs
- [ ] ceo_memory updated: ceo:memory:drive_indexer = live

title: [WORKER] GitHub PR merge webhook → auto-close Supabase task
description:
## Problem

PR merges on GitHub don't update Supabase task status. KEI tasks stay "available" or "active" after their PR merges. This breaks the dependency chain — Phase N+1 tasks don't surface because Phase N tasks never show as "done".

This is structural staleness. NATS doesn't fix it. The Dispatcher + webhook bridge does.

## Solution

Extend the existing GitHub webhook handler to close tasks on PR merge.

When a PR merge event fires:

1. Extract the PR title and body
2. Parse KEI ID from title (regex: `KEI-\d+`)
3. Fallback: match via `linear_url` column if title parse fails
4. Run: `UPDATE tasks SET status='done', updated_at=NOW() WHERE id = 'KEI-XXX'`
5. Dispatcher picks up via Supabase Realtime — next phase tasks surface automatically

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] PR merge webhook event handled in existing webhook handler
- [ ] KEI ID parsed from PR title (regex: `KEI-\d+`)
- [ ] Fallback: match via `linear_url` column if title parse fails
- [ ] Task marked `done` in Supabase on merge only (not on open/push)
- [ ] Test: merge a test PR → confirm task status updates within 5 seconds
- [ ] Paste verbatim Supabase row showing status='done' after test merge
- [ ] If no matching KEI found: log and skip, do not error

title: [ELLIOT] Supervisor dep-blocked drift fix — regex parse 'FOLLOW-UP after KEI-N' before claim dispatch
description:
Aiden flagged 3rd drift category. Mechanical fix via regex parse on KEI title + description for dep-blocked work.

**Context:** 3-way ratified (Aiden + Max + Elliot). Path (b) regex over path (a) JSONB column. Reasons: ships in 1 session (\~5-10 LoC at claim_next_task SELECT), empirically covers all 4 drift patterns this session.

**Patterns to match:**

* `FOLLOW-UP after KEI-N` (e.g. KEI-191)
* `sub of KEI-N` (e.g. KEI-193/194)
* `(KEI-N follow-up)` (e.g. KEI-196/197/198)
* `depends on KEI-N` (Linear convention)

**Implementation:** in scripts/fleet_supervisor.py claim_next_task, after fetching candidate task, extract blocker KEI-IDs from title + description via regex. Query: `SELECT id FROM public.tasks WHERE id IN (...blockers...) AND status != 'done'`. If non-empty result: skip this candidate, try next.

**Acceptance:** test KEI 'FOLLOW-UP after KEI-185' exists; KEI-185 status='active' (not done); supervisor cycle does NOT dispatch the FOLLOW-UP KEI; logs 'skip — blocker KEI-185 not done'.

**Sub-KEI under KEI-183** (Supervisor v2). Sister to KEI-194 (JWT hash rotation) + KEI-199 (PR-existence pre-check). Three sub-KEIs close all 3 supervisor blind spots: already-shipped, cross-lane, dep-blocked.

**Long-term (a) JSONB path** filed separately as future follow-up after persona-column lessons (KEI-183) prove out the Linear-webhook-writer pattern.

**Phase:** 0.5 **Priority:** P1 **Granularity:** 1 session. 3-way ratified (Max + Aiden + Elliot).

title: [SCOUT] Slack historical extraction to Weaviate — bulk backfill + incremental indexer
description:
## Why

Slack contains the complete operational history of the Keiracom system — months of fleet supervisor observations, CEO directives, agent escalations, architectural decisions, completion reports, and debugging sessions. None of this is currently indexed in Weaviate. Extracting it would dramatically enrich the memory system with historical patterns that agents can immediately draw on.

## What

Bulk historical extraction of all relevant Slack channels into Weaviate, then ongoing incremental indexing.

**Channels to extract:**

* #ceo — CEO directives, fleet status, escalations, architectural decisions
* #execution — Agent work, CONCUR events, PR reviews, completions
* #completed_directives — Completion records
* #alerts — System alerts and incidents
* #ops — Operational events

**What NOT to extract:**

* \[READY:callsign\] heartbeat spam — pure noise, no signal value
* Fleet status posts (fleet supervisor will write these directly to agent_memories via [KEI-200](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-200/atlas-fleet-supervisor-writes-observations-to-agent-memories-on-every) going forward)
* Duplicate messages (dedup by content hash)

**Categorisation on ingest:**
Each message gets tagged by type before indexing:

* `ceo_directive` — CEO instructions and decisions
* `agent_escalation` — Agent asking for help or flagging a blocker
* `architectural_decision` — Design or architecture ratification
* `supervisor_observation` — Fleet supervisor detection + action
* `completion_report` — Task completion with evidence
* `debug_session` — Problem diagnosis and resolution
* `governance_event` — CONCUR, HOLD, ratification events

**Target Weaviate collection:** `slack_history` (new collection, tagged by channel + type + timestamp)

## Implementation

1. Use Slack API to paginate through each channel's history from the beginning
2. Filter out noise messages (\[READY\], basic fleet status heartbeats)
3. Categorise each message using a lightweight classifier
4. Write to Weaviate `slack_history` collection with text2vec-transformers vectorizer
5. Store metadata: channel, timestamp, callsign/author, message_type, thread_ts if threaded
6. After bulk load: set up incremental indexer (runs every 15 minutes, indexes new messages since last run)

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `slack_history` Weaviate collection created with text2vec-transformers vectorizer
- [ ] Historical messages extracted from all 5 channels with noise filtered
- [ ] Messages categorised by type
- [ ] Semantic query "psycopg asyncpg DSN bug" returns relevant results from historical #execution messages
- [ ] Semantic query "fleet supervisor idle detection" returns relevant results
- [ ] Incremental indexer running as systemd service, updating every 15 minutes

## Value

This gives the memory system immediate access to the complete operational history of the system. Every problem ever encountered, every solution ever applied, every decision ever made — all immediately retrievable semantically. Agents stop repeating mistakes that are documented in Slack history.
notes: Shipped via PR #1044 (commit d9c613390). 5/5 spec channels live in Slack_history (14,772 rows). Root cause: resolve_channel_ids defaulted to public_channel only — silently dropped private channels. Fix: types=public_channel,private_channel + exclude_archived=False. Together with PR #1001 + PR #1025, KEI-201 fully delivered. GOV-9 GAP (79.5% agent_chatter fallback) standing as separate KEI.

title: [ATLAS] Fleet supervisor writes observations to agent_memories on every detection and resolution
description:
## Why

The fleet supervisor detects \~25 types of agent idle scenarios every cycle and takes corrective action. Currently all observations go only to Slack and are lost. This institutional knowledge — what broke, when, why, how it was resolved — is exactly what Weaviate needs to eventually replace the supervisor with intelligent Dispatcher routing.

## What

Add a write call to `agent_memories` on every supervisor cycle where a detection + resolution occurs.

**Write on each of these events:**

* Stale claim detected and released
* Dead session detected and restarted
* Agent idle with queue items — task injected
* PR re-dispatched (and to which agent)
* Cross-lane assignment made (and why)
* Agent context full — restarted
* Any recovery action taken

**Schema for each write:**

```python
{
  "callsign": "system",
  "source_type": "supervisor_observation",
  "content": "{agent} was {idle_reason} at {timestamp}. Action taken: {action}. Result: {result}",
  "typed_metadata": {
    "agent": "<callsign>",
    "idle_reason": "<stale_claim|dead_session|no_claim_queue_items|context_full|...>",
    "action_taken": "<released|restarted|injected|re-dispatched>",
    "task_id": "<kei_id if applicable>",
    "resolved": true/false
  },
  "tags": ["supervisor", "fleet_observation", "<idle_reason>"],
  "confidence": 1.0,
  "trust": 1.0
}
```

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Supervisor writes one `agent_memories` row per detection+resolution event per cycle
- [ ] Rows visible in Supabase with callsign=system, source_type=supervisor_observation
- [ ] After 3 supervisor cycles, at least one observation row exists per agent
- [ ] Weaviate indexer picks these up on next index cycle (via AgentMemories collection)

## Why this matters

This is the dataset that allows the Dispatcher + Weaviate to eventually replace the supervisor. Every pattern it learns from resolving idle scenarios becomes retrievable institutional knowledge. The supervisor builds its own replacement.

title: [ELLIOT] Supervisor PR-existence pre-check — skip claim-dispatch if open PR mentions KEI (4 drift-syncs anchor)
description:
Aiden flagged 4 drift-syncs this session: KEI-90, KEI-122, KEI-187, KEI-188 — each got auto-claimed by the supervisor AFTER an OPEN PR existed implementing the work. Agents had to drift-sync done state + write task_verifications by hand.

**Root cause:** supervisor's claim_next_task query checks `WHERE status='available'` but doesn't query GitHub for an OPEN PR whose title or body mentions the KEI.

**Fix:** Add `pr_exists_for_kei(kei_id)` helper in fleet_supervisor.py mirroring the existing `agent_has_reviewed` shape. Before claim dispatch, call it:

```python
if pr_exists_for_kei(kei_id):
    log.info(f"[supervisor] skip {kei_id} — open PR already in flight")
    continue
```

Implementation: `gh pr list --state open --search "<KEI-ID>" --json number,title,body` then bool(len(results) > 0). Cache results within a single supervisor cycle to avoid redundant gh calls.

**Sub-KEI under KEI-183** (Supervisor v2). Can ship in parallel or as fix-up after KEI-183 merges.

**Acceptance:** test PR #N exists with title containing 'KEI-NN'; supervisor cycle queries KEI-NN; supervisor logs 'skip — open PR' and does NOT inject claim or send tmux prompt.

**Phase:** 0.5 **Priority:** P1 — mechanical drift prevention, anchored on 4 real drift-syncs this session.

title: M3: Update agent_query default min_score to score-distribution-aware (KEI-192 follow-up)
description:
Aiden's audit revealed hard min_score=0.50 floor is fragile. When scores are all 0.0 (vectorizer regression), everything filters out. Defensive: replace hard floor with p50 of returned scores OR top-N picker that returns best-available regardless of score.

**Fix:** edit src/retrieval/agent_query.py — instead of `qualified = (c for c in citations if c.score >= min_score)`, use `top_n = sorted(citations, key=lambda c: c.score, reverse=True)[:k]` — always return best-N regardless of absolute scores. Add hard fallback OFF only when ALL scores are zero (sentinel flag).

**Acceptance:** test with synthetic 0-score citations returns top-N anyway (not empty); test with mixed-score citations returns highest-scored first; integration test against current vectorless collections returns SOMETHING not nothing.

**Phase:** 0.5 **Priority:** P1 — defensive against future vectorizer regressions. Lower than M1 but still important.

title: Install 4 indexers as systemd user services on this host (was KEI-th6 in Beads)
description:
Per Dave 2026-05-18 execution directive Step 1-7 parallel slot for \[ATLAS\].

The 4 indexers exist as code + systemd unit files but NOT installed as user services. This is the load-bearing memory-pipeline gap (per Aiden + Max in 7-area deliberation).

**The 4 indexers:**

* linear_state_indexer (scripts/orchestrator/linear_state_indexer.py + infra/systemd/agents/linear-state-indexer.service + scripts/install_linear_state_indexer.sh)
* git_commits_indexer (same shape, KEI-85 phase C)
* ceo_memory_indexer (already running per earlier session — verify)
* elliot_memories_indexer (KEI-109 sub 2/3 — verify)

**Lane clarification for Atlas:** No Vultr SSH needed. The agent worktree IS on the Vultr host (you ARE on Vultr; /home/elliotbot/clawd/Agency_OS-atlas is local). The install scripts are `bash scripts/install_<X>.sh` running as elliotbot user; they use `systemctl --user enable + start` not system-level.

**Acceptance:**

* `systemctl --user is-active linear-state-indexer = active`
* `systemctl --user is-active git-commits-indexer = active`
* `systemctl --user is-active ceo-memory-indexer = active` (or verify existing systemd unit)
* `systemctl --user is-active elliot-memories-indexer = active`
* All 4 timers firing on their scheduled cadence
* Weaviate Aggregate counts on Keis + Codebase + Decisions + AgentMemories increase within first poll cycle

**Phase:** 0.5 **Priority:** P1 **Granularity:** 1 session. Per 3-way ratified consolidated sequence step 4.

title: KEI-194 (sub of KEI-185): JWT ratified_decisions_hash rotation — short TTL + auto-reissue, never global-halt

Without short-TTL + auto-reissue, the day Dave ratifies a new global_governance_patterns row = every active container halts mid-task on next Dispatcher call. Implement per Max's path (a): JWT TTL 5min + auth_minter auto-reissue on hash mismatch (matches KEI-115C existing 1-hour JWT expiry pattern). Aiden's mechanical JWT.persona vs IDENTITY.md.callsign check stays at Dispatcher proxy validator.

**Acceptance:** Dispatcher integration test: ratify a test decision in global_governance_patterns → verify active containers receive reissued JWT with new hash on next call within 5min, NO mid-task halt.

**Phase:** 0.5 **Priority:** P1 **Granularity:** 1 session. Refinement of KEI-185 scope. 3-way ratified.

title: KEI-H: Supervisor symmetric HOLD parsing — recognize [REVIEW:HOLD:callsign] same as APPROVE
description:
KEI-176 PR #979 fixed APPROVE detection but supervisor still re-dispatches HOLD'd PRs every 5-min cycle (4+ re-dispatches per HOLD'd PR today per Scout+Max+Aiden complaints). Add HOLD pattern to fleet_supervisor.agent_has_reviewed: parse \[REVIEW:HOLD:callsign\] + \[REVIEW:HOLD-FINAL:callsign\] same as approve markers; if any HOLD by callsign present, skip dispatching.

**Acceptance:** supervisor runs cycle, PR has \[REVIEW:HOLD:scout\] comment, supervisor does NOT inject review prompt to scout; agent confirms via tmux capture no auto-claim prompt.

**Phase:** 0.5 **Priority:** P1 **Granularity:** 1 session. 3-way ratified (Aiden addition).

Encode dual-endpoint check in supervisor review-brief generator: require BOTH /api/issues/search AND /api/qualitygates/project_status results in the review prompt. Encodes feedback_sonarcloud_verify_pattern memory pin mechanically.

**Acceptance:** supervisor review-brief shows both Sonar issues + QG conditions; agent reviewers see QG state separately from issues; HOLD criteria trigger on either endpoint failing.

**Phase:** 0.5 **Priority:** P1 **Granularity:** 1 session. 3-way ratified (Aiden addition).

Sources: AU Privacy Act 1988 + Australian Privacy Principles + GDPR Articles 5/6/13/17/20/28 + CCPA basics + OAIC breach notification + Paddle MoR DPA template + standard SaaS ToS patterns + AI Act draft. Each chunk metadata-tagged with source_url + source_date for traceability.

title: KEI-D: Single-reviewer-eligible gate in fleet supervisor review-brief emission
description:
Encode low-risk PR review path in supervisor. Single-reviewer-eligible when ALL: Sonar issues=0 AND QG=OK (dual endpoint) AND CI all mandatory green AND diff ≤100 LoC AND only touches docs/ tests/ supabase/migrations/ infra/ paths AND no new public.\* table/column AND no new route/webhook AND no xfailed tests in diff AND no new file under supabase/migrations/.

**Phase:** 0.5 **Priority:** P1 **Granularity:** 1 session. 3-way ratified.

title: LiteLLM Prisma can't connect to Supabase pooler — connection layer fix
description:
KEI-178 follow-up. The Anthropic-credit-block was resolved (boot check now Gemini-first per Dave directive) but a deeper layer remains:

LiteLLM's Prisma client fails on startup with httpx.ConnectError 'All connection attempts failed' when trying to connect to Supabase pooler ([aws-1-ap-southeast-1.pooler.supabase.com:6543](<http://aws-1-ap-southeast-1.pooler.supabase.com:6543>)).

Suspected causes (any/all):

* Password URL-encoding (current password contains '!' which may need %21)
* Pooler requires SSL/TLS that Prisma isn't configuring
* Pooler txn mode incompatible with Prisma's prepared-statement pattern (same family as reference_psycopg_supabase_pgbouncer pin — psycopg needs prepare_threshold=None)
* Network firewall on outbound to pooler port

Progress so far (commit-ready, just not pushed):

* /home/elliotbot/clawd/Agency_OS/scripts/litellm_boot_check.py — Gemini-first logic (Anthropic optional)
* /home/elliotbot/.config/litellm/config.yaml — Gemini 2.5 models
* /home/elliotbot/.config/agency-os/.env — GOOGLE_API_KEY alias added
* /home/elliotbot/.config/systemd/user/litellm.service.d/no-prisma.conf — DATABASE_URL override stripping +asyncpg
* prisma client generated in litellm-venv (prisma-client-py shebang fix + prisma generate run successfully)

**Acceptance:** systemctl --user is-active litellm = active (not activating); curl [http://127.0.0.1:4000/health](<http://127.0.0.1:4000/health>) returns 200.

**Phase:** 0.5 **Priority:** P1

title: Fix completion_sync_worker drive_manual sink — write_manual_mirror.py rejects --task-id flag
description:
67 rows stuck in completion_sync_queue at attempts=3 with: write_manual_mirror.py: error: unrecognized arguments: --task-id KEI-NNN

The worker code (src/orchestrator/completion_sync_worker.py or similar) passes --task-id <KEI> to write_manual_mirror.py but the script doesn't accept that flag.

Fix: either (a) add --task-id arg support to scripts/write_manual_mirror.py, or (b) remove the --task-id flag from the worker's drive_manual sink call.

After fix: reset attempts=0 on the 67 stuck drive_manual rows so they re-try.

**Acceptance:** completion_sync_queue rows with target_sink=drive_manual process successfully; queue depth drops below 5.

title: Slack indexer (KEI-109 sub-pipeline 3/3) — Slack messages → Weaviate via conversations.history
description:
Final sub-pipeline of KEI-109. ceo_memory_indexer (existing) + elliot_memories_indexer (PR #940 commit 6f0da690) shipped. Slack indexer remaining.

Aiden + Atlas concurred on option (a): direct Slack Web API conversations.history poll. Rationale: (i) mirrors git_commits cursor pattern (channel_id + ts cursor), (ii) no new Supabase schema = no migration coordination = ships in one PR, (iii) Slack-API rate limits are well-documented per-channel and the 429-Retry-After helper extracted via PR #937 can be reused.

Acceptance:
- scripts/orchestrator/slack_indexer.py reads conversations.history per relevant channel (#ceo, #execution, #completed_directives) using channel_id + ts cursor.
- Deterministic UUID via uuid5("slack", f"{channel_id}:{ts}").
- Filters bot/system messages if needed; agent messages get agent property derived from sender or message tag.
- Indexes into a Weaviate class (proposed: SlackMessages with 5 mandatory BaseIndexer ABC properties).
- infra/systemd/agents/slack-indexer.service + scripts/install_slack_indexer.sh + tests/scripts/test_slack_indexer.py.
- KEI-108 anti-false-complete gate satisfied.
- Live --once smoke: outcome with success > 0, failed = 0.
- Can use indexer_base.run_db_indexer pattern as parent OR a new run_http_indexer sibling — note in Agency_OS-qdo's review that 2 of 3 existing indexers (linear, git) are also non-psycopg-backed and may share this run_http_indexer helper.

Parent KEI-109. Atlas filed this split to release the KEI-109 claim for KEI-103 work. Unassigned — first-come-first-served per Dave standing order.
notes: Filed by atlas as split of KEI-109. Slack source decision: option (a) — direct conversations.history (Aiden + Atlas concur). SLACK_BOT_TOKEN is in env per slack_relay.py reference.

Without this, Linear doesn't reflect fleet execution state. The webhook handler on Railway already has the Linear API client — add an outbound call on tasks.status change.

Implement as either a Supabase cron function or a check in bd ready. Prevents abandoned claims from blocking the queue permanently.

title: KEI-87 follow-up — migrate ~11 ceo:* writer call-sites to ceo_memory_writer wrapper
description:
## Why

KEI-87 (PR #922 / commit ac0150ad) shipped the mechanical write-guard trigger for ceo_memory keys with `ceo:*` prefix. Migration is NOT auto-applied — Max's banner says: caller-migrate → apply → tighten. Without this caller migration, applying the trigger via `mcp__supabase__apply_migration` BREAKS all existing ceo:\* writers (they don't `SET LOCAL agency_os.callsign`).

## Audit (Aiden 2026-05-17 — deep-check complete)

*IN SCOPE — 11 ceo:* writer call-sites requiring wrapper migration:\*

 1. `scripts/gmb_pilot2.py:617` + `:620` — `key='ceo:directives'`
 2. `scripts/gmb_pilot2_resume.py:435` + `:438` — `key='ceo:directives'`
 3. `scripts/gmb_pilot2_retry.py:450` + `:453` — `key='ceo:directives'`
 4. `scripts/gmb_pilot3.py:796` + `:799` — `key='ceo:directives'`
 5. `scripts/gmb_pilot3_resume.py:607` + `:610` — `key='ceo:directives'`
 6. `scripts/gmb_pilot4.py:652` + `:655` + `:657` — `key='ceo:directives'`
 7. `scripts/gmb_process_snapshot_v2.py:277` + `:282` — `key='ceo:directives'`
 8. `scripts/gmb_process_snapshot.py:175` — `key='ceo:directive_225_complete'` (deep-check confirmed)
 9. `scripts/session_end_hook.py:197` — `key=f'ceo:session_end_<date>_<callsign>'`
10. `src/services/cis_outcome_service.py:384` — `key='ceo:propensity_weights_v3'` (deep-check confirmed)
11. `scripts/three_store_save.py:224` — `key=f'ceo:directive_{directive}_complete'` (deep-check confirmed)

*OUT OF SCOPE — trigger ignores (non-ceo:* prefix):\*

* `src/observability/heartbeat.py:130` — `key='heartbeat:<service>'`
* `scripts/orchestrator/completion_sync_worker.py:95` — `key=f'completion:{task_id}'` (deep-check confirmed)

Audit closed. 11 confirmed in-scope (matches Max's \~11 estimate from migration banner). 0 remaining uncertain.

## Spec

Migrate each `ceo:*` writer to `from src.governance.ceo_memory_writer import upsert_ceo_memory_key, update_ceo_memory_value` (canonical wrapper from KEI-87 / PR #922). The wrapper:

* accepts callsign explicitly (from `CALLSIGN` env or arg)
* runs `BEGIN; SET LOCAL agency_os.callsign = '<callsign>'; UPDATE/INSERT; COMMIT`
* handles psycopg connection with `prepare_threshold=None` (Supabase pgbouncer txn-mode)

For the gmb_pilot\* writers (all write `ceo:directives` last-number tracking), the wrapper call is:

```python
upsert_ceo_memory_key(os.environ.get('CALLSIGN', 'unknown'), 'ceo:directives', {...})
```

Note: wrapper currently does full value replacement. The gmb_pilot\* writers use `jsonb_set` to patch a single field — needs either a JSONB-patch helper added to the wrapper OR caller-side read-modify-write semantics.

three_store_save.py + cis_outcome_service.py use async (asyncpg / SQLAlchemy text). Wrapper currently uses sync psycopg — async caller-side either uses asyncio.to_thread OR an async wrapper variant needs to be added.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All 11 confirmed ceo:\* writer call-sites migrated to wrapper.
- [ ] gmb_pilot jsonb_set sites: either wrapper grows a JSONB-patch helper OR call-site does read-modify-write.
- [ ] Async call-sites (three_store_save, cis_outcome_service): either asyncio.to_thread bridge OR async wrapper variant.
- [ ] Each call-site passes correct callsign source (CALLSIGN env or hardcoded if context-bound).
- [ ] Tests updated/added: existing writer tests still pass; new tests assert wrapper invocation (mock psycopg).
- [ ] Negative test: synthetic UPDATE without SET LOCAL raises `psycopg.errors.CheckViolation` post-migration.
- [ ] KEI-108 anti-false-complete gate PASS (no new .service or webhook).
- [ ] After this PR merges + CI green, Max applies the KEI-87 trigger migration via `mcp__supabase__apply_migration`. Verbatim post-apply: synthetic UPDATE with `agency_os.callsign='aiden'` on ceo:\* key returns CheckViolation; same UPDATE with elliot succeeds.

## Sequencing

1. THIS KEI: migrate call-sites (this PR). Phase 0.5.
2. THEN: Max applies migration (Supabase MCP). Phase 0.5.
3. KEI-87 ships fully (trigger + caller compliance).

## Owner

Aiden (audit lead). Pair with Orion for the call-site rewriting if KEI-89 Gate 2 merge stalls; otherwise serial after KEI-89.

## Source

Aiden audit + deep-check during KEI-89 Gate 2 build downtime (2026-05-17). 3-way concur cleared on the audit propose; deep-check cleared by Max ts \~1779021850.

title: Cross-cutting ceo_memory write-guard (GOV-12 mechanical) — covers 400+ keys
description:
## What

Aiden's audit (during PR #917 review) found ALL 400+ rows in public.ceo_memory share the same write-guard hole: no RLS, no trigger, no code-level guard. Any callsign with DATABASE_URL can UPDATE any "supposedly CEO-only" key. The `ceo:` prefix is documentation-hint only.

This is a GOV-12 violation (Gates As Code, Not Comments). KEI-86 (Max's phase-lock) added READ enforcement but the WRITE side inherits the cross-cutting gap.

## Pattern-fix vs point-fix

Patching one key per PR (KEI-86 phase-lock, etc) is point-fix and leaves 400+ keys unguarded. Single cross-cutting guard closes the entire surface; subsequent PRs inherit it for free.

## Spec

Pick one of:

(a) Supabase RLS policy on public.ceo_memory restricting UPDATE/INSERT on keys matching prefix `ceo:` (or a configurable allowlist) to a specific role / app user.

(b) BEFORE UPDATE/INSERT trigger that checks `current_setting('agency_os.callsign', true)` matches a configurable allow-list (elliot, dave) and raises EXCEPTION otherwise. Trigger fires per row.

Either path: requires plumbing `SET LOCAL agency_os.callsign = 'elliot'` (or equivalent) into the writer code so the trigger sees the caller identity. That's the meaningful change — every existing writer (slack_relay, openclaw_auto_kei, etc) gets a small wrapper that sets the session var before issuing the UPDATE.

## Acceptance

- [ ] Migration adds RLS or trigger covering all `ceo:` prefix keys (or allowlist).
- [ ] Writer wrapper installed in all known ceo_memory call-sites (grep src/ scripts/ for `UPDATE public.ceo_memory` / `INSERT INTO public.ceo_memory` and audit).
- [ ] Negative-path test: synthetic UPDATE with `agency_os.callsign='aiden'` on a `ceo:*` key returns permission denied / EXCEPTION (paste verbatim).
- [ ] Positive-path test: same UPDATE with `agency_os.callsign='elliot'` succeeds (paste verbatim).
- [ ] KEI-108 anti-false-complete gate PASS.

## Why not point-fix on KEI-86

Per Max's reasoning + Aiden's audit: 400+ keys share the same gap. Patching only KEI-86's phase-lock is theatre — any caller can still mutate every other ceo: key. Cross-cutting fix is the structural answer.

## Source

Aiden's audit during PR #917 review, ratified by Max + Elliot. Surfaced to Dave alongside PR #917 option (c) sign-off request.

title: KEI-68 — Living governance layer: rules in Supabase, not static markdown
description:
## Problem

Governance lives in static markdown files — CLAUDE.md, GOVERNANCE.md, IDENTITY.md, Enforcer rules. When architecture changes, governance must be manually updated across 6 agent worktrees. It often doesn't catch up. Agents operate under stale rules.

Tonight alone, six governance changes were implied by architectural decisions:

* \[READY:callsign\] pattern deprecated → Enforcer rules watching for it need updating
* Beads replaced by Supabase → rules about `bd linear sync --pull` become obsolete
* Prime-clone pairings ratified → rules about who posts to #execution need updating
* Deliberation gate designed → `deliberation_required: true` classification needs governance
* bd complete hook shipped → polling fallback rules change
* tasks table live → claiming rules change

Each requires manually editing files across 6 worktrees. Each is a gap where agents operate under outdated rules until someone notices.

## Fix: Governance as structured data in Supabase

A `governance_rules` table — rules as rows, not markdown. Agents query on session start. Rules are always current.

```sql
CREATE TABLE governance_rules (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  category TEXT NOT NULL,  -- 'communication', 'claiming', 'concur', 'deliberation'
  rule TEXT NOT NULL,
  active BOOLEAN DEFAULT true,
  deprecated_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
  deprecated_reason TEXT,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
);
```

When architecture changes, rule changes in the same PR — not separately, not later. Deprecated rules are flagged with reason, not deleted. Agents query `WHERE active = true`. History is preserved.

## Enforcer evolution

Enforcer patterns come from the governance table, not hardcoded regex. When \[READY:callsign\] is deprecated, one UPDATE to the table. Enforcer regex updates automatically on next session start. No CLAUDE.md edits across 6 worktrees.

## Session start protocol update

CEO and agents query governance_rules on session start alongside tasks table. Always current. Never stale.

## Acceptance criteria

* governance_rules table created with initial ruleset migrated from [CLAUDE.md/GOVERNANCE.md](<http://CLAUDE.md/GOVERNANCE.md>)
* Agents query table on session start (not read markdown files)
* Enforcer patterns sourced from table
* When a rule changes: one SQL UPDATE, no markdown edits required
* Deprecated rules preserved with reason and timestamp

## Priority

High — not urgent. Ships after [KEI-22](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-22/kei-22-automated-linear-beads-bidirectional-sync) and [KEI-45](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-45/kei-45-event-driven-pull-model-supabase-realtime-subscriptions) complete. Governance stability matters but doesn't block the idle fix.

title: KEI-52 — Claude Code tool call log export: capture what agents actually did into Weaviate
description:
## What

At each session compaction or completion, export the Claude Code tool call log as a structured record and index it into Weaviate. Captures what the agent actually did — files read, commands run, errors encountered — without noise.

## Why

The tmux pane contains "Generating... thinking some more" noise. The tool call log inside Claude Code contains the signal: every file read, every bash command run, every error hit, every file written. This is what the agent actually experienced, not what they were thinking about.

## What gets captured

```
Tool call: Read file → /home/elliotbot/clawd/Agency_OS/src/cognee/client.py
Tool call: Bash → cat /proc/meminfo | grep MemAvailable
  Output: MemAvailable: 892044 kB
Tool call: Bash → ulimit -v 3145728 && python cognee_ingest.py
  Output: Error: MemoryError — virtual memory limit exceeded
Tool call: Bash → python cognee_ingest.py --cgroup-limit 3g
  Output: Stream 3 starting: 1289 chunks...
```

Not the thinking about what to try. The actual tries and their results.

## What gets indexed

Structured JSON per session:

```json
{
  "session_id": "max-2026-05-13-cognee",
  "agent": "max",
  "kei": "KEI-44",
  "duration_hours": 4.2,
  "files_read": ["src/cognee/client.py", "scripts/cognee_ingest.py"],
  "commands_run": [{"cmd": "ulimit -v...", "outcome": "failed"}, ...],
  "errors_encountered": ["MemoryError", "Lance concurrent write"],
  "files_written": ["scripts/run_cognee.sh"]
}
```

## Export mechanism

* Claude Code stores conversation history including all tool uses
* At compaction trigger: export tool calls from current context
* Parse into structured format above
* Index into Weaviate `sessions` collection

## What this enables

Agent B queries: "what commands were tried for the Cognee memory problem?"
Gets: every command Max ran, with outcomes. Knows instantly which approaches failed.

## Acceptance criteria

* Tool call log exported at every compaction and session end
* Structured JSON format consistently applied
* Indexed into Weaviate sessions collection
* Test query: "what bash commands were run for [KEI-44](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-44/kei-44-cognee-subprocess-3gb-memory-cap-cgroupulimit)?" returns Max's command history

## Dependencies

[KEI-46](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-46/kei-45-layer-3-idle-enforcement-daemon-systemd-polling-service-per) + [KEI-47](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-47/kei-migration-completeness-guard-regex-false-positive-on-update-from)

## Owner

Scout — after [KEI-47](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-47/kei-migration-completeness-guard-regex-false-positive-on-update-from) ships.

title: KEI — Migration Completeness Guard regex false-positive on "UPDATE FROM" prose
description:
## Problem

`.github/workflows/ci.yml` Migration Completeness Guard uses this regex on removed diff lines:

```
(INSERT[[:space:]]+INTO|UPDATE)[[:space:]]+[a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_.]*
```

`awk '{print $NF}'` then takes the last field, which means a prose pattern like `[UPDATE FROM {callsign}]` gets parsed as `UPDATE FROM` and the script tries to verify residual readers of a "table" called `FROM` — a false positive.

## Manifested in

PR #843 (Aiden, KEI-41 Phase 2 Telegram rename) — guard failed because a deleted f-string `[UPDATE FROM {callsign}]` got captured. No actual schema change in the PR.

## Fix options

1. Exclude SQL reserved words from valid target list (FROM, WHERE, SET, INTO, JOIN, AS, WHERE, etc).
2. Use a stricter pattern: require the target to look like a real table identifier (schema.table or just lowercase identifiers, exclude all-caps SQL keywords).
3. Run the check only on changes inside files known to contain SQL (migrations/, .sql files, anything with `sqlalchemy` or `asyncpg` imports).

## Acceptance criteria

* PR #843-equivalent case passes the guard.
* A real removed-INSERT-without-migration still fails.
* New unit test in `tests/scripts/test_check_migration_completeness.py` covering the prose false-positive case.

## Ownership

Open — Atlas or Orion when one of them clears their current PR. Not blocking PR #843 merge (Dave override path: confirm no schema change in the diff and merge with admin).

title: KEI-40 — slack_relay.py rate limit handling: exponential backoff + Retry-After respect
description:
## Problem

`slack_relay.py` is hitting Slack API rate limits during burst posting periods. Error: "Server is temporarily limiting requests (not your usage limit) · Rate limited."

Currently the relay has no rate limit handling — it fires requests immediately and fails silently or errors out when rate limited.

## Impact

Message delivery failures during peak governance activity (multiple agents posting simultaneously). Creates appearance of broken communication when underlying work is proceeding correctly.

## Fix

Add exponential backoff with retry to `slack_relay.py`:

```python
import time

def post_with_backoff(client, channel, message, max_retries=4):
    delay = 1
    for attempt in range(max_retries):
        response = client.chat_postMessage(channel=channel, text=message)
        if response.get("ok"):
            return response
        if response.get("error") == "ratelimited":
            retry_after = int(response.headers.get("Retry-After", delay))
            time.sleep(retry_after)
            delay *= 2
        else:
            raise Exception(response.get("error"))
    raise Exception("Max retries exceeded")
```

Also: respect `Retry-After` header from Slack when present.

## Acceptance criteria

* No silent message drops during burst posting
* Rate limit errors logged but retried automatically
* `Retry-After` header respected

## Owner

Aiden — post Stream 3+4 completion, before Elliot restart.
close_reason: PR #847 merged @ 5d926ef4. Triple-bot FINAL (Aiden + Max + Elliot). slack_relay.py exponential backoff + Retry-After respect live on main.

title: KEI-36 — Fix HEARTBEAT.md placeholder population in pre-compact hook
description:
## Problem

`scripts/pre_compact_alert.py` snapshots HEARTBEAT.md before context compaction. When agents fail to fill the template manually, the snapshot contains raw placeholders: `<directive number or label>`, `<one-line>`, `<git short-sha>`, etc. The anchor is useless for post-compact session recovery.

Observed in production: two consecutive PRE-COMPACT alerts today showed blank template fields across Elliot's session.

## Fix — Two parts

### Part 1: Auto-populate from mechanical sources

Before snapshotting, `pre_compact_alert.py` should detect placeholder text (grep for `<` followed by known field names) and auto-fill:

* **Last Good Commit SHA + Subject** → `git log -1 --format='%h %s' HEAD`
* **Branch** → `git branch --show-current`
* **Model (Configured)** → read callsign from IDENTITY.md, look up in CLAUDE.md model table
* **Active Task / Goal** → `bd ready` or most recent Linear In Progress issue for this agent
* **Blockers** → `bd list --blocked` for current worktree
* **Next Action** → derive from current branch name + last commit subject if no explicit entry

No LLM call required. Pure mechanical sources.

### Part 2: Validate before snapshot

After auto-fill attempt, scan for remaining placeholders. If any found:

* Post warning to #execution: `[HEARTBEAT-INCOMPLETE] callsign=X — N fields still contain placeholders`
* Still proceed with compaction (don't block) but flag clearly
* Never silently snapshot a blank template

## Acceptance criteria

* Pre-compact snapshot of any agent HEARTBEAT.md contains zero `<placeholder>` fields
* Warning posted to #execution when fields cannot be auto-resolved
* All 6 agent worktrees covered

## Owner

Aiden — extend `scripts/pre_compact_alert.py`. Branch off main. Standard dual-CTO concur before self-merge.

Same class as stream-crash + concurrency-bug + retry-decision protocol (Dave ts ~1778642820).

title: KEI-35 — Automatic agent session recovery (dead tmux detection + restart)
description:
## Problem

When an agent's tmux session dies (crash, OOM, detached process taking it down), the machine currently surfaces this to Dave as an operator action. This is wrong. It is an internal mechanical operation.

## Required behaviour

1. Polling loop detects dead agent session (tmux session missing or unresponsive)
2. Machine restarts the session automatically — no human involvement
3. Agent re-reads IDENTITY.md, HEARTBEAT.md, reconnects to Slack, resumes work
4. Activity log records: `[RECOVERY] Max session restarted at HH:MM — cause: tmux session lost`
5. Only surfaces to #ceo if two recovery attempts fail

## Beads memory pattern to encode

`agent_session_recovery = internal_mechanical`

Every Keiracom deployment inherits this behaviour. Dead sessions restart automatically. This is foundational to the machine being self-healing.

## Implementation

* Add tmux session liveness check to polling loop (alongside existing subprocess detection)
* If session missing: `tmux new-session -d -s [agent_name]` + reinject IDENTITY context
* If session exists but unresponsive: kill + restart
* Log to ceo_memory + activity feed
* Two-failure gate before #ceo escalation

## Why this matters for the Keiracom product

This is the core self-healing behaviour that makes Keiracom production-grade. Users of the bundle should never restart an agent manually.

title: KEI-24 — Polling loop peak-window timezone bug
description:
## Cancelled — superseded by Supabase Realtime ([KEI-45](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-45/kei-45-event-driven-pull-model-supabase-realtime-subscriptions))

The polling loop timezone bug only matters as long as polling is the primary mechanism for agent task notification. [KEI-45](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-45/kei-45-event-driven-pull-model-supabase-realtime-subscriptions) (Supabase Realtime subscriptions — event-driven pull model) replaces polling entirely. Agents subscribe to Supabase Realtime on session start and receive task events instantly. No polling loop. No timezone calculation. No bug to fix.

Superseded by [KEI-45](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-45/kei-45-event-driven-pull-model-supabase-realtime-subscriptions) (Supabase Realtime subscriptions + event-driven pull model).

Fix: use it.get('assignee') or {} pattern instead. Trivial 1-line. Scout's prior diagnosis missed this because bd_ready=0 short-circuited the Linear path before it could crash. Now manifesting.

title: Build: Durable hold-survival — watchdog queries ceo_memory on resume, injects active holds into wake prompt + HEARTBEAT.md
description: Fix for the failure mode where a Dave-posted hold vanishes at context-cycle compact/clear and the resumed agent acts as if no hold exists. Scope: (1) watchdog checks ceo_memory for any key matching ceo:hold:* on every resume cycle; (2) if a hold key has hold=true, its reason is injected into the wake prompt before the CONTEXT-CYCLE RESUME message is sent; (3) the same hold reason is written as a hard-block line into HEARTBEAT.md before /clear fires; (4) the compact state includes the active hold in a dedicated ACTIVE HOLDS section (not buried in heartbeat). Proof gate: with ceo:hold:phase1_battery_gate hold=true present, simulate a context clear (write compact state, /clear, inject wake). The resumed agent's first response must surface the hold before taking any action, and must not take any action that violates the hold. Verified by running the simulation and observing the output — not asserted from code inspection alone. Owner: Atlas. Target: 2026-06-10.

title: Warm pool — pre-spawned agent processes for near-instant chain dispatch latency
description:
## Context

Battery run 2026-05-30 shows current spawn_overhead (dispatch → first attribution write) at:

* Simple tasks: 22–25s
* Complex tasks: 50–58s

Cold-start breakdown per hop: Python process spawn + imports (\~5–10s) + fetch_persona (\~0.5s) + model inference (\~3–30s depending on task) + Gemini classify (\~1–2s) + DB write (\~0.1s).

The 50–58s complex-task figure includes inference time in the "overhead" number — the metric is wall-clock from dispatch to first DB write, not pure process spin-up.

## The lever

Pre-spawn a pool of idle agent processes on a timer/on-demand basis so that when a chain hop dispatches, a process is already warmed up (imports loaded, persona fetched, NATS connected). Dispatch goes from \~10s cold-start overhead to <1s.

## Scope (deferred — NOT built now)

* Design: size the pool per callsign (aiden, max, nova, orion, atlas), idle TTL, replenishment trigger
* Implement: dispatcher manages pool; `/dispatcher/spawn` checks pool before cold-spawning
* Gate: only worthwhile once latency matters at the customer phase

## Why deferred

Current single-user trial accepts 60s–3.5min chain latency (Dave confirmed). The lever is real but premature.

## Ratified by

Dave directive 2026-05-30 — filed as future latency lever, not built now.

title: [ATLAS] Path A nd3b inertness — chain hops via per-callsign claude session bypass nd3b suppression
description: Per Orion bd Agency_OS-0bgt research + Atlas PR #1343: scripts/dispatcher/_spawn.py spawns claude directly (per-callsign long-lived session), NOT agent_cold_start. nd3b notify-suppression (PR #1337) lives in agent_cold_start.run() reading CHAIN_STEP/AGENT_CHAIN_STEP — that function is not invoked on this path. 0bgt's env-injection lands AGENT_CHAIN_STEP in claude's env but no consumer reads it. So V1 chain hops via NATS→bridge→Path A still spam #ceo on intermediate steps. Options: (a) refactor _spawn.py to invoke agent_cold_start (changes per-callsign session model — significant), OR (b) persona-level CHAIN_STEP awareness in the per-callsign claude sessions (live agents check env + self-suppress). Architecture call needed before pick. Discovered-from Agency_OS-0bgt.

title: v1_chain_orchestrator: migrate JSON state file to Postgres chain_runs table (TOCTOU fix)
description: Current v1_chain_orchestrator.py uses a JSON state file (atomic write via tempfile+os.replace). This prevents partial-write corruption but has a TOCTOU window: advance_step publishes NATS then crashes before _save_state → state not updated → retry re-dispatches the same step (double-dispatch). Postgres with a transaction around 'INSERT INTO chain_runs + NATS publish' would make advance_step atomic. Schema: chain_runs(chain_id PK, task_id, brief, current_step, steps_done JSONB, atom_ids JSONB, pending JSONB, started_ts, updated_ts). advance_step becomes a single Postgres transaction: UPDATE chain_runs SET steps_done=..., current_step=... WHERE chain_id=? AND completed_step NOT IN steps_done (idempotent via WHERE) + after commit, publish NATS. Gates on V1 running end-to-end (jb4e). Filed per Max review of PR #1329.

title: work-loop: reconcile() re-enqueue gap — crashed task slot freed but task not re-enqueued for retry
description: Scout avii 2026-05-29: reconcile() frees the crashed-agent slot but does NOT re-enqueue the crashed task. _spawn_with_attempts handles retries through a separate path. These are two distinct legs. The spec phrases crash→reconcile→retry as one causal chain; the code implements them separately. Design question: should reconcile() also re-enqueue the crashed task so the retry counter increments? Currently a crashed task can have its slot freed without entering the retry path. Follow-up architectural decision needed.

comments.author: Aiden
comments.text:
[MAX] Code-quality + tracing analysis — CONCUR with two-leg design (gap was pre-#1310, now fixed).

──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
FINDING: gap existed, is now closed. Two-leg separation is CORRECT by design.
──────────────────────────────────────────────────────

**What happens to a crashed task's message today (post-PR #1310, main):**

reconcile() now does three things per expired lease:
  1. raw = r.get(_task_raw_key(tenant_id, task_id))   ← reads raw before slot-free
  2. release_slot()                                    ← frees active counter, pops overflow
  3. if raw: _handle_crashed_task(task_id, raw)        ← re-enqueue OR dead-letter

_handle_crashed_task():
  - increments keiracom:tasks:crash_attempts:{task_id}  (24h TTL)
  - below max_attempts: r.publish(TASKS_CHANNEL, raw)  ← back in queue
  - at max_attempts: _dead_letter() + notify Dave #ceo

So the message is NOT lost. It re-enters via TASKS_CHANNEL publish and goes through process_task → _spawn_with_attempts on next delivery.

**Pre-PR #1310 state (what Scout correctly flagged):**

reconcile() only called release_slot(). No task_raw key existed. Crashed tasks were permanently lost without exhausting retry attempts. That WAS a real gap. PR #1310 closed it.

**Why two-leg separation is the correct design:**

Leg 1 (release_slot): slot management — frees the active counter, drops the lease key, unblocks the overflow queue. Pure resource housekeeping.
Leg 2 (_handle_crashed_task): retry policy — decides re-enqueue vs dead-letter based on crash_attempts. Policy, not housekeeping.

Mixing them would couple slot management to retry policy. If retry logic ever changes (exponential backoff, different per-tier limits), you'd need to touch slot management code. Separation is the right call.

**One counter nuance Aiden's zr7e.2 design should know:**

crash_attempts (keiracom:tasks:crash_attempts:{task_id}, 24h TTL) is SEPARATE from spawn_attempts (keiracom:tasks:attempts:{task_id}, 3600s TTL). When a crashed task is re-enqueued:
  - _spawn_with_attempts starts fresh (original spawn succeeded → counter was deleted)
  - Each crash-recovery cycle gets max_attempts fresh spawn attempts

For the V1 chain (Face→Aiden→Max→Worker): the work-loop consumer is task-level, NOT chain-level. If a Worker task crashes and is re-enqueued, the consumer re-enters at the Worker task — it does NOT restart from Face. The chain orchestrator (zr7e.2) operates above this layer. One design constraint for zr7e.2: it must not treat a stale `active` tasks row as genuinely in-progress when the worker-loop lease has expired — the task may already be back in the queue. The tasks.status column (active/available/done/blocked) needs to stay consistent with the Valkey lease state. Current agent_cold_start.py writes task status transitions; the chain orchestrator should not assume active = running without checking lease freshness.

RECOMMENDATION: no code change needed for the crash-retry mechanism. Two-leg design is sound. zr7e.2 should treat the task layer as a black box for retries and design the chain-level handoff (NATS atom_id pointer) so that a re-enqueued Worker task can reconstruct its context from Hindsight L2 recall rather than from in-memory chain state.

title: [ATLAS] fix openai-cost daily+weekly: tg not on systemd PATH crashes rollup
description: openai-cost-daily.service FAILED (exit 1): openai_cost_rollup.py:96 (and openai_cost_weekly.py:127) call subprocess.run(['tg',...]) but tg (~/.local/bin/tg) is NOT on the systemd --user PATH → FileNotFoundError; check=False does not catch a missing binary → whole rollup crashes. Fix: resolve tg via shutil.which() or ~/.local/bin/tg fallback + fail-open try/except OSError. Verified: daily script exits 0 with fix. Surfaced during ja8d deploy.
close_reason: FIXED + verified live (PR #1323 merged + deployed 2026-05-29). git pull main → fix in deployed script. Before: failed/failed. systemctl --user start → rc=0; reset-failed → rc=0. After: 'Active: inactive (dead)', 'Main PID 151273 (code=exited, status=0/SUCCESS)'. Journal: '14:16:10 → [ELLIOT] sent to Slack #C0B3QB0K1GQ' (tg resolved + notification posted as elliot, NO FileNotFoundError) → 'Finished openai-cost-daily.service'. Root cause: tg (~/.local/bin/tg) not on systemd --user PATH; check=False doesn't catch missing binary. Fix: shutil.which + ~/.local/bin/tg fallback + fail-open. openai_cost_weekly.py same bug fixed in same PR. Discovery logged (systemd PATH gotcha).

title: V1 chain: cost_usd not returned to Slack (V1 goal: result + cost)
description: V1 goal is 'result + cost back to Slack' (ceo:v1_chain_architecture: cost_visibility). TaskCompleteRequest has no cost_usd field. The task_complete Slack message shows status and rc but no cost. ceo_memory notes this as a TODO pending Anthropic API billing hook availability. The Anthropic SDK does track usage in response.usage (input_tokens + output_tokens) — cost can be estimated from token counts * model pricing even before a formal billing API exists. This blocks the demo goal of making cost visible to Dave on every chain run.

title: CI flakiness — backend suite doesn't reliably re-trigger on rebase/force-push (partial check sets); risks merging unverified
description: Recurring (#1276, #1282): after a rebase --force-with-lease, the backend CI jobs (Pytest/Ruff/MyPy) sometimes don't re-trigger — only Vercel checks appear — leaving the PR without a fresh green on the actual commit. Mitigated case-by-case by verifying the diff is identical to a prior-green state, but that's fragile. Investigate the workflow triggers (on: pull_request synchronize? concurrency cancel? path filters?) so rebased/stacked PRs always get a full re-run. Related to bwla (KEI-108 no-merge-base). Low-priority but real cutover-hygiene.

title: Graphiti doctrine graph — temporal knowledge graph in retrieval orchestrator
description:
Dave ratified Option A 2026-05-28: add Graphiti temporal knowledge graph alongside Weaviate in the retrieval orchestrator.

Architecture position: Agents → Dispatcher → Retrieval Orchestrator (Weaviate + Graphiti + reranking + contradiction filtering + compression) → Spawn hydration.

What Graphiti adds: graph traversal for doctrine/relationship queries — 'what decisions depend on X?', 'what doctrine constrains this task?' Complements Weaviate semantic recall (similarity) with relational traversal.

Library: Graphiti by Zep — temporal knowledge graph built from conversations and decisions.

Does NOT block cutover. Add after: Weaviate recall + reranking + contradiction filtering + compression are running in production.

title: Hindsight mirror writes time out under indexer backlog — 30s client timeout vs slow synchronous embedding
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-28 after the shm_size fix (Agency_OS-mzpp) cleared the HTTP 500. With shm fixed, the async:false write path works (direct probe 200 in ~6s; fleet_decisions grew 2->3), but under the indexer backlog (selected:200/batch) some hindsight_mirror writes still hit the 30s client timeout (HINDSIGHT_TIMEOUT=30 in scripts/orchestrator/indexer_base.py).

Cause: the mirror posts async:false, so Hindsight does inline OpenAI embedding/extraction per item; large rows + concurrent indexers exceed 30s. Fail-open (Weaviate OK, mirror skipped, WARN-not-fatal), so non-blocking, but it slows convergence of the Hindsight corpus.

Options:
- Switch the mirror POST to async:true (queue; let Hindsight process in background) — fastest API return, but must verify background processing actually lands + add a verification/retry.
- Raise HINDSIGHT_TIMEOUT (e.g. 60-90s) for the mirror path.
- Throttle indexer batch size / add small inter-write delay to reduce concurrent embedding load.

Acceptance: over a full backlog drain, hindsight_mirror WARN-timeout rate ~0; bank counts converge toward Weaviate class counts.

LABELS: infra, P2, corpus-health. Parent: Agency_OS-mzpp.

title: Pin Hindsight image tag before cutover window (currently :latest + pull_policy:always)
description: docker-compose for Hindsight uses :latest with pull_policy:always — a service restart silently adopts v0.7.0+ and could invalidate the hybrid recall evidence gate. Pin to a specific SHA or version tag before the cutover window closes. Non-blocking now, blocker before cutover.

title: Golden query set + eval harness for FlashRank vs sidecar A/B baseline (precision@5)

Measures precision@5 for FlashRank vs cross-encoder reranker sidecar. Required for defensible ship signal on Wave 3 swap. Prerequisite: flashrank installed in venv + real corpus available. title: Phase B11 — Composio white-label proxy (simplified per Dave 2026-05-26)
description:
Per KEI-PHASE-B11-COMPOSIO-WHITELABEL Dave directive 2026-05-26 ~1779754450. QUEUED — sequenced after PR #1155 (A6 first-workflow) + PR #1156 (A7 cache design) admin-merge. Atlas A3-c2 remains primary.

Simplified scope per Dave accepts OAuth screen leak (Composio appearing on consent screen = legitimate-B2B-middleware trust signal, not brand failure):

IN:
  1. Upload Keiracom logo + app title to Composio dashboard Project Settings → Auth Screen (Dave handles OR me if shared credentials)
  2. Build redirect proxy at keiracom.com/api/composio-redirect that 302s to backend.composio.dev/api/v3.1/toolkits/auth/callback with query params forwarded. ~10 lines Next.js OR FastAPI
  3. Pass callbackUrl parameter to all session.authorize() calls — customers land on keiracom.com after auth
  4. Update Composio auth configs to use the custom redirect URI

OUT (Dave verbatim too-complex / accepted-leak):
  - Own OAuth apps registration (Google/GitHub/Slack/Stripe) — 4-8wk Google verification, rejected pre-revenue
  - Removing Secured by Composio badge — requires own OAuth apps
  - Removing Composio wants to access Gmail from consent screen — requires own OAuth apps

Cost: FREE plan supports white-label per Composio FAQ. No upgrade needed.
Effort: half-day.
Revisit trigger (V1.x candidate): multiple customers asking what is Composio OR sales objection → escalate to own-OAuth-apps work.

Canonical-key gate (audit-dispatch checklist):
  ceo:dave_decision_phase_b11_composio_whitelabel
  ceo:keiracom_architecture_v2_locked Cat 11 gov.composio_per_customer_segregation
  ceo:keiracom_architecture_v2_locked Cat 13 cust.multi_channel_communication

Deliberators: Aiden + Max (per orchestrator-merge-after-NATS-concur pattern).

title: Temporal contract V2 amendment 3 — cache-hit short-circuit in pre-call sequencing
description: Aiden Phase 2 amendment 3 from PR #1152 contract V1. Current sequence fires token_gate AFTER cache_check; on cache hit the token_gate work is wasteful (no LLM call). New sequence: tier_gate -> content_check -> cache_check (if HIT: SHORT-CIRCUIT emit cache-hit-pass + return cached response; if MISS: continue) -> token_gate (only on cache MISS) -> LLM call -> listener+audit -> async post_validation. Cleaner cost-flow + better audit signal. Engineer-tier wiring during gate pipeline build after Phase 2 deliberation concur on the new ordering.

title: Temporal contract V2 amendment 2 — Sandbox token_gate calibration (warn-only -> enforce-with-cap)
description: Aiden Phase 2 amendment 2 from PR #1152 contract V1. Current warn-only on Sandbox tier is unbounded fleet-cost risk (10K tokens/task * 10 tasks/day * N tenants). Move to: ENFORCE with generous-cap (e.g. 50K tokens/day total per Sandbox tenant); WARN below 50% of cap (visibility into paid-tier behaviour); ENFORCE at 100%. Calibration tune in Phase 2 with actual Sandbox usage data. Communicate cap honestly in Sandbox onboarding flow (Cat 19 ux.surface.onboarding_wizard). Depends on metering pipeline P1 follow-up (PR #1137 production wiring).

title: Temporal contract V2 amendment 1 — audit schema extension for V1.x regulated verticals
description: Aiden Phase 2 amendment 1 from PR #1152 contract V1. Add to common audit event schema: session_id / chat_thread_id (multi-call correlation, HIPAA episode tracking), event_id + trace_id (distributed-trace correlation Temporal -> mem.wrap.trace -> Layer 12), signature / audit_hmac (tamper-resistance via Vault Transit HMAC; pairs with Atlas Go Sidecar NIT-7 HMAC-signed-config), explicit phi_filtered:bool flag on detail field (HIPAA broader than secrets/payload). Engineer-tier wiring after Phase 2 schema deliberation concur.

description: Phase A2 V1 (bd Agency_OS-31bk) ships Vault with Shamir-shares-on-disk unseal posture (Dave-delegated operational decision 2026-05-25). title: Fix Orion next_work_prompter bare-tag loop (9 occurrences this session)
description:
Aiden reported 9 bare [ORION] tags via NATS this session — each one publishes '[ORION]' with no payload, no action. Pattern is the next_work_prompter loop-nudge bug: Orion's auto-prompter publishes a wake/nudge to Aiden's inbox but with empty content.

Impact:
- Pollutes Aiden's inbox; Aiden correctly holds per role-lock (no payload = no work to take)
- Adds noise across NATS substrate
- Wastes API budget on Aiden's empty-message processing

Scope:
1. Locate next_work_prompter implementation (likely scripts/ in Orion worktree or fleet-supervisor)
2. Identify trigger condition that fires bare-tag publish
3. Either fix at source (don't publish empty payloads) OR add filter at inbox-watcher (reject zero-content [ORION] messages)
4. Verify fix by observing Aiden inbox for 1hr — expect zero bare tags

Anchor: Aiden NATS reports 2026-05-25 between ~12:15Z and ~13:45Z (9 occurrences across that window).

Owner: TBD; engineer-tier work. Not blocking V2.0 build but worth cleaning up before Phase A dispatches add more inter-agent traffic.

title: Hindsight per-tenant export endpoint (backup-DR prerequisite)
description:
Phase 2 sub-deliberation item surfaced by Aiden in Viktor-7 delta review of V2.0 architecture inventory infra.backup_dr row. HARD GATE prerequisite for V1 launch backup-DR validation.

UPSTREAM INVESTIGATION (empirical, 2026-05-25):

Inspected vectorize-io/hindsight (14.5k stars, MIT, default main). Verified the full per-tenant operation surface via hindsight-cli/src/api.rs which is the authoritative HTTP client wrapping hindsight-api routes:

  Available primitives (25+ methods): list_agents, get_profile, get_stats, recall, reflect, retain, file_retain, list_memories, list_documents, list_entities, get_memory, get_entity, regenerate_entity, get_graph, create_bank, update_bank, delete_bank, update_agent_name, set_mission, add_background, poll_operation, delete_memory, clear_memories, delete_document, list_operations, cancel_operation

  ZERO export-equivalent: no export_bank, no backup_bank, no dump_bank, no export endpoint at any HTTP path. Verified hindsight-cli/src/commands/{bank,audit}.rs surface bank=list/disposition/stats and audit=list/stats only.

VERDICT: upstream does NOT support per-tenant filtered export. Option 3 from dispatch applies (large work) OR a composition middle path (smaller, avoids fork-maintenance cost per ceo:memory_abstraction_layer_v1 position 7 risk acceptance).

TWO IMPLEMENTATION PATHS — choose during build (deliberator concur):

  PATH A — COMPOSITION (recommended V1, ~300-500 LoC):
    Build src/keiracom_system/backup/per_tenant_export.py that calls existing read primitives per bank:
      list_memories(bank_id) -> paginated dump
      list_documents(bank_id) -> paginated dump
      list_entities(bank_id) -> paginated dump
      get_graph(bank_id) -> graph state snapshot
      get_profile + get_stats -> metadata
    Serialise to JSONL stream; encrypt with tenant BYOK envelope per infra.secrets_management.
    Restore via clear_memories + retain (batched) + file_retain for documents.
    NO upstream changes. Zero fork-maintenance cost. Mirrors PR #1127 Path 3 strategy.
    RISK: round-trip fidelity depends on whether list_* + retain primitives are lossless (preserve all metadata, embedding vectors, entity-relation edges). Must verify in scoping spike.

  PATH B — UPSTREAM FORK (durable but high-cost):
    Add export_bank(bank_id) -> stream + import_bank(bank_id, stream) to hindsight-api + CLI surface.
    Upstream PR (~150-250 LoC + 1-4wk review cycle); if upstream rejects, fork + delta-maintain.
    Fork-maintenance cost flags risk position 7 of canonical key.

EXPORT SHAPE (per dispatch req 4):
  - JSON Lines (one record per line) — chosen for streamability + grep-ability + easy reconstruction
  - One file per bank (named <bank_id>.jsonl)
  - Bundle: tar.gz of per-bank JSONLs + manifest.json (tenant_id, bank_ids, schema_version, sha256-per-file, encryption envelope reference)
  - Postgres dump format NOT chosen — too engine-specific, locks future engine swap

ENCRYPTION (per dispatch req 5):
  - Per-tenant export encrypted with that tenants BYOK envelope key
  - Cross-references infra.secrets_management; uses existing keys_service pattern from Agency_OS-era code (pgcrypto.pgp_sym_decrypt)
  - Envelope reference (not the key) stored in manifest.json so future restore can resolve via tenant.control_plane lookup

DEPENDENCY EDGES:
  blocks: infra.backup_dr (LOOSE-BLOCKER HARD GATE for V1 launch per inventory Cat 1)
  blocked_by: Agency_OS-isil (Hindsight wrapper layer — needs to land first to provide bank-id resolution interface)
  cross-ref: tenant.control_plane (Atlas PR #1131 keiracom_tenants — bank_id to tenant_id mapping)
  cross-ref: infra.secrets_management (BYOK envelope key resolution)
  cross-ref: ceo:memory_abstraction_layer_v1 eleven_agreed_positions position 7 (fork-maintenance risk if Path B chosen)
design: Phase 2 sub-deliberation item from V2.0 inventory infra.backup_dr; HARD GATE prerequisite for V1 launch. Investigation-first scoping complete (2026-05-25, this bd); empirical upstream-API finding: NO export endpoint exists in vectorize-io/hindsight upstream. Two implementation paths scoped (composition vs fork); Path A composition recommended for V1 per PR #1127 Path 3 precedent (avoids fork-maintenance cost flagged at ceo:memory_abstraction_layer_v1 position 7). Path B fork preserved as fallback if composition round-trip fidelity verification fails. Round-trip acceptance criteria covers data identity + cross-tenant isolation + encryption fail-closed. Export shape: JSONL per bank + tar.gz bundle + manifest.json with sha256 + encryption envelope ref. Build dispatched only after deliberator concur on path choice + composition fidelity spike result.
acceptance_criteria:
ROUND-TRIP + MULTI-FACET (9 criteria, Max-concur 2026-05-25 with refinements):

#1 ROUND-TRIP FIDELITY (original)
  setup -> ingest 50+ memories + 5+ documents + 10+ entities -> export -> wipe (delete_bank + create_bank) -> restore -> verify identical:
    memory.content + document.content + entity.label fields exact
    embedding vectors within 1e-6 floating-point tolerance (BGE-small 384-dim)

#2 CROSS-TENANT NON-LEAK (original)
  ingest distinct fixture in T2 -> export T1 -> grep T1 bundle for T2 fixture markers -> 0 matches required

#3 ENCRYPTION FAIL-CLOSED (original)
  bundle unreadable without tenant T1 BYOK envelope key; decrypt with wrong-tenant key fails closed (no silent partial reveal)

#4 ATOMICITY AT EXPORT (Aiden + Max refinement)
  Postgres REPEATABLE READ snapshot floor (SERIALIZABLE acceptable but overkill). Document T_start in snapshot manifest.json. Concurrent writes during export must not corrupt snapshot — verified by spawning N=10 concurrent retain calls during export, asserting export contents reflect pre-T_start state only.

#5 ATOMICITY AT RESTORE (Aiden + Max refinement)
  STAGE-AND-SWAP pattern preferred over target-clear-before-restore. Import to bank_id_staging -> verify -> atomic rename (Postgres ALTER RENAME transactional at catalog). Mid-flight failure leaves prior bank contents intact (no partial overwrite); staging bank cleaned up by next bring-up cycle or explicit GC.

#6 CARA SIDE-EFFECT PRESERVATION (Aiden + Max refinement)
  Confidence weights round-trip: epsilon=1e-9 conservative floor OR base64 IEEE 754 bit-exact preferred. If using re-derivation path: document N <= 100 operations reconvergence bound + assert measured N empirically.

#7 PERFORMANCE BENCHMARK MULTI-POINT (Aiden + Max refinement)
  Three corpus sizes: 100K + 500K + 1M nodes. Wall-clock export <30min @ 100K, <2.5hr @ 500K, <5hr @ 1M (linear scaling target). >20% deviation from linear = architecture concern (flags need for batch-pagination redesign). Restore SLO 2x export time.

#8 CROSS-TENANT 403 ATTEST (Max addition)
  Tenant B attempting to import tenant A export must receive HTTP 403 (NOT 404 — 404 leaks existence of tenant A). Verified via curl with wrong-tenant API key.

#9 PERMISSIONERROR BEFORE RETAIN (Max addition)
  KeiracomTenantExtension.authenticate() raises PermissionError BEFORE retain hits Hindsight on a cross-tenant attempt. Hindsight engine must never see a cross-tenant write attempt at the data layer. Verified by network trace / Hindsight log absence of the cross-tenant payload.

V1-READINESS GAPS (G1-G5, Max review — MUST land before V1 promote; NOT blocking spike):
  G1 idempotency — re-running export of same bank produces identical bundle (manifest sha256 matches)
  G2 resource cleanup — temp files + staging banks GC after spike or post-failure
  G3 snapshot integrity verification — manifest sha256 verified pre-restore
  G4 restore verification — post-restore primitive counts asserted before declaring success
  G5 audit log emission — export + restore emit structured audit events for compliance

title: GitHub Action: nats-concur-recorded CI required-check enforcing orchestrator-merge pattern
description:
Aiden 5(d) + Max (b) follow-up to PR #1116 (orchestrator-merge-after-NATS-concur pattern naming). Aiden architecture-lens 2026-05-24: missing from PR #1116 prose, file as follow-up.

Goal: convert the orchestrator-merge-after-NATS-concur pattern from convention to code-enforced gate (GOV-12 — gates as code not comments).

What it does: a GitHub Action runs on every PR that scans PR comments for [REVIEW:approve:<callsign>] tags from N distinct callsigns (2 by default, or 2-of-3 with author-exclusion logic). Posts a required-check status: pass if 2-of-N native concurs present + author-exclusion respected; fail otherwise. Branch protection adds this check as required → merge blocked until pattern is satisfied.

Tasks:
1. Write the GitHub Action workflow (.github/workflows/nats-concur-recorded.yml). Scans PR comments via gh API. Parses [REVIEW:approve:<callsign>] tags. Counts distinct callsigns. Detects author callsign via PR commit-message [<CALLSIGN>] prefix. Applies author-exclusion math.
2. Post a check-run named 'nats-concur-recorded' with pass/fail conclusion.
3. Update branch protection: add 'nats-concur-recorded' as a required status check.
4. Optional: support multiple approve-tag formats ([REVIEW:approve:elliot], [CONCUR:elliot], etc.) for legacy compatibility.
5. Negative-path test: PR with only 1 concur fails; PR with author concurring own PR fails; PR with 2 concurs from non-author callsigns passes.

Replaces current 'I trust the orchestrator to count NATS concurs correctly' convention with native GitHub-checked gate. Critical IF/WHEN per-callsign GitHub bot accounts (Agency_OS-57tp) are not yet provisioned — this check enforces the count via the existing shared-bot signed PR comments.

Priority: P2 — important but not blocking. Sequenced after Phase 1.2.5 + Phase 2.0 + Phase 2.1.

title: supervisor-wake: document 'supervisor-wake' as recognised system-origin sender
description:
Aiden architecture-lens N1 follow-up to Agency_OS-Phase-0 supervisor-wake PR (commit 8e5e9c7cc).

Wake messages now arrive in elliot's pane every 5min tagged `[supervisor-wake@nats:elliot.inbox] [SUPERVISE-WAKE] ...`. The 'supervisor-wake' from-field is a system-origin namespace, not a callsign. Anyone reading the tmux pane or auditing the session needs to recognise it as a system heartbeat rather than a peer dispatch.

Two improvements:
(a) Append to elliot IDENTITY.md / canonical runbook Substrate section: recognised system-origin senders include 'supervisor-wake' (5-min fleet supervision heartbeat). One-line entry.
(b) Optional forward-looking: add 'origin_class': 'system_heartbeat' field to wake envelope so downstream consumers can filter system heartbeats without string-matching the from field.

Scope: small. Identity/runbook edit (governance-tier) + envelope-schema addition. Not blocking on anything; lands incrementally.

title: install_worktree_identity.sh awk extraction bug — stops at first close fence, breaks on canonical blocks with inner bash fences
description: Discovered by Orion 2026-05-24 during Agency_OS-e02v (runbook coverage matrix). The install_worktree_identity.sh script uses awk to extract the canonical ```markdown ... ``` block from each runbook. Existing awk pattern stops at the FIRST close fence encountered, which means canonical blocks containing inner ```bash code blocks get truncated on install. Orion preempted in e02v by inlining bd commands (no inner fences) but the script remains broken for the general case. Fix: extend awk to count fence levels — only stop on the close fence at level 0. Pattern sketch: awk '/^```markdown$/{depth++;next} /^```/{if(depth==1){depth=0;exit}; if(depth>1) depth--} depth>=1'. Engineer-tier work, decoupled, ~30min. Test with synthetic runbook that has inner bash fences.

title: Nova agent-keepalive systemd sd_notify NotifyAccess violation — Type=notify rejects child notifications
description: Discovered by Orion 2026-05-24 during Agency_OS-z3wn nova worktree conflict resolution. After resolving the merge conflicts and fixing the git pull rebase issue (status=128 → status=0/SUCCESS), nova-agent.service restart exposed a separate underlying bug: ExecStart of agent_keepalive.sh fails with sd_notify NotifyAccess violation. Root cause: the systemd unit declares Type=notify but agent_keepalive.sh spawns child processes that try to call sd_notify. Type=notify only accepts notifications from the main PID — child-process notifications are rejected. Fix options (per Orion's diagnostic): (a) change NotifyAccess=all on the unit (allow child-process notifications), or (b) switch Type=simple (drops the notify-based readiness contract entirely; service goes active on ExecStart spawn). Recommended: NotifyAccess=all keeps the readiness contract; Type=simple loses observability of when nova is actually ready. Engineer-tier fix, no deliberator review needed for the systemd unit change. Worktree: /home/elliotbot/clawd/Agency_OS-nova. Service file likely at ~/.config/systemd/user/nova-agent.service. Service currently stopped clean (not restart-looping). tmux nova session alive but no claude inside. Discovered while Orion was working Agency_OS-z3wn; that issue's close is blocked separately by Agency_OS-0kjh (tasks_cli dual-backend bug). Priority P2 — nova is held until this lands but other agents are unaffected.
