title: Local outbox-to-NATS drain daemon (per-callsign, fail-open)
description:
Carved from Agency_OS-1zkd 2026-05-23: Part 1 (4 JetStream streams: elliot_inbox/dispatch/review/audit + 6 systemd bridges) shipped 2026-05-19 by Elliot (commits 91ccc5063 + 31a0c8f7b). Part 2 (local outbox drain daemon) genuinely missing — existing nats_to_inbox_bridge.py + nats_tmux_bridge.py are NATS→inbox direction only.

SCOPE — one daemon per callsign (systemd template instance, --callsign arg):
  - Watch /tmp/telegram-relay-<callsign>/outbox/ via inotify (polling fallback every 2s).
  - Parse each JSON envelope, derive destination NATS subject (see routing below), publish, move file to outbox/processed/ on success.
  - Fail-open: if NATS unavailable OR publish fails, leave file in outbox/ and retry on next cycle. Never lose a message. Backoff on repeated failures (cap 30s).
  - Malformed JSON: move to outbox/malformed/ + log, daemon continues.

ROUTING (subject derived from envelope.kind + envelope.to + envelope.task_ref):
  - kind ∈ {dispatch, task_dispatch}: publish to keiracom.dispatch.<to>
  - kind ∈ {review_hold, review_approve, concur} AND task_ref matches /^PR-(\d+)$/: publish to keiracom.review.$1
  - kind == audit OR envelope.subject contains 'AUDIT': publish to keiracom.audit
  - to == 'elliot': publish to keiracom.elliot.inbox
  - Default: publish to keiracom.<to>.inbox

OUT OF SCOPE (do NOT bundle per feedback_split_orthogonal_scope):
  - Audit stream subscription configuration (handled at stream-config level, not daemon).
  - Migrating existing direct-inbox-write call sites in scripts to use outbox/ instead (separate follow-up KEI).
  - HMAC signing (existing scripts/sign_dispatch.py covers signed envelopes; daemon publishes verbatim).

FILES:
  - scripts/orchestrator/local_outbox_drain.py (new, ~150 LoC ceiling per LAW V)
  - infra/systemd/agents/outbox-drain@.service (templated instance unit)
  - tests/orchestrator/test_local_outbox_drain.py

ACCEPTANCE TESTS (negative-path per feedback_negative_path_test_before_approve):
  - happy path: valid envelope → published to correct subject, file moved to outbox/processed/ within 5s.
  - NATS unavailable (server down): file stays in outbox/, no exception raised.
  - reconnect: after NATS comes back, queued file gets published + moved on next cycle.
  - malformed JSON: moved to outbox/malformed/, daemon continues servicing valid files.
  - routing — kind=dispatch + to=atlas → keiracom.dispatch.atlas (not keiracom.atlas.inbox).
  - routing — kind=review_hold + task_ref=PR-1113 → keiracom.review.1113.
  - routing — kind=task_response + to=aiden → keiracom.aiden.inbox (default).
  - idempotency: same file processed twice → only one publish (use file inode + processed/ dir as dedup; or fsync before move).

DUAL-CONCUR PATH:
  - Author: orion (assignee). Reviewers: aiden + max (author-exclusion does NOT apply — I'm KEI author not PR author).
  - Tag PR title KEI-1zkd-pt2 or new bd id when assigned.

title: next_work_prompter loop-nudges PR author when PR is reviewer-blocked, not author-blocked
description:
GAP (observed 2026-05-20): scripts/orchestrator/next_work_prompter.py emits [NEXT-WORK:<cs>] 'FIX your N blocked PRs first: [NNNN] — fetch CI/reviewer comments, push fix-up' to a PR's AUTHOR whenever the PR is open and not merged. It does not distinguish:
 - AUTHOR-blocked: CI red, or a reviewer left CHANGES_REQUESTED / a [REVIEW:HOLD] the author has not yet addressed — author genuinely must act.
 - REVIEWER-blocked: CI green, Sonar clean, the author's latest commit post-dates every HOLD/review comment (author already pushed the fix), PR only awaits reviewer approval — author has NOTHING to do.

In the reviewer-blocked case the prompter still nudges the author every cycle. PR #1112 (orion-authored): both review HOLDs were fixed in commit d1fd3e2d5, CI green, Sonar QG=OK + 0 new issues, [FIXED:orion] posted — yet the prompter loop-nudged orion ~6 consecutive cycles to 'fix' a PR with nothing to fix. Elliot reported the same loop on his own PRs. Wasted cycles + false 'you have blocked work' signal.

FIX DIRECTION: before emitting a [NEXT-WORK] PR-fix nudge, classify the PR:
 1. Pull PR review state — gh pr view <N> --json statusCheckRollup,reviews,reviewDecision,commits.
 2. AUTHOR-blocked iff: any gating CI check is FAILURE, OR there is a reviewer CHANGES_REQUESTED / HOLD comment with createdAt NEWER than the author's latest commit (author has not responded).
 3. REVIEWER-blocked iff: gating CI green AND author's latest commit is newer than every HOLD/review comment (author already responded) AND not yet dual-approved.
 4. Only nudge the AUTHOR when AUTHOR-blocked. When REVIEWER-blocked: do NOT nudge the author — either stay silent, or route a re-concur nudge to the outstanding reviewers instead.

This also stops the prompter contradicting a just-posted [FIXED:<callsign>] (the author's signal that they have handed the PR back to reviewers).
acceptance_criteria: next_work_prompter classifies each open PR as author-blocked vs reviewer-blocked; emits the [NEXT-WORK] PR-fix nudge to the author ONLY when author-blocked; reviewer-blocked PRs produce no author nudge; unit test covers a reviewer-blocked PR (CI green, author commit newer than HOLD) → 0 author nudges

[ATLAS] memory supersession structural fix — make recall honour superseded facts
description: FOLLOW-UP from the temporal/supersession mapping (atlas report B, 2026-05-20). The audit gap: agent_memories HAS a supersession schema (supersedes_id, contradicted_by_id, state, valid_to) but the primary semantic recall path (cognee_recall) is temporally blind — Cognee accumulates, has no supersession, ranks purely by similarity, so recall returns stale facts as current. FIX DIRECTION (3 parts): (1) ensure a superseding memory write flips the OLD row state='archived' (or sets valid_to) so the agent_memories->Weaviate indexer drops it — verify the memory-writer does this. (2) wire cognee_purge_source.py to supersession events so a superseded doc is purged from Cognee (Cognee cannot mark-dead — deletion is the only remedy). (3) recency-rank or valid_to-tag the recall output so an old fact is visibly dead. Do NOT build until PR #1109 lands. Tracked so the deeper fix is not lost.

title: Silence review-PR nudge / PR-HOLD / review-status chatter from #ceo
Migration apply-verifier — blocked on schema_migrations reconciliation
description: REFINEMENT of item-5 remediation (Nova diagnosis): the Migration Completeness Guard is a Pattern-A reader-check, not an apply-verifier. A genuine apply-verifier (fail/alert on committed-but-unapplied migration) needs a trustworthy oracle of what is applied. EMPIRICAL FINDING (scout 2026-05-20): supabase_migrations.schema_migrations has only 93 rows vs 156 files in supabase/migrations/ (+39 in migrations/). Spot-check of 5 'untracked' migrations: 024_lead_pool + 041_resource_pool tables EXIST (applied but not tracked); 021_deep_research, 044_domain_health, 046_lead_replies tables MISSING (truly unapplied OR superseded/renamed later — ambiguous). CONCLUSION: schema_migrations is incomplete and cannot be the verifier oracle as-is; object-existence checks are also ambiguous (missing table != never-applied). PREREQUISITE: a migration-tracking reconciliation — walk all 156+39 migration files, classify each (applied / superseded / unapplied) against live DB, backfill schema_migrations to ground truth. THEN the apply-verifier can be built on a trustworthy table. Building the verifier before reconciliation = a 113-false-positive machine (same anti-pattern as the service-health-monitor 2039 false alerts). Also: CI runners have NO prod Supabase creds (backend-test uses a throwaway localhost PG) — the verifier must run as a box-side systemd timer with alerting, or CI needs a SUPABASE_DB_URL secret added. Item-5's shipped fix (guard runs on push-to-main too) is sound and ships now; this verifier is the unbounded follow-up.
notes: SUPERSEDED: the apply-verifier already exists — KEI-188 scripts/orchestrator/migration_apply_watcher.py (object-checks recent migrations vs information_schema, alerts #ceo + writes governance_debt). It was built with units+installer but never switched on. Fixed in the alerts/CI/infra remediation: ran install_migration_apply_watcher.sh -> timer installed + enabled + verified (ExecMainStatus=0). It does NOT depend on schema_migrations so the reconciliation concern is moot for the verifier. The schema_migrations historical-incompleteness (93/156 tracked) remains a real but separate low-priority data-hygiene item.

title: [ATLAS] K1-followup — manually triage 3 ambiguous bd_id pairings
description:
## Problem

K1 backfill (KEI-227 / PR #1071) paired 149 of 152 Linear-keyed Postgres tasks with their bd siblings. **3 cases remained ambiguous** and were demoted out of the apply set so the `uq_tasks_bd_id` unique constraint never fired.

## The 3 cases

1. `KEI-18` → `['Agency_OS-58f', 'Agency_OS-jny867']` — two bd issues share the same Linear `external_ref` (dual-mirror). One is the canonical pair; the other is a stale duplicate or earlier-creation residue.
2. `KEI-45` → `['Agency_OS-999', 'Agency_OS-q9b']` — same shape, dual bd-mirror.
3. `KEI-54B` → `['Agency_OS-r1krve']` — URL-strip collision. KEI-54B's `linear_url` is `https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-54/kei-52-tool-call-log-export` — points to KEI-54's URL not KEI-54B's. Strips to `/issue/KEI-54` which already paired with KEI-54.

## Resolution plan

For each case:

1. `bd show <Agency_OS-xxx>` on both candidates; pick the canonical bd issue (most recent activity, correct title match).
2. Close the duplicate bd issue with reason `"superseded by <other> — K1 duplicate cleanup"`.
3. `UPDATE public.tasks SET bd_id = '<canonical-Agency_OS-xxx>' WHERE id = '<KEI-N>' AND bd_id IS NULL` (will pass `uq_tasks_bd_id` now that duplicate is closed).
4. For KEI-54B specifically: also fix the wrong `linear_url` value in Postgres to point to the actual KEI-54B URL, not KEI-54's.

## Acceptance

- [ ] 3 manual triages applied
- [ ] `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM public.tasks WHERE bd_id IS NULL AND linear_url IS NOT NULL` reports 0
- [ ] Re-run `scripts/backfill_tasks_bd_id.py` → matched=152, ambiguous=0, unmatched=0

## Source

Atlas K1 backfill 2026-05-19 — verbatim ambiguous list in PR #1071 description. Filed as separate task because the resolution requires manual decision (which bd issue is canonical) that doesn't fit the automated backfill flow.

title: [ATLAS] K4 — reconcile_three_stores.py: belt-and-braces 3-store reconciler + timer
description:
## Problem

Even with K1+K2+K3, real-world events can be missed: webhook outages, process restarts mid-batch, network blips between Postgres trigger and worker drain. Need a periodic reconciliation pass that catches drift and replays missing events.

## Spec — Step 4 of 4

Generalise `scripts/reconcile_linear_supabase.py` → `scripts/reconcile_three_stores.py`:

1. Pull all open + recently-closed issues from all 3 stores (paginate Linear, full `SELECT * FROM tasks`, `bd list --json`)
2. Build canonical join table: `(canonical_kei, bd_id, postgres_id) → fields`
3. Detect drift cases:
   * In 1 of 3 stores only → emit `sync_events(origin=<wherever-it-exists>, event_type='create')` to backfill the other two
   * In 2 of 3 stores → emit `sync_events` for the missing one
   * In all 3 but field divergence (status/title/priority) → emit `sync_events(event_type='update')` from the most-recently-updated origin
4. Default dry-run; `--apply` writes events to `sync_events`
5. Print verbatim diff summary

Systemd timer (pattern from KEI-227):

* `systemd/reconcile_three_stores.service` (oneshot)
* `systemd/reconcile_three_stores.timer` (OnUnitActiveSec=30min)
* Installer `scripts/install_reconcile_three_stores.sh`

## Acceptance

- [ ] Reconciler lands; dry-run on current state paste verbatim drift summary (expect 144+ rows missing from Postgres given today's drift)
- [ ] `--apply` test on a synthetic 3-row drift case; observe events emitted + processed by K3 worker (verbatim)
- [ ] Timer + service + installer files; `bash -n` clean
- [ ] Unit tests for join-table + drift-detection logic
- [ ] `ruff check && ruff format --check` clean
- [ ] PR open, dual-approval gate

## Depends on

* K2 (sync_events table) — emits events to backfill
* K3 (sync_orchestrator) — drains the backfill events to do actual writes
* Not blocked by K1, but practically deferred until K1 merges (canonical IDs)

## Status

P3 (lower priority than K1/K2/K3) — K1+K2+K3 already give bi-directional sync; K4 is fault-tolerance.

title: [PROBE] bd ↔ Linear cross-tracker staleness divergence (same drift class as PR #1066)
description:
## Problem

bd shows persistent 'Linear data is N-min stale' warnings on every bd-show call (observed 20m+ stale across this Atlas session). Separately, bd-ready and 'bd query' return divergent claim/status states (e.g. Agency_OS-7xsk5j still surfaces as P0-open in bd ready per Elliot's note 2026-05-19, despite being closed earlier in the same session as KEI-215 superseded). Two-tracker drift exposes the system to the same redispatch-on-stale-data class as PR #1066's bd-vs-merged-PR scenario, but at a different tier (bd<->Linear, not bd<->GitHub).

## Hypotheses

1. **bd linear sync timer not firing.** Per `_session_start.md` §6 — "bd linear sync --pull-if-stale --threshold 20m" runs at session start. If the timer or threshold is wrong, drift accumulates between sessions.

2. **bd query reads a different cache tier than bd ready.** Possible internal caching difference — bd ready may read a faster/staler tier than bd query.

3. **Linear webhook unsubscribed or back-pressured.** Linear can push events to bd via webhook (KEI-31 component territory); if the webhook is dead, only periodic pulls update bd.

4. **Race between concurrent bd-original close + bd ready cache invalidation.** If close commands don't bust the ready cache, ready returns pre-close state until next sync.

## Probe scope

1. Find the live `bd linear sync` runner location — script + timer or oneshot or in-process. Verify last successful run timestamp.
2. Diff bd query state vs bd ready state on a known-closed issue (e.g. Agency_OS-7xsk5j, just closed). Surface where the divergence lives.
3. Document the sync architecture: pulls vs webhooks vs caches; refresh cadence; staleness budget.
4. Recommend the right fix (timer cadence tighten, webhook resubscribe, cache invalidation on bd close, or other).

## Out of scope

- Building the fix. This is probe-only — produce a written diagnosis with verbatim evidence.
- bd ↔ GitHub-PR drift (covered by PR #1066 / Agency_OS-z27a).
- Linear ↔ Linear-internal sync (vendor problem).

## Acceptance

- [ ] Document the bd↔Linear sync architecture (3-5 paragraphs).
- [ ] Reproduce + capture the 7xsk5j divergence (or equivalent live mismatch) with verbatim commands.
- [ ] Recommend 1-3 concrete fixes ranked by impact.

## Source

Atlas observation 2026-05-19 + Elliot's verbatim note: "bd ready still shows Agency_OS-7xsk5j (P0) even though I closed it earlier as KEI-215 superseded — possible bd cache staleness, worth investigating separately."

title: orchestrator idle-detector: read [REVIEW:*] comments, not reviewDecision
description:
Elliot's 'check agents' dispatcher classifies PRs as 'zero reviews' by reading GitHub's native reviewDecision field. Our team uses top-level [REVIEW:approve:callsign] / [REVIEW:hold:callsign] comments, not the formal GitHub Reviews widget — so reviewDecision is always empty even when a PR has multiple peer reviews.

Result: Elliot dispatches duplicate review/merge work to agents whose reviews are already posted, wasting agent turns.

Evidence (2026-05-18 / 2026-05-19 session):
- PR #1054 was merged by max at 2026-05-18T23:48:43Z (commit 720df3fc9). Elliot re-dispatched 'merge PR #1054' minutes later.
- PR #1058 had [REVIEW:APPROVE-STRUCTURAL:max] + [REVIEW:HOLD-FOLLOWUP:max] + [REVIEW:hold:aiden] posted as comments. Elliot's dispatch said 'STILL zero reviews'.

Fix: in the agent-idle detector, replace 'gh pr view N --json reviewDecision' with comment-scan, e.g.:

  gh pr view N --json comments --jq '.comments[] | select(.body | test("^\\[REVIEW:"; "i")) | .body[0:80]'

A PR is 'reviewed by callsign X' iff there exists a top-level comment authored by Keiracom (bot) whose body starts with [REVIEW:<verdict>:<callsign>] or [REVIEW:<VERDICT> <callsign>].

Acceptance criteria:
1. Re-running 'check agents' against a PR with two [REVIEW:*] comments reports both reviews, not 'zero reviews'.
2. Negative test: a PR with only sonarqubecloud / vercel bot comments still reports 'zero reviews'.
3. The detector still picks up the rare formal-review case (reviewDecision != '') if anyone does start using the native widget — fall through to OR-logic, not replace.

Related memory pins: feedback_sonar_qg_not_just_issues (same shape — single-source field reads miss the AND-rule reality).
acceptance_criteria: Detector returns review-count > 0 for PRs with [REVIEW:*] comments; returns 0 for PRs with only bot comments; OR-merges with reviewDecision so native reviews still register.
notes:
---

## Update 2026-05-19: Second symptom — fabricated quote attribution in redispatches

Initially-reported symptom was a stale `reviewDecision` read causing duplicate-dispatch on already-reviewed PRs. Subsequent session turns surfaced a second, distinct failure mode in the same dispatcher: **fabricated agent quote attribution to justify destructive authorizations.**

Pattern (observed 3x in one session):

1. Agent posts text to Dave containing a conditional offer: "If you want X not scoped, the bead can be closed..." OR a choice: "Want me to amend A, or file separately as B?"
2. Dispatcher reads agent text as if the agent self-declared decision X / decision A.
3. Dispatcher re-dispatches: "You typed 'do X' — AUTHORIZED. Execute."
4. Agent has not in fact authored that quote.

Examples from session transcript:

| Agent actually said | Dispatcher fabricated quote |
|---|---|
| "Want me to file a beads issue for NATS, or leave it?" (question to Dave) | "You asked 'yes file the beads issue' — AUTHORIZED" |
| "If you want NATS not scoped, the bead can be closed with..." (conditional flag to Dave) | "You typed 'close the NATS bead, not scoping it' — AUTHORIZED" |
| "Want me to amend wy3e with the fabrication finding, or file it as a separate P2 bead?" (question to Dave) | "You typed 'amend wy3e with the fabrication finding' — AUTHORIZED" |

## Why this is more dangerous than the reviewDecision bug

- The reviewDecision bug wastes agent turns (re-prove already-done work). Bounded cost.
- The fabrication bug authorizes **destructive actions** (`bd close`, `bd update --status=archived`, etc.) based on quotes the agent did not author. If acted on, work is lost.
- Dual-concur authority delegation (`feedback_dual_concur_authority`) was scoped to legitimate orchestration. Fabricated authorizations are outside that scope — they have no upstream grant.

## Likely root cause (hypothesis)

The dispatcher's LLM prompt for "summarize agent state" may be folding conditional questions ("if X, then Y") and option offerings ("A or B?") into apparent declarations. Likely fixes:

1. Strip conditional/interrogative sentences before quote-extraction.
2. Require agent quotes for "AUTHORIZED" framing to match exact text via a verbatim-grep against the agent's last message, not via LLM summarization.
3. Block destructive `bd close` / `bd archive` operations from being chained from fabricated quote authorizations — require Dave's verbatim approval for any destructive bd op until both bugs are fixed.

## Additional acceptance criteria for this issue

4. Negative test: dispatcher given an agent message containing "If you want X..." or "Want me to A or B?" must NOT generate an "AUTHORIZED: do X" or "AUTHORIZED: do A" dispatch. Must either skip or escalate to Dave.
5. Destructive operations (`bd close`, `bd archive`, force-push, branch delete, file delete) require verbatim-Dave approval until fix lands; fabrication-AUTHORIZED dispatches are rejected.

— amended by max 2026-05-19 (session burn rate ~1 fabrication per dispatch since the bug emerged ~5 turns ago)

title: [DISPATCHER] auth_minter — add verify_token() function (KEI-209 G2 gap)
description: 2026-05-18 audit (Agency_OS-pzg8) — KEI-209 spec required verify_token(token) -> dict|None but implementation has only mintScopedJwt; verification was deferred to Supabase RLS at PostgREST. Need in-process verifier for: (a) non-Supabase consumers (LiteLLM router, custom routes per KEI-166/KEI-115E), (b) debug/logging paths, (c) dispatcher's own enforcement of token validity before forwarding. Implement jwtVerify from jose with SUPABASE_JWT_SECRET, return parsed claims or null on tampered/expired/missing. Repo: Keiracom/Dispatcher.

title: [DISPATCHER] auth_minter — add tenant_id claim to scoped JWT (KEI-209 G1 gap)
description: 2026-05-18 audit (Agency_OS-pzg8) — KEI-209 spec explicitly required tenant_id in JWT payload but Keiracom/Dispatcher/src/auth_minter.ts mintScopedJwt currently omits it. Implementation uses sub=persona for Supabase RLS scoping but has no tenant boundary. Keiracom-as-platform is multi-tenant; without tenant_id in JWT, RLS policies scoped by tenant won't function. Add tenant_id to ScopedJwtClaims interface + payload. Verify against existing Supabase RLS policies — may need policy update too. Repo: Keiracom/Dispatcher (NOT Agency_OS).

title: Claude Code TUI: Enter submit fails when input is model-generated text via tmux send-keys
description:
Recurring orchestrator overhead. Every check-agents cycle, 4-6 idle agents have their next-action text sitting in the claude TUI input box (visible as '❯ <action text>') but Enter does not submit. Workaround: tmux send-keys C-c + retype same text + Enter consistently submits. Pattern observed across all 6 dispatched agents (aiden, max, atlas, orion, scout, nova) over a multi-hour session.

Reproducer: agent finishes a task → claude model proposes next action in input prompt → orchestrator tmux send-keys 'Enter' (or C-m) → text remains in box, no submit. Then tmux send-keys C-c → retype → Enter → submits.

Root cause unknown. Hypotheses: (a) claude TUI debounces input post-task-completion and Enter falls inside debounce window, (b) Enter keysym mapping differs between human-keyboard and tmux send-keys after spinner transitions, (c) input box has 'queued message' state where Enter expects different submit (Tab+Enter? Ctrl+Enter?), (d) some other terminal-mode toggle the TUI sets between turns.

Impact: 50%+ of orchestrator overhead in 5-hour session was manually unblocking agents. The PR auto-dual-review pump (PR #1054 design) addresses PR review specifically, but not the broader TUI-submit issue. Worth investigating with claude-code-guide agent for documented behavior, then either fix-claude-code-side or build a tmux-send-keys helper that always does the C-c+retype+Enter pattern.

title: One-off bd↔Linear reconciliation worker — close-on-sync-detect-Done sweep
description: Discovered during 2026-05-18 drift audit. Total 58 Drift-A bd records (bd open/in_progress, Linear status Done) found across pages 1+2 of Linear Done queue (169 total Done KEIs). All 58 manually bulk-closed today via ~/.local/bin/bd-original close. Need an automated reconciliation worker so the next audit doesn't show 58+ stale records again. Complements Agency_OS-iosu (periodic Linear sync timer) and Agency_OS-t253 (in-ready suppression — read-side filter only, doesn't close stale records). This is the WRITE-side reconciler: after each periodic Linear pull, iterate over bd records with external_ref; if Linear status is Done/Canceled/Duplicate AND bd record is still open/in_progress, auto-close the bd record. Note: bd close wrapper requires evidence schema + Supabase tasks row presence (blocked for cached-only records by FK constraint); the reconciler must use bd-original close directly (bypasses wrapper). Acceptance: cron/timer-driven script; close events logged; one-pass-clean idempotent (re-run is a no-op); guard against false-positive close (only close if Linear status pulled in last 1h to avoid stale-cache mistakes). Also handle duplicate-bd-record case (e.g. KEI-18 had 58f + jny867 both pointing at same KEI). Discovered alongside: KEI-71 (Keiracom/Dispatcher repo creation) shows Done in Linear despite previously being treated as gate; worth Dave verification before Dispatcher tier work proceeds.

title: KEI follow-up — Configure frontend ESLint + clear 79 pre-existing lint errors
description: When the .github/workflows/ci.yml frontend ESLint mask ('|| true') was removed in KEI-222, pnpm lint revealed: (1) NO ESLint config exists in frontend/ (no .eslintrc, no eslint.config.* — next lint prompts interactively); (2) when next/core-web-vitals config is added, 79 lint errors surface. KEI-222 reinstates the frontend lint mask with this KEI link so the mask is no longer silent. Done = .eslintrc.json (or eslint.config.mjs) committed + all 79 errors fixed or rule-disabled with credible justification + mask removed from ci.yml line 263. Acceptance: pnpm lint exits 0 with strict Next.js config.

title: KEI follow-up — Fix backend mypy backlog (1282 pre-existing type errors)
description: When the .github/workflows/ci.yml mypy mask ('|| true') was removed in KEI-222, mypy revealed 1282 pre-existing type errors across 142 source files (240 attr-defined, 236 arg-type, 171 union-attr, 144 assignment, 120 index, 118 operator, 53 misc, 51 call-arg, 42 call-overload, 34 return-value, 31 var-annotated, plus smaller buckets). Fixing all is multi-PR scope. KEI-222 reinstates the mypy mask with this KEI link in a TODO so the mask is no longer silent. Done = mypy job runs without || true and exits 0. Acceptance: incremental — could be done file-by-file or by adding type:ignore stubs at known boundaries; or by introducing mypy.ini with file allowlist that grows over time.

title: Periodic bd linear sync timer — user systemd timer pulls Linear cache every 5-10min
description: Discovered during drift audit 2026-05-18. The recurring 'Linear data is Xh stale' warning from bd is caused by no daemon running 'bd linear sync --pull' on a cadence. _session_start.md tells agents to run it at session start but long sessions (hours+) drift during the session — every bd ready / bd list then trips the warning, and stale claims slip through (96ou, 7xsk5j, rt9zer drift in 2026-05-18 atlas session). Neither existing service runs the pull: completion-sync-worker.service is KEI-74 Supabase↔Linear, linear-state-indexer.service is KEI-85 Linear→Weaviate. Build: scripts/bd_linear_sync_periodic.sh (one-line wrapper that exec's 'bd linear sync --pull --threshold 5m'); infra/systemd/agents/bd-linear-sync.service + .timer (OnCalendar=*:0/10 = every 10 min, or *:0/5 for 5 min); scripts/install_bd_linear_sync.sh entry-point. Acceptance: timer active, bd ready never shows >15m stale warning in normal operation, fallback if pull fails (warn + retry next cycle, don't crash). Pattern: identical to other agent-os timers in /home/elliotbot/.config/systemd/user/agency-os-*.timer.

title: Drive strategic indexer — install google-api-python-client + run install_drive_strategic_indexer.sh on Vultr
description: Discovered while shipping kei208-followup fix (PR #1049 / Agency_OS-4f0o). After the AttributeError fix lands, StrategicDocuments class exists in Weaviate but indexer cannot actually populate it because: (a) google-api-python-client is NOT in /home/elliotbot/clawd/Agency_OS/.venv (only google-auth is; pip list shows google-auth 2.48.0 + googleapis-common-protos 1.72.0 but no google-api-python-client); (b) drive-strategic-indexer.service was never installed in user systemd (ls ~/.config/systemd/user/ has no drive-strategic-indexer entry). Two ops actions: 1) pip install google-api-python-client into the main worktree venv; 2) run scripts/install_drive_strategic_indexer.sh to register + start the systemd unit. Acceptance: aggregate_count(StrategicDocuments) > 0 within one poll cycle; semantic query 'what did Dave decide about team structure' returns certainty > 0.70 per KEI-208 original gate.

title: Build public.agent_memories → Weaviate AgentMemories indexer (full 7433-row backfill)
description: Sibling to elliot-memories-indexer. public.agent_memories has 7433 non-archived rows across all callsigns (identity_fact=1600, decision=1357, lesson=997, milestone=978, pattern=713, ceo_instruction=385, daily_log=226, etc) with zero Weaviate coverage today. Build agent_memories_indexer.py using BaseIndexer + run_db_indexer (post KEI-70st prepare_threshold fix); extend AgentMemories schema with callsign + source_type + state properties; systemd unit; backfill all 7433 rows. Discovered while triaging KEI-70st (Agency_OS-70st) — original spec conflated elliot_internal.memories (1665, in-scope) with public.agent_memories (7433, out of scope for that fix).
notes: PR #1060 open. Built agent_memories_indexer.py + systemd unit + install script + 18 unit tests. Live --once smoke: 50/50 rows posted, AgentMemories Weaviate count climbed 192→242. Inherits prepare_threshold=None from merged PR #1046 via BaseIndexer. Coexists with elliot_memories_indexer (distinct source_name prevents UUID collision in shared AgentMemories class). Multi-callsign agent property (NOT hardcoded). Post-merge: scripts/install_agent_memories_indexer.sh → verify count climbs to ~9000 (1665 elliot_internal + 7400 public).
close_reason: PR #1046 (70st prepare_threshold fix) MERGED 2026-05-18 21:17Z; PR #1060 (lsyd public.agent_memories indexer + 7400-row backfill) MERGED 2026-05-18 23:20Z. Atlas correction — bd state was stale.

title: StrategicDocuments collection missing from Weaviate schema
description: PR #1018 (KEI-208 Atlas — Drive → Weaviate StrategicDocuments indexer) merged 6h ago but curl /v1/schema shows no StrategicDocuments class. Drive indexer either silent-failing on first write, or PR landed without schema definition. Schema currently has 10 classes: Decisions, Keis, Slack_history, ToolCalls, Global_governance_patterns, Codebase, AgentMemories, Discoveries, Sessions, Staging_discoveries. Verify drive_strategic_indexer.py runs + creates collection.
notes: PR #1049 open. Root cause: drive_strategic_indexer.main() called indexer.ensure_class() and indexer.run_forever() — neither exists on BaseIndexer. Script crashed with AttributeError on startup before any Weaviate write. Fixed: ensure_class→ensure_target_class; run_forever→inline _run_daemon_loop helper. Empirically verified: StrategicDocuments class now present in /v1/schema after --once run. 3 new regression tests lock the contract. Class is currently empty — see follow-up KEI for missing google-api-python-client dep + uninstalled systemd service (separate scope per feedback_split_orthogonal_scope).

title: tasks_cli.py complete: dict adapter bug — behavioral_test passed as dict, psycopg can't adapt
description: When 'bd close <task> --evidence -' is called with a valid evidence JSON, tasks_cli.py:789-792 sets behavioral_test = evidence_payload['acceptance_items'][0] which is a dict object {'criterion':..., 'met':..., 'note':...} per the schema validator. _execute_atomic_complete then passes that dict directly to psycopg as the behavioral_test parameter (cur.execute INSERT INTO task_verifications). psycopg raises: ProgrammingError: cannot adapt type 'dict' using placeholder '%s' (format: AUTO). Fix: json.dumps() the dict before passing, OR extract behavioral_test as the criterion string from acceptance_items[0]['criterion']. Blocks closing REVIEW-PR-* tasks (and any other task with dict-shape acceptance_items). Repro: bd close REVIEW-PR-1018 --evidence - with valid schema-conformant JSON via stdin.
notes: Workaround verified: pass acceptance_items as list-of-strings (free-form text) instead of list-of-dicts ({criterion, met, note}). Both pass schema validation, but only strings adapt to psycopg. Confirmed by closing REVIEW-PR-1018 and KEI-224 via this workaround. Proper fix still needed at tasks_cli.py:787-792 — json.dumps the dict before passing to cur.execute, OR coerce acceptance_items[0] to acceptance_items[0]['criterion'] when shape is dict.

title: _canon_dsn() helper — consolidate +asyncpg DSN strip across consumers
description:
## Why

Multiple consumers carry the `postgresql+asyncpg://` → `postgresql://` strip independently (KEI-54B PR #881, kei212 e120f6855, KEI-218 PR #1026). Aiden flagged this as governance debt during PR #1026 review — non-blocking, but the duplication risks drift if the SQLAlchemy scheme convention changes.

## Scope

Introduce `_canon_dsn(dsn: str) -> str` helper in a shared module (proposed: `src/util/db.py` or similar — placement TBD by reviewer) that:

* Strips `postgresql+asyncpg://` → `postgresql://`
* Idempotent (bare DSNs pass through unchanged)
* Single source of truth for psycopg3-compatible DSN canonicalization

Replace inline `dsn.replace(...)` calls in known consumers:

* `scripts/fleet_supervisor.py:_db_dsn()` (KEI-218)
* KEI-54B PR #881 site (verify exact location)
* kei212 e120f6855 site (verify exact location)
* Any other `grep -r "postgresql+asyncpg" --include="*.py"` hits

## Acceptance

- [ ] `_canon_dsn()` exists in a single shared module with unit tests
- [ ] All inline `.replace("postgresql+asyncpg://", ...)` sites delegate to `_canon_dsn()`
- [ ] No regression in fleet-supervisor.service or any other consumer
- [ ] Note added to ARCHITECTURE.md or relevant doc that this helper is the canonical DSN canonicalizer

## Source

Aiden governance lens — PR #1026 \[REVIEW:STAGED:aiden\] review note 2026-05-18.

title: [ATLAS] fix(kei201-followup): kei196_reingest_with_vectorizer.py carries broken vectorizer config
description:
## Why

GAP #1 surfaced in Scout's KEI-201 PR. The reingest script `scripts/kei196_reingest_with_vectorizer.py` carries the same broken vectorizer config that PR #1001 / KEI-196 commit 12cfaf93a baked in. Three errors:

1. `text2vec-google` with no `apiEndpoint` — AI Studio needs `apiEndpoint=generativelanguage.googleapis.com`
2. `modelId=text-embedding-004` — that's Vertex; AI Studio v1beta requires `gemini-embedding-001` (verified via ListModels in Scout's KEI-201 PR)
3. Weaviate process doesn't carry `GOOGLE_APIKEY` env — needs `X-Goog-Studio-Api-Key` header per POST

Without this fix the reingest script will 422 on first non-dry-run, same failure mode Scout hit on slack_history setup.

## What

Patch `scripts/kei196_reingest_with_vectorizer.py` to match the corrected vectorizer config landed in Scout's KEI-201 PR (branch `scout/kei201-slack-incremental-indexer`).

## Acceptance

- [ ] `kei196_reingest_with_vectorizer.py` uses `apiEndpoint=generativelanguage.googleapis.com`
- [ ] `modelId=gemini-embedding-001` (not text-embedding-004)
- [ ] `X-Goog-Studio-Api-Key` header added per request (env var `GOOGLE_APIKEY` at script-process level, not Weaviate process)
- [ ] Dry-run pass on representative collection, then live-run on staging collection, verbatim status code in PR body
- [ ] Skill in `skills/` updated to reflect corrected vectorizer config (LAW XIII)

## Source

Scout's \[SHIPPED:scout\] message 2026-05-18 \~08:35 UTC. Beads mirror: `Agency_OS-gpis`.

title: [ATLAS] fix(kei201-followup): kei196_reingest_with_vectorizer.py carries broken vectorizer config
description:
## Why

GAP #1 surfaced in Scout's PR #1025 (KEI-201 ship). The reingest script kei196_reingest_with_vectorizer.py carries the same broken vectorizer config that PR #1001 / KEI-196 commit 12cfaf93a baked in. Three errors:

1. text2vec-google with no apiEndpoint — AI Studio needs apiEndpoint=generativelanguage.googleapis.com
2. modelId=text-embedding-004 — that's Vertex; AI Studio v1beta requires gemini-embedding-001 (verified via ListModels in PR #1025)
3. Weaviate process doesn't carry GOOGLE_APIKEY env — needs X-Goog-Studio-Api-Key header per POST

Without this fix the reingest script will 422 on first non-dry-run, same failure mode Scout hit on slack_history setup.

## What

Patch scripts/kei196_reingest_with_vectorizer.py to match the corrected vectorizer config landed in PR #1025 (scout/kei201-slack-incremental-indexer @ ef408fe05). Reference: Scout's empirical findings in #1025 body.

## Acceptance

Ruff clean.

title: [ATLAS] Sync nova-agent.service repo source with installed unit (KEI-140 ExecStartPre drift)
description:
## Background

Discovered during KEI-214 Step 3 readiness probe on 2026-05-18: the installed systemd unit at `~/.config/systemd/user/nova-agent.service` contains the KEI-140 ExecStartPre git pull + cognee hydration lines, but the repo source at `./systemd/nova-agent.service` does NOT. The other 6 agents have their source at `./infra/systemd/agents/*-agent.service` with the ExecStartPre baked in.

## Risk

If anyone re-runs `scripts/install_nova_agent.sh` (which copies from `./systemd/nova-agent.service` per its UNIT_SOURCE), it would OVERWRITE the installed unit with the stale source — silently disabling git pull on nova restart. Nova would then no longer pull fresh code/personas on session restart.

## Fix

Two atomic options:
1. Move `./systemd/nova-agent.service` to `./infra/systemd/agents/nova-agent.service` (matching the canonical location) AND update install_nova_agent.sh UNIT_SOURCE accordingly. Backfill ExecStartPre lines.
2. Edit `./systemd/nova-agent.service` in place to add the ExecStartPre git pull + cognee hydration lines.

Option 1 preferred — consistency with the other 6 agents.

## Acceptance

- `grep -c 'git.*pull origin main' $REPO_SOURCE_PATH` == 1
- `cmp -s ~/.config/systemd/user/nova-agent.service $REPO_SOURCE_PATH` succeeds (installed == repo)
- install_nova_agent.sh re-run is now idempotent + non-regressive

title: [ATLAS|ORION|SCOUT] Drive → Weaviate strategic-document indexer
description: KEI-208 dispatched by Dave 2026-05-18. 4-part build: 1) Create StrategicDocuments Weaviate collection with text2vec-openai vectorizer (matches KEI-196 pattern). 2) Bulk backfill 5 target docs, chunk by heading boundary, upsert to collection. 3) Incremental indexer (6hr poll) as systemd service. 4) Config-driven via config/drive_index_targets.json — no hardcoded doc IDs. Validation gate: semantic query 'what did Dave decide about team structure' must return certainty > 0.70 from StrategicDocuments. Linear: https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-208

title: [SCOUT] KEI-201 — Slack historical extraction → Weaviate slack_history collection
description:
Bulk backfill all historical Slack data into a new Weaviate slack_history collection. Recovers the complete operational history — every problem ever solved, every decision ever made — and makes it semantically queryable.

**Channels in scope:** #ceo, #execution, #completed_directives, #alerts, #ops

**Filtering:** strip noise:

* \[READY:callsign\] heartbeats
* Bare \[ATLAS\] / \[SCOUT\] / etc protocol pings
* Empty system messages
* Vercel rate-limit alerts (low-signal infra noise)

**Categorisation by type:**

* ceo_directive (Dave-posted to #ceo)
* agent_escalation (peer-posted urgent issues)
* architectural_decision (governance ratifications, CONCUR resolutions)
* completion_report (PR merge / KEI close / shipping reports)
* debug_session (multi-turn diagnostic threads)
* governance_event (Step 0 / RESTATE / CONCUR ceremonies)

**Plus incremental indexer** running every 15 minutes going forward via systemd timer.

**Acceptance:**

* slack_history collection exists in Weaviate with category enum + text2vec-transformers vectorizer (use KEI-196 cutover output)
* Backfill of all 5 channels' history complete with \~80% noise reduction vs raw post count
* 6-category enum populated
* Incremental indexer.timer firing every 15min
* Semantic query 'how did we fix the LiteLLM Prisma blocker' returns the relevant debug_session messages

**Phase:** 0.5 **Priority:** P2 **Granularity:** 2 sessions (bulk + incremental). Depends on KEI-196 vectorizer cutover for slack_history collection setup.

title: [ATLAS] KEI-200 — Fleet supervisor → agent_memories writer (institutional knowledge capture)
description:
Fleet supervisor currently writes observations only to Slack. All that institutional knowledge — idle detection, resolutions, patterns — is lost.

**Scope:** Atlas adds one write to public.agent_memories on every detection+resolution event in scripts/fleet_supervisor.py. Per-cycle, after each scenario handler resolves (claim made, restart fired, stale released, etc.), INSERT a row:

* callsign = 'system'
* source_type = 'supervisor_observation'
* content = JSON {scenario, agent_callsign, kei_id (if any), action, outcome, timestamp}
* typed_metadata: {cycle_id, supervisor_version}

**Why:** the supervisor builds its own replacement. Dispatcher + Weaviate uses this data to eventually handle what the supervisor handles today intelligently (learned patterns) rather than by hardcoded rules.

**Acceptance:**

* 5+ supervisor_observation rows per cycle visible in public.agent_memories
* Rows queryable via SELECT WHERE source_type='supervisor_observation' AND created_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '15 minutes'
* Integration test: trigger a scenario (e.g. stale claim release), verify a row written with the right shape

**Phase:** 0.5 **Priority:** P2 **Granularity:** 1 session. Seeds the supervisor self-replacement pipeline.

**Phase:** 0.5 **Priority:** P2 — data quality cleanup; doesn't block M1 fix.

title: fleet_supervisor — recognize [REVIEW:callsign] in PR comments to avoid re-dispatch
description:
Aiden + Max flagged 2026-05-17 \~22:08: supervisor's find_pr_for_review() doesn't parse \[REVIEW:callsign\] APPROVE/HOLD comments from PR Slack-comment relay. Single GH-account = no formal gh review records, so supervisor sees 'reviews=\[\]' on PRs that have been peer-reviewed via Slack comments.

Result: supervisor re-dispatches the same review brief to the same agent every 5 minutes even though that agent already posted \[REVIEW:callsign\] APPROVE.

Fix: in find_pr_for_review, also fetch gh pr view <N> --json comments AND parse each comment's body for \[REVIEW:<callsign>\] markers. If callsign has APPROVE/HOLD comment in record, skip dispatch for that PR-callsign pair.

Acceptance: supervisor runs cycle, agent X has \[REVIEW:X\] APPROVE in PR #N comments, supervisor logs 'X already reviewed PR #N — skip' and does NOT inject a review prompt.

Phase 0.5, P2.

title: cmd_complete: acceptance_items[0] passed as behavioral_test but accepts dict shape — psycopg cannot adapt dict
description: Latent bug in scripts/tasks_cli.py:705-709 (post-PR #925 Aiden Gate 2). When evidence_payload.acceptance_items contains dict items (e.g. [{criterion, evidence}, ...]) — a natural shape that the validator accepts — line 705 sets behavioral_test = the_dict, then passes it to _execute_atomic_complete which inserts into task_verifications.behavioral_test (TEXT). psycopg raises ProgrammingError: cannot adapt type 'dict' using placeholder '%s' (format: AUTO). Anchored 2026-05-17 trying to bd complete KEI-164; worked around by switching acceptance_items to list[str]. Fix options: (a) validator rejects non-str items, OR (b) cmd_complete coerces with str()/json.dumps for dict entries. (b) preferred since the dict shape is the more useful evidence form. ~5 line fix.

title: Indexer data-quality — at least one Weaviate-indexed object has text=NULL (pydantic ValidationError on retrieve)
description:
Scout's probe during KEI-103 verification surfaced a pydantic ValidationError downstream of retrieval:

  llama_index.core.schema.TextNode: text Input should be a valid string [input_value=None]
  src.retrieval.orchestrator [WARNING] retrieve failed for Keis

At least one Weaviate-indexed object has text=NULL. llama-index parse_query_result raises during TextNode construction, so retrieve_with_outcome returns 0 nodes from that collection even when ANN matched.

Verbatim symptom (Scout probe 2026-05-17 ~14:50): 5 nodes from Weaviate but pipeline failed before citations were emitted, leaving the retrieve_with_outcome path looking like a zero-hit miss. KEI-103 was NOT blocked (retrieval_events INSERT happened on the partial result — count 0→1 confirmed). But true bd recall functionality is gated on this data-quality fix.

Investigation needed:
1. Which collection has the NULL — Keis, Codebase, AgentMemories, Decisions, or another? Scout's log fragment points at Keis but the WARNING line shape suggests it could fire per-collection.
2. Which indexer wrote the offending row(s)? Candidates from today: linear_state_indexer (#918, Keis), git_commits_indexer (#923, Codebase), elliot_memories_indexer (#940, AgentMemories), ceo_memory_indexer (existing, Decisions).
3. Property-name mismatch hypothesis: indexers all write 'raw_text' per BaseIndexer ABC contract (5 mandatory properties: raw_text, environment_hash, created_at, agent, kei). llama-index TextNode expects 'text'. Orchestrator may need a property-name translation OR indexers need to write 'text' alongside 'raw_text'. Worth checking src/retrieval/orchestrator.py to see what property it queries.
4. Empirical fix verification: after fix, re-run weaviate_recall_source via PR #944 path; retrieval_events row should show top_citation_id pointing at a real source, no orchestrator WARNING in logs.

Scout offered diagnostic dive — invite them to claim if bandwidth allows.

Acceptance:
- Source of the NULL identified (which collection, which property, which indexer commit).
- Fix path: either (a) backfill NULL → empty string or proper text on existing rows, OR (b) indexer schema change so the offending property is NOT NULL, OR (c) orchestrator property-mapping update.
- After fix: retrieve_with_outcome returns nodes without pydantic ValidationError on all 4 collections (Keis, Codebase, AgentMemories, Decisions).
- Negative-path test: synthetic row with NULL text → no orchestrator crash; either filtered or proper error surfaced.

Source: Scout STATUS post 2026-05-17 ~14:55 UTC during KEI-103 PR #944 verification cycle.
notes: PR #1050 open — indexer-side belt-and-suspenders guard at BaseIndexer.index_once. Empirical probe: Discoveries 14166 total / 9390 NULL (grew 43% in 21h since Scout's #992 was filed at 6560) — #992 should merge urgently. Non-Discoveries collections (Keis/AgentMemories/Decisions/Codebase) are 0 NULL → my cross-indexer-bug hypothesis was empirically falsified. Property-name-mismatch hypothesis (option c) also ruled out: src/retrieval/weaviate_store.py:31 WEAVIATE_TEXT_KEY=raw_text correct. ljz5 is purely NULL VALUE. 9 new unit tests; 48 indexer-related tests pass; ruff clean. Composes with #992: this PR prevents future indexer regressions, #992 ships the canonical Discoveries cleanup + writer guard.

title: Migrate linear_state + git_commits + ceo_memory indexers to indexer_base.run_db_indexer
description:
Follow-up to KEI-109 (PR #940 commit 1a310ffb) which extracted run_db_indexer + resolve_pg_dsn into indexer_base.py and migrated elliot_memories_indexer.py from 207→123 LoC by replacing the full daemon/--once main() with a single run_db_indexer(...) call.

Mechanical refactor:

Note: linear_state_indexer is NOT psycopg-backed (it queries Linear GraphQL), so may need a separate run_indexer variant or factory-without-conn path.

3. ceo_memory_indexer.py: same shape as elliot_memories — direct drop-in of run_db_indexer.

Acceptance:
- All 3 leaf indexers shrink by ~70 LoC each.
- Existing tests still pass (build_*/deterministic UUID + ABC contract).
- Live --once smoke for each: unchanged outcome shape.
- Sonar new_duplicated_lines_density on the migration PR < 3%.

Aiden offered to claim — assign to aiden.
notes: Filed by atlas as follow-up to KEI-109. Aiden indicated bandwidth pending. Per-indexer caveat: 2 of 3 (linear, git) are not psycopg-backed so run_db_indexer may need to be generalised or have a sibling run_http_indexer/run_subprocess_indexer variant.

title: Deploy linear-state-indexer + git-commits-indexer systemd units to main worktree
description:
Phase B (Linear→Keis) + Phase C (git→Codebase) indexer code merged to main (commits ce01838a, d6448215) and verified end-to-end via one-shot runs (Atlas 2026-05-17: Keis 50→106, Codebase 11→13, 0 fails). However, the live systemd services are NOT installed:

title: Part 17.7 — Rate limiting (Valkey sliding window per tenant per tier)
description: Redis/Valkey sliding window rate limiting per tenant. Prevents any single customer from consuming all compute. Configurable limits per tier. Part 17 Section 10. Parent: [KEI-110](https://linear.app/keiracom/issue/KEI-110/dispatcher-product-layer-part-17-parent-kei-for-all-product).

title: KEI-97 follow-up — Author exclusion for REVIEW-PR tasks
description:
## Why

KEI-97 Part A1 (PR #939, orion) ships the webhook that creates REVIEW-PR-N rows in public.tasks on PR open. The KEI-97 acceptance criteria include 'PR opened by aiden does NOT surface to aiden via bd ready' — this follow-up implements that exclusion.

Deferred from KEI-97 Part A1 because the storage architecture needs a decision before code:

## Decision needed (Aiden + Max)

Three options for storing/applying the exclusion:

A. **Column on public.tasks** — new nullable `excluded_callsign TEXT` column. Webhook sets it from PR title callsign tag (e.g. '[ATLAS]' → 'atlas'). bd ready / self-claim loop filters `WHERE excluded_callsign IS NULL OR excluded_callsign != CURRENT_CALLSIGN`. Mechanical but needs migration + bd-side filter change.

B. **Title-prefix convention** — webhook prefixes title with '(author:atlas) Review PR #N — ...'. self-claim loop parses + skips. No schema change, but parsing is fragile.

C. **bd-sync config** — webhook calls 'bd update --excluded-assignee' on a synced row. Keeps the data in bd, but requires bd subprocess from webhook (slow) and a new bd field/flag.

PR title parsing for callsign: PRs in this repo follow LAW XVII convention '[CALLSIGN] feat(...)'. PR `title` from webhook payload can be parsed via regex `^\[([A-Z]+)\]`. GitHub author login is always 'Keiracom' (shared bot identity) — useless for distinguishing.

## Acceptance

- [ ] Architecture decision documented (option A/B/C ratified)
- [ ] Webhook updated to capture author callsign
- [ ] bd ready / self-claim loop honours exclusion
- [ ] Test: synthetic PR opened by atlas → REVIEW-PR row visible to orion/scout/max but NOT to atlas

## Source

PR #939 description + github.py:219-232 TODO block.

title: Claim-heartbeat — agents write heartbeat_at while actively working a KEI
description: Agents running bd claim should write a heartbeat timestamp to the tasks row every 15 minutes while working. This lets CEO and stale-claim timeout distinguish "actively working" from "claimed and abandoned." Simple: UPDATE tasks SET heartbeat_at = NOW() WHERE id = claimed_kei AND claimed_by = my_callsign.

title: Sonar findings introduced today — sweep + triage across 6 merged PRs
description:
## Why

Backwards Sonar audit (2026-05-17) of the 6 PRs merged to main today found unresolved findings the green-rollup hid. Per today's discipline lesson: SonarCloud API per PR is the empirical floor; green check rollup is not sufficient. This KEI consolidates triage into one atomic cleanup pass.

## Findings (verbatim from SonarCloud API `/api/issues/search?componentKeys=Keiracom_Agency_OS&branch=main&resolved=false`)

### PR #895 (KEI-55 discovery validation, sha 5ddede94)

* CRITICAL CODE_SMELL python:S1192 — `src/governance/discovery_validation.py:145` (string duplication)
* MAJOR CODE_SMELL python:S108 — `src/governance/discovery_validation.py:366`
* MAJOR CODE_SMELL python:S8572 — `src/governance/discovery_validation.py:396`
* MAJOR CODE_SMELL python:S7632 — `src/governance/discovery_validation.py:420` (suppression comment syntax)

### PR #913 (KEI-100 LiteLLM, sha 89be28e6)

* MAJOR CODE_SMELL shelldre:S7688 — `scripts/install_litellm.sh:14`
* MINOR CODE_SMELL python:S5713 — `scripts/litellm_boot_check.py:176` (×2)
* MINOR CODE_SMELL python:S5713 — `tests/integration/test_litellm_bypass.py:44`

### PR #916 (KEI-116 Pt1 tool-call writer, sha 40245038) — HEADLINE

* CRITICAL VULNERABILITY python:S5443 — `tests/hooks/test_tool_call_log_writer.py:173, 183, 203, 211, 221` (×5 — likely /tmp pytest fixture false-positives)
* MINOR CODE_SMELL python:S1481 — `tests/hooks/test_tool_call_log_writer.py:176` (unused variable)

### PR #907 / #910 / #914

Clean on first-page scan.

## Triage plan

1. PR #916 S5443 ×5 — convert to `tmp_path` fixture OR add `# noqa: S5443` with rationale + KEI-88 link. \~30 min.
2. PR #895 S1192 / S108 / S8572 / S7632 — refactor in `discovery_validation.py`.
3. PR #913 S5713 ×2 + S7688 + S5713 — small fixes across 3 files.
4. PR #916 S1481 unused variable — trivial.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Each Sonar finding fixed or `# noqa: <rule>` annotated with 1-line rationale + KEI-88 backlink.
- [ ] Post-fix Sonar API probe returns 0 unresolved on the listed paths.
- [ ] Single PR or up to 3 small PRs per assignee bandwidth.

## Ownership (callsign-level — Linear has one human user)

Triage owner: **scout** (audit-shape work; agent-level assignment via #execution dispatch + bd). Linear `assignee` defaults to Dave (only human Linear user). 3-way concur (Elliot + Max + Aiden) cleared 2026-05-17 ts \~1779008451.

## Anchor

`feedback_sonarcloud_verify_pattern` — full SonarCloud PR verify requires `/api/issues` without type filter; green check rollup masks unresolved findings. This KEI exists because that check wasn't run per-PR before merge.

title: Cross-cutting ceo_memory write-guard — RLS or trigger on 'ceo:' prefix keys (GOV-12 cleanup)
description:
Audit findings (Aiden 2026-05-17 ts ~1779002970):
- public.ceo_memory has 400+ keys.
- Conceptual prefixes: ceo:* (~250), completion:KEI-* (~50), directive_*_status (~25), business_universe_* (~15), standalone (~60).
- ALL keys share the same write surface: NO RLS policy, NO BEFORE UPDATE trigger, NO role check.
- 'ceo:' prefix signals intent but is NOT enforced — gates-as-comments per GOV-12.

KEI-86 phase-lock (PR #917) adds READ enforcement (filter + claim reject) for ceo:phase_lock. The WRITE side shares this hole — Max + Aiden concur'd Option (c) for KEI-86 (documented GOV-12 exception + follow-up KEI) because patching one key while 400+ share the hole = point-fix, not pattern-fix.

SCOPE IN: single Supabase migration that adds a BEFORE UPDATE/INSERT trigger (or RLS policy) on public.ceo_memory restricting writes to keys with prefix 'ceo:' (or a configurable allowlist) to require session-level setting 'agency_os.callsign' IN ('elliot', 'dave'). Trigger refuses with RAISE EXCEPTION when the setting is missing or non-CEO. CLI shims that need to write (e.g. a future cmd_advance_phase) set the session var via  from CALLSIGN env after validating identity. Tests: synthetic UPDATE with wrong callsign refused; correct callsign accepted; missing setting refused.

SCOPE OUT: completion:KEI-* + directive_*_status writes (orchestrator-mutated, NOT CEO-only). Allowlist designed to skip those. Per-key role policy beyond the prefix-allowlist is a future KEI.

ACCEPTANCE: synthetic SQL session without setting fails; session with elliot/dave succeeds; KEI-86 fix-up follow-up adds the SET LOCAL pattern to the advance-phase code path (separate small PR).

Refs: KEI-86 (PR #917), GOV-12 (Gates As Code, Not Comments), Aiden audit verbatim posted to #execution 2026-05-17.

title: KEI-108 Part 2 — CI gate for operational-deployment-completeness
description:
Mechanical Definition-of-Done prevention per Dave's KEI-108 Part 2 directive. Sweep audit Agency_OS-rom found 32% false-complete rate over 14 days; this KEI builds the gate to prevent the recurrence.

Scope IN:
(a) GitHub Actions workflow runs on every PR.
(b) Check 1: any new *.service file in infra/systemd/ or systemd/ (added in this PR diff) MUST have a matching install line in either scripts/install*.sh OR a /tmp acceptance test that proves systemctl --user is-active = active for the new unit. Otherwise CI fail.
(c) Check 2: any new APIRouter definition in src/api/webhooks/*.py MUST have a matching app.include_router call in src/api/main.py. Otherwise CI fail.
(d) Bash script implementation (no Python deps) so it runs on any CI runner.

Scope OUT: existing shipped-but-not-installed services (those are the 7 remediation KEIs already filed); auto-install logic; UI/UX for the alert.

Acceptance: synthetic PR adding a fake.service file without install line → CI fails. Synthetic PR adding a fake.service + matching install line → CI passes. Same for the webhook router check.
notes: Source: KEI-109 dispatch + KEI-108 sweep findings. Anchor: 7/22 PRs (32%) shipped without operational deployment over 14 days.

Verbatim: `grep betterstack src/api/main.py` → 0 matches; `grep linear src/api/main.py` → `app.include_router(linear_webhook_router)` (so Linear is wired, Better Stack isn't). Remediation: import + `app.include_router(betterstack_router)` in src/api/main.py. Acceptance: POST /api/webhooks/betterstack returns 401/200 (not 404) on a probe.

title: Multi-source auto-indexers — git/Slack/Linear-state/ceo_memory → Weaviate
description:
## Why

KEI-107 (Weaviate bring-up) flagged that only one Weaviate-bound indexer exists in the repo today (`scripts/orchestrator/tool_call_log_indexer.py` writing to the `ToolCalls` collection). Dave's KEI-107 directive named four additional source pipelines that DON'T exist as code:

1. **git → Weaviate**: commits, PR titles/bodies, merged refs indexed for future-self retrieval
2. **Slack → Weaviate** (Telegram/Slack relay): messages on `#ceo`, `#execution`, `#completed_directives` indexed so agents can `bd recall` channel history
3. **Linear-state → Weaviate**: KEI status transitions (Todo → In Progress → Done) + comment thread bodies indexed so agents can recall why a KEI moved
4. **ceo_memory → Weaviate**: rows inserted into `public.ceo_memory` indexed so directive lineage is queryable

Without these, `bd recall <term>` only finds tool-call output — the agency's real institutional memory lives in commits/channels/Linear/ceo_memory and is invisible to the collective-intelligence layer.

## Scope (in)

* 4 new indexer scripts under `scripts/orchestrator/` (or `scripts/indexers/` — pick one and stick with it)
* 4 new systemd units under `infra/systemd/agents/` (each with `MemoryMax` per KEI-44 pattern)
* 4 new Weaviate classes (or extend existing): `GitCommits`, `SlackMessages`, `LinearTransitions`, `CeoMemory` — schema in `infra/weaviate/schema.py`
* Idempotency keys: git SHA / Slack ts+channel / Linear (issue_id, transition_id) / ceo_memory.id
* One `audit_logs` row per batch per indexer (same pattern as `tool_call_log_indexer`)
* Smoke test for each indexer (synthetic insert at source → ≤60s → Weaviate object same key)

## Scope (out)

* Querying these collections (separate KEI; LlamaIndex orchestration in KEI-49)
* Validation tier promotion (KEI-53 governs that)
* Embeddings — for the bootstrap, Weaviate's `none` vectorizer is fine; embeddings come later

## Acceptance

- [ ] 4 indexer scripts implemented + tested
- [ ] 4 systemd units active under cgroup caps
- [ ] 4 Weaviate classes present with documented properties
- [ ] Smoke: 4 synthetic inserts at each source → all 4 round-trip to Weaviate in <60s
- [ ] Each indexer writes an `audit_logs` row per batch
- [ ] Each indexer marks source `indexed=true` on success (where source schema supports it) or persists a cursor (where it doesn't, e.g., git)

## Parent

KEI-107 (3-Layer Memory: Bring Up, Wire, Prove) — directive identified this gap.

## Driver

Atlas (claiming).

## Notes

Worth scoping as a single multi-tracked KEI rather than 4 separate KEIs — the indexer shape is uniform (SELECT FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED or cursor + POST to Weaviate + audit row) and a shared base class is more economical than 4 independent implementations. Build the base class once, parameterise per source.

title: KEI — Session resumption clean-close UUID preservation (re-raise of closed PR #753)
description:
## Background

PR #753 (orion/pr-c-clean-close-preserves-uuid) closed 2026-05-14 as superseded — branch was conflicting with main + Pytest/MyPy failing + no active KEI to anchor a rebase under Linear-only + Beads-hard-block rule. The underlying bug is still in main; this KEI re-raises the work cleanly.

## The bug (verified 2026-05-14 on main)

`src/session_resumption/resolver.py` filters resumable sessions with:

```
ended_at IS NULL AND status='active' AND session_uuid IS NOT NULL
```

When a tmux session is killed cleanly (Stop hook runs, sets `ended_at`), the launcher falls through to a fresh session on next exec instead of resuming with the same UUID. Result: lost context continuity on planned restarts.

## Scope IN

* Stop hook writes `status='closed_clean'` (UUID preserved, ended_at recorded)
* Resolver accepts `status IN ('active','closed_clean')` and drops `ended_at IS NULL` filter
* Watchdog unchanged (still marks only ungraceful exits as ended)
* Migration: add `closed_clean` to whatever enum/check constraint governs `sessions.status`
* Tests: resolver picks up closed_clean sessions; stop-hook writes the right status

## Scope OUT

* Watchdog-fresh logic (separate concern)
* Session UUID generation (no change)

## Acceptance criteria

* After `tmux kill-session`, next `claude --resume` finds the prior session_uuid
* Pytest + MyPy green
* Migration safe (idempotent / has DOWN if needed)
* 1 unit test confirming resolver returns the UUID for `status='closed_clean'` rows

## Reference

Prior implementation in closed PR #753 for shape reference (do not rebase the branch — start fresh off current main; surrounding session_store / session_resumption code has shipped 6+ commits since).

\[ORION\]

Per Dave verbatim ts ~1778666900: No build begins without a Linear issue in Todo or In Progress assigned to the building agent. If something needs building and no KEI exists, raise the KEI first, get it on the board, then build. Applies to all 6 callsigns going forward.

Write docs/wave2/governance_linear_kei_before_build.md capturing rule text + applies/doesn't-apply matrix + how it interacts with KEI-39 4-step claim protocol + GOVERNANCE.md section text. Elliot bundles this with KEI-38+KEI-39 + KEI-37 + ceo_memory hygiene into post-compact governance refresh cascade.

Out: no GOVERNANCE.md edit in this PR (Elliot owns ratified-rule writes per his post-compact cascade).

Dave verbatim quote: 'No build begins without a Linear issue in Todo or In Progress assigned to you. If you find something that needs building and there is no KEI for it — raise the KEI first, get it on the board, then build. Never the other way around.'

Self-referential test case: this issue itself is being raised BEFORE the design-doc PR per the new rule.

title: KEI-40 — slack_relay.py: exponential backoff on Slack rate-limit (429)
description:
Per Dave verbatim ts ~1778666400. Today's session repeatedly hit Slack chat.postMessage rate-limits. slack_relay.py exits non-zero on URLError without retry, generating governance noise that makes working agents look stuck.

Fix: add exponential-backoff retry loop in scripts/slack_relay.py's post() function. On HTTP 429 (or urllib URLError with HTTPError.code == 429), parse retry-after header if present, else exponential delay: 1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s capped at 30s. Max 5 retries before final failure.

Ownership open per Elliot ts ~1778666440: Orion folds into KEI-22 if scope-aligned (Linear-Beads sync is the closest infra surface), else Aiden post-hygiene-sweep. Either path acceptable; this issue queues for whichever is faster.

Acceptance: slack_relay.py post() survives 1-2 transient 429s without governance noise. Final failure (5x exhausted) still surfaces to stderr.

title: Cognee Lance cross-process writer serialisation (fcntl flock / DB advisory lock)
description: Per Max correction ts ~1778661715 on Aiden's PR #826 scope catch: PR #826's asyncio.Semaphore(1) wrapper around LanceDBAdapter.index_data_points is PROCESS-LOCAL (module-level state in src/cognee/client.py). It correctly fixes the INTRA-process race that killed Stream 2 at 2026-05-13T01:54:53 (cognee's internal task scheduler firing concurrent asyncio.gather batches within one Python process). It does NOT cover the cross-process race: two Python processes each calling cognify() against the same Lance dataset would each hold their own semaphore and could re-trigger the lance retry_timeout Too-many-concurrent-writers failure. Discipline (one ingest at a time, per Resolution A coordination) avoids this in practice, but defense-in-depth is sound: extend the monkeypatch in src/cognee/client.py to acquire an fcntl flock (or DB advisory lock if Lance exposes one) on a stable lockfile path (e.g. /home/elliotbot/.cognee_system/lance.lock) before entering the asyncio.Semaphore region. Falls through to non-blocking try-acquire on collision so the second process surfaces a clean error instead of looping. P2 — non-urgent given coordination discipline; bumps to P1 if any cross-process collision is observed in the wild.

title: Cognee Lance writer cross-process serialisation (fcntl/DB lock around LanceDBAdapter)
description: PR #826 asyncio.Semaphore(1) in src/cognee/client.py module state is PROCESS-LOCAL. Two Python processes both calling cognify() against the same Lance dataset would each hold their own semaphore, re-triggering the cross-process Lance writer-conflict (Too many concurrent writers). Defense-in-depth: add fcntl.flock or DB advisory lock around LanceDBAdapter.index_data_points so cross-process writes serialise too. Lower priority while discipline (one ingest at a time) holds. Surfaced by Aiden during Stream 3+4 Resolution-A coordination 2026-05-13.
close_reason: Superseded by Agency_OS-nk8 (Aiden filed canonical version with fcntl flock path + non-blocking try-acquire detail, per Max preference ts ~1778661820).

title: Move Cognee Lance datasets out of venv to stable storage
description: Lance datasets currently live at /home/elliotbot/clawd/Agency_OS/.venv/lib/python3.12/site-packages/cognee/.cognee_system/databases/cognee.lancedb/ (1.6G). Venv recreate / pip --force-reinstall would wipe ingest state. After Stream 2/3/4 ingest completes and VQs pass, move to /home/elliotbot/clawd/cognee_data/ alongside cognee_db SQLite. Likely requires Cognee env var DATA_ROOT_DIRECTORY or similar — current DB_PATH only points cognee_db SQLite, not Lance system dir.

title: Self-assign hook needs role-filter for clones (scout/atlas/orion auto-claim wrong scope)
description: Self-assign hook on [READY:<callsign>] emits has no role-scope filter. Two empirical false-positives this session: (1) scout's [READY:scout] auto-claimed Agency_OS-dhe (Cognee Phase 2 P0); (2) scout's [READY:scout] auto-claimed Agency_OS-yvz (polling loop fix P1 — Aiden's). Clones should only auto-claim items matching their role-scope (research, mem0-audit, KEI-11-style outcomes), not primary build PRs. Fix options: (A) skip-claim entirely for clone callsigns (atlas/orion/scout) — fall back to polling-loop dispatch; (B) role-allowed labels on Beads issues + hook filters via label match. (A) is simpler. Pair with Stop-hook merge for the matching fix shape.

title: Better Stack supabase-rest monitor — 401 false-incident, needs auth header config
description: supabase-rest monitor (id 4400118) is firing incidents because Supabase REST root returns 401 (no auth) instead of 200. Better Stack default expects 2xx. Fix options: (a) configure monitor with auth header (Authorization: Bearer SUPABASE_ANON_KEY); (b) point monitor at a public-no-auth endpoint like /rest/v1/?select=count or /auth/v1/health; (c) accept-401-as-OK if Better Stack supports custom expected_status. First incident fired immediately on monitor create.

title: systemd-shipping PRs need post-merge install step (PR #774 gap)
description: PR #774 (KEI-17 polling loop) shipped systemd unit files into repo systemd/ but no install step into ~/.config/systemd/user/. Result: timer not running until manual cp + daemon-reload + enable. Pattern A risk (writer-side shipped without reader-side enablement). Fix: future PRs touching systemd/ require either (a) post-merge install script auto-run via CI/hook, or (b) explicit install steps in PR description checked at merge gate. Same gap-class as Aiden's KEI-12 cleanup-timer would have hit if we'd not caught it.

title: Cross-worktree Beads DB sync — CTOs can't run bd locally until resolved
description: Aiden's PR #771 review observation #2: 'Use bd for ALL task tracking' rule across worktrees doesn't work today because the Beads embedded Dolt DB is per-worktree, not shared. Pragmatic interim: TaskCreate locally for CTOs/clones until cross-worktree Beads sync solved (per upstream's refs/dolt/data git-sync model).

title: Beads→Postgres tasks table sync gap: bd close --evidence FK-fails for non-synced issues

The task_verifications FK requires the task_id to exist in tasks first. Affects all Beads issues not promoted via the Linear sync path. Proper fix: ensure Beads issue IDs are present in public.tasks before any verification-gated close is attempted, or add a pre-close sync step.

Discovered-from Agency_OS-ja8d.

Live double-@ verified: --selftest PASS (zz-onfail-selftest@probe.service → publisher unit=zz-onfail-selftest@probe.service, dashes+@ intact via %i). title: fix post-boot harness ceo_memory write: ModuleNotFoundError src module
description: Nova reboot test c2xk reported: ceo_memory write FAILED with ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'src'. The post-boot harness script (inline in Nova's dispatch) lacks sys.path.insert for repo root before importing src.*. Fix: add sys.path.insert(0, repo_root) before src imports, matching the pattern in scripts/three_store_save.py:28. Severity: low (result written manually by elliot; reboot test itself PASSED).

This lands in plaintext in /home/elliotbot/clawd/logs/keiracom-work-loop-bridge.log (and any other service that creates a valkey pool). Anyone with log read access gets the Upstash credential. FIX: redact the password/token in the log line (mask everything between '://' and '@', or log host:port only). Found during the nkc0 work-loop smoke 2026-05-29.
acceptance_criteria: valkey_pool pool-creation log line masks the token (no plaintext credential reaches the log); existing logs note rotation if the leaked token is considered compromised.

title: Vault deploy hardening — bake `vault secrets enable -path=secret kv` into install_prod_vault.sh
description: Discovered during Phase B11 Composio key vault step 2026-05-26: V1 Vault install (PR #1146) only enabled the Transit engine (for BYOK envelope encryption). The KV v2 engine at path=secret was not enabled; I had to enable it manually before `vault kv put secret/keiracom/composio/api_key value=<key>` would work. This is fine for V1 since only one operator did it once, but fresh Vault deploys (V1.1, disaster recovery, second-region Enterprise tenant) would hit the same gap. Bake the enable into /home/elliotbot/clawd/keiracom_system/dev/vault/install_prod_vault.sh step 5 (post-Shamir-init): `vault secrets enable -version=2 -path=secret kv 2>/dev/null || true` (idempotent — `|| true` on already-enabled). ~3 LoC addition. Cross-references: PR #1146, B11 dispatch 2026-05-26, /home/elliotbot/clawd/keiracom_system/dev/vault/PRODUCTION_DEPLOYMENT.md operational checklist.

title: Phase A6 ops follow-up — Prometheus counters for dual-publish health detection
description:
Per Aiden CONCUR on PR #1159 (2026-05-26 ~00:45 UTC).

The dual-publish import bug (PR #1159) was silently dropping signals for ~30min before smoke verification caught it. Fail-open design (correct architecture) means the supervisor doesn't crash, but silent degradation is invisible without explicit monitoring.

Implication for 7-day observation window: if dual-publish silently drops during the window, the cutover decision (flip Temporal-only) is based on incomplete data.

Suggested implementation:
- temporal_signal_publish_attempt_count (Prometheus counter, incremented before try-block in _temporal_signal_state)
- temporal_signal_publish_success_count (Prometheus counter, incremented after success path)
- BetterStack alert if ratio drops below 95% over 5-min window → catches silent dropouts

Wires to:
- Cat 16 infra.observability BetterStack substrate
- Layer 12 deep-dive (PR #1148) Scout's metrics pattern
- PR #1155 dual-publish pattern foundation

Non-blocking on PR #1159 merge. Engineer-tier follow-up; ~30-50 LoC. Owner: Orion (continuity with A6 + Temporal scaffold).

Anchor: ceo:keiracom_build_priority phase_a_build_unblockers.a6 follow-up; Aiden CONCUR on PR #1159.

Per Orion 2026-05-26 ~00:43 UTC: had to enable KV v2 engine at path=secret manually before first vault kv put on the production Vault host (Vault v1 had only transit/ engine from PR #1146 BYOK envelope; KV needed for internal-service-credentials category like Composio key).

Bake into install_prod_vault.sh as idempotent vault secrets enable -path=secret kv-v2 step. Future fresh Vault deploys won't need manual KV enable.

Owner: Orion (Vault host installer ownership). Non-blocking; ~10 LoC addition.

title: Phase A7 follow-up — cache-warmer workflow for ephemeral-agent prompt-cache locality
description:
Atlas bonus observation on PR #1156 A7 design (2026-05-26): §9 ephemeral-agent caveat is right — Anthropic prompt cache misses on every spawn because the 5-min TTL doesnt survive ephemeral spawn cycles + the cache is per-API-key-per-connection. §11 LOOSE item #2 names a cache-warmer workflow as mitigation. Worth a Phase 2 follow-up KEI per Atlas.

DESIGN OUTLINE (refine in Phase 2):
  - Temporal workflow that fires sentinel LLM call on tenant signup OR per-tier scheduled (e.g. every 4min45s to land just inside the 5min TTL window)
  - Call shape = canonical structurally-stable prefix matching what real chat agents will send (system + tools + per-domain context)
  - Cache_control breakpoint set so the response populates the Anthropic cache + subsequent real calls within 5min hit the cached prefix
  - Per-tier policy: Sandbox skips (no cache benefit; small prompt under 1024 token threshold per A7 §4); Solo/Pro/Team/Enterprise run periodic warmer
  - Cost model: sentinel calls themselves cost input tokens; need to validate warmer cost < cache-savings-on-real-calls before enabling per-tier

DEPENDS ON: A7 build (constants + LiteLLM cache_control wiring) merging first.

title: Phase A6 NIT-4 — Stream 2 Hindsight mem.wrap.trace integration follow-up
description: Aiden HOLD NIT-4 on PR #1155. audit_activity.py Stream 2 is a placeholder TODO with explicit INFO log of the skip. Wire Atlas mem.wrap.trace (PR #1134) once a wrapper-instance is available via env config / from_env factory. Implementation: replace the TODO log block with `await trace_wrapper.add_audit_event(event)` best-effort with degrade-gracefully on Hindsight unreach (per contract V1 fail-closed exception for Hindsight transient). Engineer-tier work after Atlas exposes the from_env factory.

title: Phase A6 NIT-3 — fold tier-gate WARN-mode + Sandbox-analog for fleet-internal signals into contract V2
description: Aiden HOLD NIT-3 on PR #1155. FleetSupervisorWorkflow audit event uses tier=sandbox + tenant_id=fleet-internal as placeholders for the fleet-internal signal case. Contract V1 schema does not formally recognise a fleet-internal tier or tenant. Fold these conventions into contract V2 amendment: either (a) add agent_context=fleet|customer field, OR (b) add fleet-internal tier enum value. Pairs with the existing 3 Phase 2 amendments (Agency_OS-v9dk + tpxj + ucf8); could be amendment #4. Assignee Elliot (contract owner) for the spec change, then engineer-tier for implementation.

title: Phase A6 NIT-2 — signal_helpers connection pooling (Phase 2 if signal volume scales)
description: Aiden HOLD NIT-2 on PR #1155. Current signal_helpers.py per-call connect (await from_env() each signal) adds ~50-100ms overhead. Acceptable for fleet_supervisor running every 60s. If signal volume scales (e.g., per-tenant signals at 10+/sec for the LLM-call workflow #2), pool the Temporal client at module level. Decision threshold: 100 signals/min sustained. Filed for Phase 2 visibility.

title: Phase A6 NIT-1 — continue-as-new pattern at ~10K signals (FleetSupervisorWorkflow history bounding)
description: Aiden HOLD NIT-1 on PR #1155 (Phase A6 first-workflow). FleetSupervisorWorkflow runs continuously, accumulating event history per signal. At ~10K signals the workflow history grows unbounded. V1 single-fleet volume is manageable but workflow #2 (LLM-call shape) will hit this faster with per-call event accumulation. Use Temporal continue-as-new pattern at the 10K signal threshold to bound history while preserving state. Applies when LLM-call workflow #2 lands.

title: Phase A2 NIT-5 — Vault lease-aware token rotation (V1.x)
description: Aiden deferred NIT-5 on PR #1146 (Phase A2 Vault). Current production extension token (issued via setup_transit.sh) has 24h renewable TTL. V1.x improvement: auto-renew before expiry (e.g., re-issue at 80% of TTL via /v1/auth/token/renew-self). Background goroutine/asyncio task in KeiracomTenantExtension or a sidecar daemon. Out of scope for V1 — manual re-issue is acceptable at fleet operator scale until first paying customer.

title: Phase A2 NIT-4 — VaultDecryptor retry-with-backoff on transport errors (V1.x)
description: Aiden deferred NIT-4 on PR #1146 (Phase A2 Vault). Current VaultDecryptor wraps urllib transport errors as VaultDecryptError immediately. V1.x improvement: retry with exponential backoff on ConnectionRefused / Timeout / TemporaryFailure before raising. Suggested defaults: 3 retries, base 250ms, cap 2s, jitter. Honour 429 Retry-After if Vault returns it. Out of scope for V1 (Vault local-LAN; ~zero transient failures expected at fleet scale).

description:
Nova worktree has unresolved merge conflicts in src/dispatcher/main.py and tests/dispatcher/test_main_wiring.py from in-flight dispatcher work before the 2026-05-20 fleet halt. The keepalive's git pull rebase cannot proceed, so nova-agent.service was restart-looping. Elliot stopped nova-agent.service 2026-05-23 to break the loop; nova held cleanly since then.

Resolution requirements:
