Dave-authorized 2026-05-24 as discrete cleanup, not urgent — pick up when bandwidth allows. Priority 3 (low-backlog).

comments.author: Aiden
comments.text: Merge conflict resolved 2026-05-24 by orion. Empirical: nova's 3 local commits (0f23dca1+240fac2a+0e1bc2be) were squash-merged to main as 055829e1 (PR #1107). Rebase replayed them on top of main which already contains a squashed-and-refined superset. Took HEAD for all conflicts; main is strict superset (adds OpenAPI response docs + tmp_path fixture + collapsed _register_session). 0e1bc2be auto-dropped by git ('patch contents already upstream'); 240fac2a no-op'd. Final HEAD d2d545b3 on top of fresh main is .beads bump only. NOT pushed — would require force-push to overwrite remote pre-rebase history; flagged to elliot. nova-agent.service ExecStartPre status=128 fixed. NEW separate bug: ExecStart agent_keepalive.sh fails with sd_notify NotifyAccess violation (Type=notify unit, child-process notifications). Out of scope for this dispatch. Service stopped clean; tmux nova session alive. Recommend new P2 bd for sd_notify mismatch (NotifyAccess=all OR Type=simple).

title: Audit false-negative: self-claim loop reported not-running but all 6 instances active
Scope NATS adoption for inter-agent comms (Slack relay replacement)
description:
Slack relay (tg + inbox JSON) is workspace-rate-limited (message_limit_exceeded fired during 2026-05-19 session — 3rd time in recent sessions per memory pin reference). NATS is a candidate replacement transport. This issue scopes the migration; it does NOT commit to executing it.

Why now:
- Rate-limit blocked max from reporting completed PR review work back to #execution.
- 7 callsigns × multi-session = traffic volume only grows.
- Slack rate-limit is a workspace quota, not a per-channel issue — adding channels doesn't help.

What NATS gives us:
- ~10ms agent-to-agent message RTT (vs ~50ms via Slack).
- No third-party rate-limit. Self-hosted single binary on Railway (~-10/mo).
- Subject-based fan-out: 'agents.max.inbox', 'agents.elliot.dispatch', '#ceo.broadcast'. Multiple subscribers per subject — TG mirror + audit log + actual agent listener all consume independently.
- JetStream persistence available if we want durable streams.

Migration shape (proposed):
1. Stand up one NATS node on Railway (~30min).
2. Write 'scripts/nats_relay.py' — replaces inbox file watcher with NATS subscribe loop; publishes outbox JSON to a NATS subject.
3. Write 'scripts/nats_to_tg_mirror.py' — bridges 'agents.*.broadcast' → Slack #execution/#ceo so Dave can still read from Slack during cut-over.
4. Per-callsign cut over one at a time; keep file-based inbox as fallback for 1-2 weeks.
5. Decommission Slack relay once stable.

Cost: ~half-day to write the two bridge scripts + scoping. Per-month: -10 Railway service.

Risks:
- Dave loses unified Slack readability if mirror script breaks → mirror script is critical-path.
- Adds one more piece of self-hosted infra to monitor.
- NATS server outage = total agent comms outage (vs Slack outage which is rare).

Not committing to execute — this issue is the scoping artifact. Decision-gate: Dave + Aiden review the scope and approve/reject before any code lands.

Memory pins context: feedback_slack_relay_only (current ratified comms channel); feedback_socket_mode_single_connection (prior comm bug from multi-connection Socket Mode — NATS has no equivalent single-connection issue since pub/sub is its native mode).
acceptance_criteria: Scoping doc covers: hosting choice (Railway vs Oracle free tier), subject taxonomy, mirror-to-Slack design, rollout sequence, decommission plan, cost projection. Dave + Aiden approve scope before any implementation work claimed.

title: [DISPATCHER] verify auto-reissue logic for scoped JWTs before expiry (KEI-209 G5)
description: 2026-05-18 audit (Agency_OS-pzg8) — KEI-209 spec mentions 'auto-reissued by Dispatcher before expiry'. auth_minter.ts only handles single-shot mint; auto-reissue logic should live in dispatcher.ts caller. Audit task: read dispatcher.ts + watchdog.ts; confirm whether auto-reissue is wired up (e.g. watchdog re-mints when 80% of ttl elapsed). If not present: file follow-up to add reissue trigger. If present: confirm + close. Repo: Keiracom/Dispatcher.

title: [DISPATCHER] auth_minter — cap TTL at 15min per KEI-209 safety constraint (G3 gap)
description: 2026-05-18 audit (Agency_OS-pzg8) — KEI-209 spec specified 15-min hardcoded TTL but mintScopedJwt accepts arbitrary ttl_seconds. Caller could pass 86400 and get a 24h token, defeating the short-TTL safety story. Either: (a) cap ttl_seconds at 900 in mintScopedJwt and warn-log if caller exceeded, (b) remove ttl_seconds param + hardcode 900. Recommend (a) — preserves caller control for legitimate short-lived overrides (e.g. test fixtures with 60s) while enforcing the ceiling. Repo: Keiracom/Dispatcher.
close_reason: Drift-audit close (Agency_OS-z27a): all six in_progress entries delivered by merged PRs verified by scope match. tjgihu→#1033 (kei94 keepalive 2026-05-18T12:01Z); v1e279→#1038 (kei20 alert routing 2026-05-18T12:31Z); 50hd4r→#1028 (kei221a FLEET flag canonicalise 2026-05-18T09:57Z, corrected from Elliot's #1030 mapping per #1030 body cross-ref); p2b6→#1028 (item a) + #1030 (items b/c/d) + Supabase MCP verify (item d persona column present); qiqvu1→#954 (kei116 AES-256 pgcrypto + rotation); 524m→#1021 (auth_minter 15-min TTL hardcoded — option b from bd spec).

title: bd claim emits 'WARNING: cognee.client import failed (No module named cognee)' on every invocation — wrapper venv missing cognee or import not optional+silent
description:
Observed on every bd claim invocation in atlas session 2026-05-18: stderr emits 'WARNING: cognee.client import failed (No module named cognee)'. Two clean fix paths:

(a) Install cognee into the bd-wrapper's resolution path. The wrapper at /home/elliotbot/clawd/Agency_OS-scout/scripts/bd shells out to python3 in subcommands; cognee.client is presumably imported by one of those subcommand modules. pip install cognee into whichever venv the wrapper resolves (likely /home/elliotbot/clawd/Agency_OS/.venv given systemd-services-read-from-main-worktree pattern).

(b) Make the import optional + silent: wrap the import in try/except ImportError + log.debug instead of warn; document the degraded path (whatever cognee.client contributes — likely KEI-44 context injection per KEI-50/KEI-55 ceiling). The cognee_session_start.py hook is already documented as fail-open (see ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md _session_start.md §7); the bd claim path should match that posture.

Preference: (b) is safer (no new dependency on a 3GB-mem-capped vendor that may legitimately be down). The WARNING level is also wrong — should be DEBUG when cognee is intentionally unavailable. 

No crash, no functional break — just stderr noise on every bd invocation, which clutters session start + audit output. P3 cosmetic. Discovered while running drift audit + close-loops (58 invocations in one session = 58 WARNING lines).

Reproduce: bd claim <any-id> 2>&1 | head -2. Source likely in scripts/orchestrator/*.py imports or scripts/bd subcommand routing.

title: bd ready in-ready suppression — auto-suppress tasks whose Linear status is Done
description: Recommendation #4 from 2026-05-18 drift audit. Defense-in-depth so future Linear/bd drift doesn't surface as stale claims to agents. Logic: 'bd ready' currently filters by bd-local status + assignee + deps. Extend the filter: if a bd record has an external_ref pointing at a Linear KEI AND the cached Linear status for that KEI is Done/Canceled/Duplicate, suppress from the ready queue regardless of bd-local status. Reason: prevents agents picking up tasks that have already shipped (today: atlas picked stale 96ou KEI-208 and 7xsk5j KEI-215 — both had merged peer PRs but bd showed open). Implementation: needs the bd wrapper at scripts/bd to either query bd's local Linear-status cache (if it has one) or accept a stale-suppress flag. May also need a freshness guard: only suppress if Linear cache is fresh (<1h).

title: Drift audit page-2+ — scan Linear Done KEIs older than most recent 100
description: Recommendation #3 from 2026-05-18 drift audit. The 11 Drift-A tasks (bd open, Linear Done) cross-checked against ONLY the most recent 100 Linear Done KEIs (Linear pagination, hasNextPage:true). Earlier Done KEIs (older than the 100 sampled) weren't checked. Scan: paginate through ALL Linear Done issues; cross-reference against bd records with external_ref pointing at each; report bd records still open/in_progress that should be closed. Likely 5-50 additional Drift-A cases — these are stale claims waiting to trip future sessions. Tool: extend the audit script logic from this session's run (see chat transcript) or one-shot Python. Output: plain-text list of beads IDs + KEI for orchestrator bulk-close.

Slack_history collection in Weaviate (14772 messages) should remain queryable BUT the live incremental indexer needs to be disabled or it will repeatedly fail trying to poll dead Slack endpoints. Check scripts/orchestrator/slack_history_indexer.py + its systemd unit. Keep historical data, kill the writer.

title: [ATLAS] fix(kei201-followup): post_object docstring drift — returns claim mismatches code
description:
## Why

GAP #6 surfaced in Aiden's PR #1025 review (KEI-201). The `post_object` function in `scripts/orchestrator/slack_history_indexer.py` has docstring drift on a line not modified by Scout's PR:

* Docstring claims: "Returns True on 200/422 (idempotent)"
* Actual code: `status_code in (200, 201)`

Since the line wasn't touched in PR #1025, Aiden correctly held it out of the PR scope per the modified-lines rule. Filing as a separate clean-up KEI.

## What

Reconcile docstring with code. Either:
(a) Update docstring to match code (`200, 201`), or
(b) Update code to match docstring (`200, 422` — verify with Weaviate API what 422 actually means; if 422 = "already exists / idempotent" this is the desirable behaviour).

Pick whichever matches the actual Weaviate response semantics. Verify with a live POST against an existing object (should return 422 if idempotent-by-design).

## Acceptance

- [ ] `post_object` docstring and code statement agree on accepted status codes
- [ ] Comment or test documents what each accepted code means (200 created, 201 already, 422 conflict, etc.)
- [ ] No regression in slack_history_indexer behaviour

## Source

Aiden's \[REVIEW:hold:aiden\] message on PR #1025, 2026-05-18 \~09:00 UTC, note #6.

title: KEI-32 audit — ARCHITECTURE.md §3 must declare canonical Cognee status (active/coexists/deprecated)
description: Audit finding: ARCHITECTURE.md §3 Dead References has NO Cognee entry. Per LAW I-A and the canonical 'read ARCHITECTURE.md fresh' discipline, the Cognee status must be authoritative. Current operational state: Cognee subprocess alive under 3GB cap (KEI-44), Weaviate has memory layer (Phase 0), Atlas building 4 multi-source indexers (KEI-116 B/C/D). Acceptance: ARCHITECTURE.md §3 (or §2) adds a Cognee row stating one of: (a) ACTIVE — cognee + weaviate cohabit during Phase 0 transition, (b) COEXISTS — cognee for X / weaviate for Y with the boundary defined, (c) DEPRECATED — cognee retired on date N. Phase 0 memory now lives in Weaviate (with cognee still alive under 3GB cap per KEI-44). Preflight comment may be partial truth. description:

If superseded, retire all 4 references + monitor + heartbeat + status page entry + routing policy entry in one PR (scoped to BS surface).

title: Discovery validation: warn when tiktoken fallback active in claim injection
description: Aiden KEI-55 review (PR #895, 2026-05-17): src/governance/claim_injection.py uses len(text)//4 as a token-count fallback when tiktoken is not importable. The //4 estimator under-counts CJK and code tokens — silent over-injection past the 500-token ceiling is possible. Fix: log a warning when the tiktoken import fails so operators see they're on the fallback path. Anchored PR #895 review item (3) marked non-blocking.

title: skills/slack/ — write canonical Slack skill for LAW XII compliance
description: Aiden KEI-55 review (PR #895, 2026-05-17): _notify_dave in src/governance/discovery_validation.py uses subprocess.run(['tg', '-c', 'ceo', msg]) — established codebase pattern (8+ existing call-sites: scripts/alerts/*.py, scripts/openai_cost_*.py, scripts/stage_subset_runner.py), so LAW VI exec-tier path is correct. But for full LAW XII (Skills-First Integration) compliance, write skills/slack/ wrapping slack_relay.py so the canonical interface is a skill, not a subprocess shim. Migrate existing call-sites once the skill exists. Reference: feedback_subagent_step0_bypass + LAW XII doc.

title: KEI-108 Part 2 follow-up — tighten include_router lookback check
description: Aiden non-blocking note on PR #914: the Check 2 include_router detection uses 'grep -B1 "app.include_router" main.py | grep -q "${module}"' to confirm wire-up. In our codebase the dotted module path is rarely on the line immediately before an include_router call. On a future NEW webhook PR with proper wiring (`from src.api.webhooks.new import router; ...; app.include_router(router)`), the -B1 lookback may not find the module name → false-positive fail. Tightening: extract the imported variable name from `from <module> import ... as VAR` (or default `router` if not aliased) and grep for `app.include_router(VAR`. Aiden's recommendation; he confirmed this is NOT a blocker on PR #914 because (a) gate empirically catches real-shape false-completes, (b) first false-positive is loud + 5-min fix. Acceptance: synthetic PR with proper webhook wiring (import-as-VAR + include_router(VAR)) exits 0; synthetic PR with import-but-no-include_router exits 1.

title: BS monitor: distinguish cognify completion-exit from crash-exit
description: Per Elliot ts ~1778654958 + Aiden confirm during Stream 2 seal: Better Stack incident alert fires on cognee-phase1-ingest when the process exits cleanly (cognify() complete → done: 1678 ok / 0 failed → process exits → heartbeat stops). Monitor sees no-heartbeat and flags incident. False-positive — completion is the goal state, not a failure mode. Fix candidates: (a) BS monitor tuned to accept heartbeat-stops-after-completion-marker; (b) wrap cognee_ingest.py to fire a 'completion' heartbeat to a separate BS endpoint on success path; (c) BS monitor flapping threshold tuned per-job-type (long-running batch jobs vs daemons have different no-heartbeat semantics). P3 — non-urgent (humans can dismiss false alert), but adds noise to alerting signal-to-noise.

title: KEI-36 v2 — wire mcp__memory lookup to existing ceo_memory orchestration:model_assignment
description: Per Elliot scope correction ts ~1778654710: ceo_memory key orchestration:model_assignment IS ALREADY POPULATED with structured data (Elliot orchestrator write ts ~1778625870). Structure verified: primaries_unchanged (elliot/aiden/max opus-4-7) + clones_switched (atlas/orion/scout sonnet-4-6, scout also haiku-4-5 mechanical). Task is WIRING, not population: extend pre_compact_alert.py auto_populate_heartbeat() to query mcp__memory__get_by_id (or equivalent direct supabase read) for the key, parse the structured payload, and populate the Configured model field deterministically based on resolved callsign. Once wired, the [HEARTBEAT-INCOMPLETE] warning will stop firing for the Configured field. Running field still surfaces via warning (no --model flag introspection from inside hook process — separate gap).

title: Better Stack agencyxos.ai monitor — fix 422 redirect/cookie config
description: PR #786 uptime monitor creation for agencyxos.ai failed with HTTP 422: 'Cannot follow redirects when expecting a 3xx status code; Cannot keep cookies when redirecting when expecting a 3xx status code'. Vercel-deployed site likely returns 308 redirect to www. Fix: configure monitor with expected_status_code=308 OR follow_redirects=false OR use the canonical URL that doesn't redirect (try www.agencyxos.ai or .vercel.app). Small Aiden follow-up.

title: Fleet Supervisor — mechanical monitor + auto-resolve for all 6 agents (replaces manual tmux checking)
description:
## What

Fully mechanical fleet supervisor that replaces manual tmux monitoring. Runs every 5 minutes via systemd timer. Diagnoses AND resolves every idle scenario — no human or agent decision point.

## Architecture

`scripts/fleet_supervisor.py` + `fleet-supervisor.timer` (systemd)

For each of 6 agents, every 5 minutes:

### Scenario 1: No claim + queue has items

* Supabase: `SELECT id FROM tasks WHERE status='available' ORDER BY priority LIMIT 1 FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED`
* Supabase: `UPDATE tasks SET status='active', claimed_by=$CALLSIGN`
* Linear: fetch KEI description
* tmux: `send-keys` inject full task description into agent stdin
* Result: agent receives work mechanically

### Scenario 2: No claim + queue empty

* GitHub API: check for open PRs needing review (where agent is not author, not yet reviewed)
* If found: INSERT review KEI into tasks + claim for this agent + inject
* If none: agent is correctly idle, log "queue empty"

### Scenario 3: Claimed + no tool_call_log entry in 15 minutes

* Query agent context usage (parse tmux capture for context indicator)
* If 100%: `systemctl --user restart {agent}.service` + git pull + cognee inject + re-claim same KEI + inject
* If <100%: tmux send-keys nudge: "You have KEI-{id} claimed. Resume building. Task: {title}"

### Scenario 4: tmux session dead

* `tmux has-session -t {callsign}` returns non-zero
* `systemctl --user restart {agent}.service`
* Wait 10s for boot
* Claim next available + inject

### Scenario 5: Agent shipped PR + went idle (no claim, has open PR)

* GitHub API: agent has open PR with no reviews yet
* Create review KEI for PR (excludes author)
* Claim next BUILD KEI for idle agent
* Agent starts next task instead of waiting for review

### Scenario 6: Stale claims (active > 2 hours, no task_verification)

* `UPDATE tasks SET status='available', claimed_by=NULL WHERE status='active' AND updated_at < NOW() - interval '2 hours' AND id NOT IN (SELECT task_id FROM task_verifications)`
* Released items become claimable next cycle

## Output

One #ceo Slack message every 5 minutes:

```
Fleet Status [20:30 UTC]
atlas: building KEI-166 (last tool call 2m ago) ✅
aiden: building KEI-114 (last tool call 45s ago) ✅
orion: was idle → claimed KEI-105, injected task ⚡
scout: was idle → claimed KEI-155, injected task ⚡
elliot: 100% context → restarted, re-claimed KEI-151 🔄
max: shipped PR #957, idle → claimed KEI-104 ⚡
Queue: 28 available | 6 active | 65 done
```

## Acceptance

1. systemd timer fires every 5 minutes — verify with `systemctl list-timers`
2. Script detects idle agent + claims + injects — verify with Supabase claimed_by appearing without manual intervention
3. Script detects 100% context + restarts + re-claims — verify with service restart log + new claim in Supabase
4. Script detects dead tmux + restarts — verify by killing a tmux session and watching recovery
5. Script posts to #ceo — verify message appears with correct per-agent status
6. All 6 scenarios tested empirically, not just "it should work"

## Dependencies

* Supabase tasks table (exists)
* tool_call_log table (exists)
* tmux sessions (exist)
* GitHub API access (exists via gh CLI)
* Slack webhook (exists)
* systemd user services (exist)

## Phase: 0 Priority: P0 — this is the single highest-leverage infrastructure piece. It makes the fleet self-healing.

title: KEEPALIVE_PULL_MODE conservative-mode flag for agent_keepalive.sh
description: Follow-up to KEI-140 (PR #927 merged commit 1efc565d). Aiden + Max both flagged in review: aggressive 'git pull --rebase --autostash' on ExecStartPre is fail-open via '-' prefix but on feature branches with mid-build state, a rebase conflict leaves the agent booted in a conflicted state. Conservative-mode flag KEEPALIVE_PULL_MODE = ff-only | rebase | off would let operators tune per-callsign. Same shape as Atlas's pinned memory feedback_check_branch_before_checkout but applied at boot-time-pull rather than developer-time. Non-blocking; ship when fleet shape stabilises.
notes: Author: atlas. Suggested by Max review on PR #927 + Scout concur. Atlas+Aiden agree non-blocking on KEI-140 itself.

title: claim_source mechanical discriminator — Option B self-claim governance exemption
description:
3-way ratified Option B (Aiden+Max+Elliot ts \~1779022280) for the KEI-92 self-claim governance gap.

## What

Add `claim_source TEXT` column to public.tasks (values: 'auto_loop' | 'manual'). cmd_claim sets it based on caller. enforcer Rule 8 (DISPATCH-COORDINATION) + Step 0 gate (rule-kei-72) consult the column and SKIP enforcement when claim_source='auto_loop'.

## Why

Per GOV-12 (Gates As Code, Not Comments). Aiden's original Option B (exemption flag) refined by Max with mechanical-column discriminator: the data flag IS the gate, not a textual rule amendment that humans-must-remember.

## Acceptance

- [ ] Migration adds claim_source column with default 'manual' and NOT NULL after backfill
- [ ] cmd_claim --any path sets claim_source='auto_loop'
- [ ] cmd_claim --id path sets claim_source='manual'
- [ ] enforcer_deterministic.py Rule 8 reads task.claim_source and skips when 'auto_loop'
- [ ] rule-kei-72 Step 0 gate skipped on auto_loop tasks
- [ ] Backfill: existing rows default to 'manual' (conservative)
- [ ] Tests: auto_loop path no enforcer fire; manual path enforces as today
- [ ] KEI-108 gate PASS structural

## Pairing

Max (lead — tasks_cli + governance_rules context). \~30 LoC + 1 migration. Phase 0.5.

## Source

Dave KEI-deliberation 2026-05-17 \~12:50 UTC. 3-way ratified ts \~1779022280.</description>

title: Connect your tools
description:
Integrations turn Linear into your source of truth around product development. Keep data in sync, and eliminate manual updates between tools.

![connect-your-tools.png](https://uploads.linear.app/fe63b3e2-bf87-46c0-8784-cd7d639287c8/c2eae035-37e2-4754-adcb-b8305431aa1f/c92d70c7-e6d0-4fa2-a0fd-78f6e780993a)

### **Key integrations**

* [**Slack**](<https://linear.app/settings/integrations/slack>)
  Create issues from Slack messages and sync threads
* [**GitHub**](<https://linear.app/settings/integrations/github>)** / **[**GitLab**](<https://linear.app/settings/integrations/gitlab>)
  Automate your pull request, commit workflows, and keep issues synced both ways
* [**Agents**](<https://linear.app/integrations/agents>)
  Deploy AI agents that work alongside you as teammates

### **Browse all integrations**

Discover 150+ available connections in our [integration directory](<https://linear.app/integrations>) – from bug creation via support tools (Intercom, Zendesk), to issues created from design explorations (Figma).

### **Linear API**

If you need something more custom, you can build directly on the Linear API (built on GraphQL). [See our Dev Docs to learn more](<https://linear.app/developers>).
